title: Home

## lattice-system

A Lean 4 + Mathlib formalization project targeting general lattice models.
This project subsumes and generalizes the earlier
[ising-model](https://github.com/phasetr/ising-model) project, progressively
covering classical spin systems, quantum spin systems, Hubbard, BCS,
CAR algebras, and eventually lattice QCD.

## Design axis: graphs, not lattices

Despite the name, the **primary combinatorial abstraction in this
library is a graph `(╬Ы, E_╬Ы)`** тАФ finite for finite-volume work and
infinite for the thermodynamic-limit / algebraic-formulation work
that is a major long-term goal тАФ not "a lattice". Concrete lattices
(the 1D chain, square / cubic grids, infinite chains, тДд^d, тАж) appear
only as specific instances such as `SimpleGraph.pathGraph`,
`SimpleGraph.cycleGraph`, products of these, or their infinite
analogues. This convention follows the standard mathematical-physics
literature on many-body systems on graphs (Lieb's theorem on
bipartite lattices, the MarshallтАУLiebтАУMattis theorem, Miyao 2021
┬з3, тАж) and aligns the project with mathlib's `SimpleGraph`
foundations.

Finite-volume work uses `╬Ы : Type*` together with `[Fintype ╬Ы]`
when needed (e.g. for traces, partition functions, finite sums of
local terms); infinite-volume work drops the `Fintype` assumption
and uses graphs over types like `тДд` or `тДд^d` instead.

The bridge from a `SimpleGraph` to the pairwise coupling
`J : ╬Ы тЖТ ╬Ы тЖТ тДВ` consumed by `heisenbergHamiltonian` (and similar
operators) lives in `LatticeSystem.Lattice.Graph` (`couplingOf`).
Existing chain coupling families (`openChainCoupling`,
`periodicChainCoupling`) are characterised as
`couplingOf (pathGraph _) _` and `couplingOf (cycleGraph _) _`
respectively.

## Scope

| Area | Stage | Typical references |
|---|---|---|
| Classical spin systems | Inherited from ising-model | Friedli-Velenik, Glimm-Jaffe |
| Quantum spin systems | Current focus | Tasaki, Nielsen-Chuang (cross-check) |
| Hubbard / BCS | Medium term | Tasaki 1998, Bru-Pedra |
| CAR-algebraic formulation | Medium-long term | Araki-Moriya, Bru-Pedra |
| Thermodynamic limit (infinite graphs) | Long term, **major project goal** | Simon, Friedli-Velenik, Bratteli-Robinson |
| Lattice QCD | Longest term | Aarts, Davies |

## Refactoring conventions and review criteria

A **single-source-of-truth document** for refactoring conventions
applied as the review checklist on every pull request:
[Refactoring conventions and review criteria](refactoring-conventions.html).
Topics: test methods (decide / bridge / small-exhaustive / shim /
`#guard_msgs`), module-split criteria, generic / dedup conventions,
deprecation window policy, naming / docstring rules, linter
exceptions, public-doc synchronisation.

The companion page [Deprecation window](deprecations.html) tracks
currently-deprecated public declarations, the `since` date,
recommended replacement, and earliest-removal window.

unique-up-to-positive-scalar strictly positive PerronтАУFrobenius eigenvector тАФ equivalent to the matrix-level Tasaki (2.5.4) ground-state expansion `╬г_╧Г c_╧Г \|╬и╠Г^╧ГтЯй` with `c_╧Г > 0`. The canonical proof witnesses are `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` (PR #3891, toy coupling) and `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_bipartiteCompletePositive` (PR #3893, general bipartite `J` positive on the complete bipartite graph). These declarations are the structurally repaired replacements for the older `h_intermediate` capstones removed in PR #3917. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 | Truly-unconditional closure at all `N тЙе 1` (PR #3891 toy, PR #3893 general) |
| P1o (Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, MattisтАУNishimori anisotropic) | **Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4 (MattisтАУNishimori)**: ground-state uniqueness + `┼Ь┬│_tot \|╬ж_GSтЯй = 0` for the anisotropic XXZ + single-ion Hamiltonian (2.5.14) on a connected bipartite lattice with `\|A\| = \|B\|`, under (i) `тИТ1 < ╬╗ тЙд 1, D тЙе 0` or (ii) `╬╗ тЙе 1, D тЙд 0`. The model is only U(1)-invariant (not SU(2)), so the proof uses even/odd-sector PerronтАУFrobenius (at most double degeneracy) + a deformation argument from the SU(2) point `(╬╗,D)=(1,0)` rather than MLM directly. bridge giving per-block submatrix-full intersection finrank equality, (h.2) #3841 strengthens involution decomposition to finrank equality, (h.3) #3842 combines (h.1)+(h.2) into `finrank тДВ (eig M ╬╝) = тИС_p finrank тДВ (eig M.submatrix_p ╬╝)` for parity-block-diagonal `M` commuting with `P`, (h.4) #3844 submatrix-based тЙд 2 wrappers. **(i) chain: Hermitian spectral chain** (#3846тАУ#3855): (i.1) #3846 submatrix Hermiticity, (i.2) #3847 submatrix eigenvalue realness (`╬╝.im = 0`), (i.3) #3848 submatrix minimum eigenvalue exists, (i.4) #3849 Hermitian eigenspace `= тКе` below min, (i.5) #3850 block-diag eigenspace `= тКе` below joint per-block min, (i.6) #3851 full eig `тЙд 2` at min(per-block mins) given per-block `тЙд 1` at min, (i.7) #3854 bare `─д'` specialisation, (i.8) #3855 bare anisotropic `─д` specialisation via axis-swap (general `N` with `AxisSwapUnitaryS` + spin-1/2 instance) тАФ the bridge to unconditional `тЙд 2` modulo PF eigenvalue identification with #3859 `hermitianMinEigenvalue тЙд ╬╝` from eigenvector existence, (j.4) #3860 packaged form, (j.5) #3861 consumer-friendly conditional bound at `hermitianMinEigenvalue` (needs the `╬╜_PF = hermitianMinEigenvalue` hypothesis explicit). **Target balanced-sector PF discharge** (Issue #3739): off-diagonal anisotropic/Heisenberg agreement, real sector matrices, the Marshall-dressed shifted anisotropic sector matrix, structural irreducibility, and CollatzтАУWielandt PF/minimum identification now provide the balanced-sector `finrank тДВ тЙд 1` input at the spin-1/2 target point and in the general spin-S target sector; the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit `h_balanced_sector_pf` callback. **Strict-gap, SU(2)-uniqueness, and MLM endpoint wrappers** now replace the explicit balanced/full equality input by the standard strict gap over all non-balanced non-empty magnetization sectors, derive that strict gap from SU(2)-endpoint global uniqueness, and then construct the SU(2) uniqueness input from the general Theorem 2.3 MLM/Casimir/PF endpoint. **D-boundary extension**: the spin-1/2 parity-block route has a dedicated `D тЙе 0` version because single-ion `┬▒2` moves are impossible for `Fin (1+1)`. The general spin-S route now also has a bond-only parity reachability replacement for the single-ion branch, giving general `D тЙе 0` parity-block irreducibility and the MLM/Casimir target wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general`. **╬╗=1 boundary**: at spin `1/2`, `(S^3)^2 = (1/4)I`, so `singleIonAnisotropyS D 1` is a scalar shift and `anisotropicHeisenbergS J 1 D 1` reduces to the Heisenberg Hamiltonian plus `D|╬Ы|/4`; the SU(2) Theorem 2.3/MLM uniqueness transfers to target uniqueness and zero magnetization at `╬╗ = 1`. For general spin-S, the SU(2)-point wrappers cover `╬╗ = 1`, `D = 0`, and for `2 тЙд N` the ion-only parity reachability route gives the `╬╗ = 1`, `D > 0` endpoint wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general`. **Case (ii) bridges**: the case-(ii) path-region lemmas show the deformation path stays in `╬╗ тЙе 1`, `D тЙд 0`; the first case-(ii) target wrappers reuse the balanced-sector PF bridge while leaving balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2` explicit; the strict-gap wrappers replace the direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input by the strict sector gap; the no-full-finrank wrappers then use sector projection and Hermitian below-min exclusion to remove the full `finrank <= 2` input once strict gap is supplied; the crossing-callback bridge compresses the remaining strict-gap derivation to a single target crossing contradiction callback; the path-callback bridge reduces that target callback to a contradiction for crossings along the case-(ii) path; the crossing-set bridge reduces the path callback to contradiction for non-empty `perMCrossingSet M тИй Icc 0 1`; the first-crossing bridge reduces crossing-set non-emptiness to contradiction at `sInf (perMCrossingSet M тИй Icc 0 1)`; the argmin-first-crossing bridge reduces the target contradiction to the sector whose first crossing minimises those `sInf` values; the first-crossing finrank bridge proves that argmin callback from a `finrank <= 2` bound at the selected first crossing; the path-global finrank bridge moves this to every `t тИИ Icc 0 1`; the block-path finrank bridge reduces it to full-ground-energy parity-block submatrix simplicity; and the block-PF/min bridge reduces that to pathwise bare parity-block PF simplicity plus PF/min identification; and the parity-gauge sign layer introduces the case-(ii) block gauge needed to flip exactly the ┬▒2 parity-bond and single-ion moves; and the local sign layer proves strict shifted-entry positivity for transverse, parity-bond, and single-ion elementary moves; and the block reachability, block non-negativity, step-support, raw support-classification, and boundary move-set bridges reduce parity-block irreducibility to conditional irreducibility under the bipartite support-zero assumption on `J`. | The public general spin-S `2 тЙд N` parameter-region wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` package the general spin-S surface. The spin-`1/2` wrappers `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` and `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` now package the exact `N = 1` surface: `-1 < ╬╗ < 1`, `D тЙе 0`; `╬╗ = 1`, using the scalar-shift boundary; and strict case (ii) `1 < ╬╗`, `D тЙд 0`. |

relations (`tJFillingCompress_su2_12`/`_23`/`_31`) and `─д_W`-commutativity (`tJFillingCompress_tJHamiltonian_commute_one`/`_two`/`_three`) тАФ exactly the inputs of the matrix Theorem A.17; axiom-free. **The filling embedding is an isometry + the Rayleigh bridge** (`TJFillingRayleighBridge.lean`): `tJFillingEmbedding_conjTranspose_mul_self` (`Tс┤┤ T = 1`), `tJFillingExpansion_dotProduct_self` (`тЯиT c, T cтЯй=тЯиc,cтЯй`), and `rayleighOnVec_tJFillingCompress` (`rayleighOnVec ─д_tJ (tJFillingExpansion c) = rayleighOnVec ─д_W c`) тАФ connecting `groundEnergyAtFilling` to the matrix `─д_W`; axiom-free. **The eigenvector lift is proven** (`TJFillingEigenLift.lean`, `mulVec_tJFillingExpansion_of_compress_eigen`): an eigenvector of `compress(A)` at `E` lifts (when `A` preserves `W`) to an eigenvector of `A` at `E` on `W`, via the projection identity `T Tс┤┤ = id` on `W`; plus `tJFillingExpansion_mem_tJFillingWSubmodule`; axiom-free. Remaining for E2-тЙе: apply the matrix A.17 (`exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate`) to the min eigenvector of `─д_W` (its hypotheses are the proven compressed su(2)/Hermitian/commute), lift it (this lemma), use odd `Ne` to force `┼Ь┬│=┬╜` (`TJFillingSpinZDiag.lean`: `┼Ь┬│` is diagonal on filling expansions, `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_tJFillingExpansion`, and the only `┼Ь┬│=0` filling state is `0` for odd `Ne`, `tJFillingExpansion_eq_zero_of_spinZ_mulVec_eq_zero`) тЯ╣ `╬╝ тЙд hermitianMinEigenvalue ─д_W`, identify `groundEnergyAtFilling = hermitianMinEigenvalue ─д_W` via the bridge, completing `groundEnergyAtFilling = ╬╝`. **E2 is now COMPLETE** (`TJGroundEnergyGe.lean`): `tJ_perronFrobeniusMin_le_hermitianMinEigenvalue` (the W-restricted A.17 application), `tJ_groundEnergyAtFilling_ge_of_sectorMin` (`╬╝ тЙд groundEnergyAtFilling`), and the capstone `tJHamiltonian_groundEnergyAtFilling_eq_perronFrobeniusMin` (`groundEnergyAtFilling = ╬╝`, with the strictly-positive PF eigenvector `v` at `╬╝`) тАФ resting only on Theorem A.17 (`exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate`, **now discharged** ┬зA.3.2). **E3a (the SU(2) upper-bound seed, `TJGroundSpinHalfFinrank.lean`) is now COMPLETE**: `tJ_groundSubmodule_spinHalf_finrank_le_one` тАФ `finrank тДВ (groundSubmoduleAtFilling ─д_tJ Ne тКУ (┼Ь┬│=┬╜)) тЙд 1` тАФ via `groundEnergyAtFilling = ╬╝`, the PF real `finrank тЙд 1`, the realтЖФcomplex eigenspace bridge `matrix_complex_eigenspace_finrank_le_one_of_real`, and the injective `тДВ`-linear embedding `tJExpansionCoeffтВЧ` (`╬ж тЖж tJExpansionCoeff ╬ж`, injective by sector-support) of the block into the complex sector eigenspace at `╬╝`; supporting lemma `tJ_spinHalf_W_complexSectorEigen` (a block element's coefficient vector is a complex sector-matrix eigenvector at `╬╝`); no new axioms. **E3b PR1 (`TJSectorRaise.lean`, toward lifting the PF vector to a highest weight) is COMPLETE**: the single-site raising operator `┼ЬтБ║_x = ─ЙтАа_{xтЖС}─Й_{xтЖУ}` is **sign-free** on the sector basis тАФ `fermionSiteSpinPlus_mulVec_tJConfigOf_of_down` (`s x=тЖУ тЯ╣ ┼ЬтБ║_x|╬ж_sтЯй = |╬ж_{s with xтЖжтЖС}тЯй` with net JordanтАУWigner sign `+1`, since the adjacent orbitals `(xтЖС,xтЖУ)=(2x,2x+1)` with `2x` empty collapse the two strings) + `fermionSiteSpinPlus_mulVec_tJConfigOf_of_not_down` (`s xтЙатЖУ тЯ╣ ┼ЬтБ║_x|╬ж_sтЯй=0`) + the config identity `tJConfigOf_update_raise`; fully axiom-free. This Marshall sign-freeness makes the iterated `(┼ЬтБ║)^m` have nonnegative config coefficients, so a strictly-positive coordinate (from `v q > 0`) gives the non-vanishing `(┼ЬтБ║)^((Ne-1)/2) ╬жтВА тЙа 0`. **E3b PR2 (`TJRaisingTower.lean`) is COMPLETE**: eigenvalue tracking along the raising tower тАФ `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_spinPlusPow` (`┼Ь┬│ (┼ЬтБ║)^k v = (m+k)(┼ЬтБ║)^k v`, via `[┼Ь┬│,┼ЬтБ║]=┼ЬтБ║`), `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_spinPlusPow` (energy `╬╝` preserved, `[─д_tJ,┼ЬтБ║]=0`), `fermionTotalNumber_mulVec_spinPlusPow` (number `Ne` preserved, `[N╠В,┼ЬтБ║]=0`); so `(┼ЬтБ║)^k ╬жтВА`, when nonzero, is a fixed-`Ne`, energy-`╬╝`, `┼Ь┬│=┬╜+k` eigenvector; fully axiom-free. **E3b PR3 (`TJRaisingTermination.lean`) is COMPLETE**: the raising tower terminates тАФ `fermionTotalDownNumber_mul_fermionTotalSpinPlus` (`[N╠В_тЖУ,┼ЬтБ║]=тИТ┼ЬтБ║`), `fermionTotalDownNumber_mulVec_spinPlusPow` (`N╠В_тЖУ (┼ЬтБ║)^k v = (mтИТk)(┼ЬтБ║)^k v`), and `spinPlusPow_succ_eq_zero_of_downNumber` (`N╠В_тЖУ v = m v тЯ╣ (┼ЬтБ║)^(m+1) v = 0`, since the `N╠В_тЖУ`-eigenvalue `тИТ1` forces `(тЖУcount+1)┬╖╧И(w)=0` per config); fully axiom-free. mirrors #4305; axiom-free); PR3i `TJHalfFillingBondAction.lean` `tJExchangeBond_mulVec_tJConfigOf_full` (the exchange bond is a half spin-swap on the filled sector: `(┬╝ n╠В_x n╠В_y тИТ ┼Ь_x┬╖┼Ь_y)|╬ж_sтЯй=┬╜(|╬ж_sтЯйтИТ|╬ж_{tJSpinSwap s x y}тЯй)` via the sign-free swap over the 4 spin cases; ground states of `tJExchange` have spin-swap-invariant bond amplitudes тАФ toward the degeneracy upper bound; axiom-free); PR3i-2 `TJHalfFillingBondGround.lean` `tJ_ground_bond_mulVec_eq_zero` (a half-filling ground state is annihilated by every adjacent bond: `bond_xy v=0` via the PSD sum `тЯиv,tJExchange vтЯй=0` тЗТ each `тЯиv,bond_xy vтЯй=0` тЗТ `╬Ф_xy v=0`; axiom-free); PR3i-3a `TJHalfFillingAmplitude.lean` `tJ_ground_amplitude_swap_invariant` (half-filling ground amplitudes are spin-swap invariant: `v(tJConfigOf s)=v(tJConfigOf(tJSpinSwap s x y))` for adjacent bonds, via filling-basis expansion + the bond half-swap action + `bond_xy v=0`; `tJFillingSwap` sector permutation; axiom-free); PR3i-3b `TJHalfFillingUpCount.lean` `tJ_ground_amplitude_eq_of_same_upCount` (half-filling ground amplitudes depend only on the up-count: equal up-counts тЯ╣ equal value-counts тЯ╣ adjacent-swap reachable [`adjacentSwapReachable_of_same_counts`] тЯ╣ amplitudes equal by reachability induction over the per-bond swap invariance; axiom-free); PR3i-3c `TJHalfFillingDegeneracyUpper.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_groundSubmodule_finrank_le` (**upper bound `finrank G тЙд N+2`**: ground states determined by amplitudes constant on the N+2 up-count classes тЯ╣ injective `G тЖк (Fin(N+2)тЖТтДВ)`; with the lower bound #4326 тЯ╣ `finrank G = N+2`; axiom-free); **PR3-cap `TJHalfFillingMaximalSpin.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_isMaximalSpinMultiplet`** (the half-filling t-J ground subspace is the maximal-spin `(N+2)`-fold multiplet: `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule N G (N+1)`, assembling `finrank=N+2` with the all-up SU(2) tower spanning `G`; boundary case of Theorem 11.26's t-J side; axiom-free, no A.17); **PR-unify `TJMaximalSpinUnified.lean` `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`** (the d=1 t-J ground subspace is the maximal-spin multiplet for ALL odd `Ne тЙд K+1`: metallic `Ne<K+1` via `proposition_11_24` + half-filling `Ne=K+1`; the half-filling chain generalized to drop `0<N` so `K=0` is covered; t-J input to Theorem 11.26, rests on A.17 via Prop 11.24); **PR-final: `theorem_11_26` is now a PROVED theorem** (`MetallicFerroModel.lean`, Issue #4314): discharged from `lemma_11_25` (documented strong-coupling-equivalence axiom) + `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`; `#print axioms theorem_11_26` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound, lemma_11_25]` (A.17's `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` now discharged ┬зA.3.2). Tasaki ┬з11.5.3 metallic-ferromagnetism Theorem 11.26 complete + **`theorem_11_26` (PROVED, Issue #4314)** (d=1, `NeтЙдK+1=|E|` odd (Tasaki's `NтЙдL`) тЗТ `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule (3K+2) (groundSubmoduleAtFilling (decHubbardHamiltonian тАж) Ne) Ne` for large `t,U` тАФ ground `S_tot=Ne/2`, `Ne+1`-fold; metallic when `Ne<K+1`); discharged from `lemma_11_25` (documented axiom) + `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`, so the model and Theorem 11.26 are proved modulo `lemma_11_25` (A.17 now discharged); Theorem 11.27 still a documented axiom); **generic single-particle-state operators (`SpinfulVectorOperator.lean`)**: `spinfulCreationFromVector`/`spinfulAnnihilationFromVector` (`─И_╧Г(╧Ж)=╬г_x ╧Ж(x)─Й_{x,╧Г}`), shared helper; axiom-free; **┬з11.5.4 TanakaтАУTasaki model + Theorem 11.27 (AXIOMATIZED, `TanakaTasakiModel.lean`, Issue #4198) тАФ ┬з11.5/Chapter 11 capstone**: the heavily-decorated lattice `╬Ы=E├Ч{1,2,3}тИкI├Ч{1,2}` (externals triplicated, internals duplicated), d=1 in `Fin(5K+5)` (`ttExtSite`/`ttIntSite`); special states `ttAlpha`/`ttBeta`/`ttDeltaP`/`ttDeltaI` (eqs. (11.5.19)тАУ(11.5.22), `├в` with `1/тИЪ(3+4╬╜┬▓)`) + ops via `spinfulCreationFromVector`; `ttHopping` (`╬г_{тЯиp,qтЯй}(тИТs ├втАа├втИТt b╠ВтАаb╠В)+uтВБ╬гb╠ВтАаb╠В+uтВВ╬гd╠ВтАаd╠В`, eq. (11.5.24)) + `ttInteraction` + `ttHamiltonian` (finite); genuine `uтВВ,UтЖСтИЮ` limit objects `ttDKernel` (`d╠В╬ж=0`, via Theorem A.12) + `ttEffectiveHamiltonian` (`uтВВ,U=тИЮ` effective hopping); **`axiom theorem_11_27`** (d=1, `uтВБ>2(|s|+2|t|)`, `K+1тЙдNeтЙд2(K+1)` (Tasaki `L^dтЙдNтЙд2L^d`) тЗТ **in the limit** every ground state in `groundSubmoduleAtFilling (ttEffectiveHamiltonian тАж) Ne тКУ ttDKernel` has max spin `S_tot=Ne/2` тАФ taken as a genuine limit, not finite thresholds (Tasaki proves it only in the limit, warns finite `uтВВ,U` is not expected to work); weaker than the multiplet predicate, Tasaki claims only the spin; metallic when `1<Ne/L<2`); model axiom-free, `theorem_11_27` documented axiom ([63])) |

| Appendix A (Tasaki Mathematical Appendices) | The book-order content after Chapter 11; foundations for the deferred Chapter-11 proof discharges (frustration-free A.9/A.10, limit A.11/A.12, PerronтАУFrobenius A.17/A.18 тАФ the last largely already in `Math/PerronFrobenius*`/`CollatzWielandt*`). **Complete тАФ A.1тАУA.28 all formalized in book order** (Issues [#4205](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4205) + [#4224](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4224), both closed; see the **status & axiomatization policy** note below the Roadmap): **Theorem A.1 (Lie product formula)** `e^{A+B} = lim_N (e^{A/N}e^{B/N})^N` **now proved (axiom-free)** (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/LieProduct.lean`, `lieProductFormula` тАФ mathlib has only the commuting case): generic `trotterProductFormula` in a complete normed `тДЭ`-algebra via exponential-series tail bounds (`тАЦe^XтИТ1тИТXтАЦ тЙд тАЦXтАЦ┬▓e^{тАЦXтАЦ}` etc.) + telescoping power estimate (`тАЦCтБ┐тИТDтБ┐тАЦ тЙд n┬╖M^{nтИТ1}┬╖тАЦCтИТDтАЦ`) + `O(s┬▓)` product comparison + the exact `n`-th-power identity, instantiated for matrices under the scoped operator norm; **Lemmas A.4** (Hermitian `├В` is `тЙе0` iff all eigenvalues `тЙе0`), **A.5** (`├В,B╠ВтЙе0 тЗТ ├В+B╠ВтЙе0`), **A.6** (`B╠ВтАаB╠ВтЙе0`, and conversely every `├ВтЙе0` is `─И┬▓` for a *unique* PSD square root `─И=тИЪ├В` via `cfc Real.sqrt`, existence+uniqueness) **proved** (axiom-free, `Math/PosSemidef/Basics.lean`). **Theorem A.7** (Weyl monotonicity: Hermitian `├ВтЙдB╠В` тЗТ `i`-th sorted eigenvalue `├В.eigenvaluesтВА i тЙд B╠В.eigenvaluesтВА i`) **now proved (axiom-free)** via the CourantтАУFischer block/pigeonhole argument (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/CourantFischer.lean`: spectral Rayleigh-sum `reтЯиx,TxтЯй=тИС╬╗с╡втАЦтЯиbс╡в,xтЯйтАЦ┬▓` + block bounds + the top-`(i+1)`/bottom-`(cardтИТi)` eigenspaces meeting by finrank pigeonhole; `EigenvalueMonotone.lean`). **Corollary A.8** (`├ВтЙдB╠В тЗТ Tr e^├В тЙд Tr e^B╠В`, eq A.2.31) **now proved (axiom-free)** (`MatrixAnalysis/TraceExpMonotone.lean`: spectral mapping `Tr e^├В = ╬г e^{a_j}` via `Matrix.exp_conj` + `exp_diagonal` + `trace_mul_cycle`, then A.7 Weyl + `Real.exp` monotone). **Lemmas A.9/A.10** (frustration-free Hamiltonian `─д=╬г─ет▒╝`, `─ет▒╝тЙе╬╡т▒╝`: A.9 simultaneous eigenstate тЗТ ground state at `╬г╬╡т▒╝`; A.10 converse) **proved** (axiom-free, `FrustrationFree.lean`). **Lemma A.11** (`├ВтЙе0 тИз тЯи╬ж|├В|╬жтЯй=0 тЗТ ├В╬ж=0`; `├В=B╠ВтАаB╠В тЗТ B╠В╬ж=0`; + angular-momentum corollary `─┤┬▓╬ж=0 тЗТ ─┤тБ╜с╡ЕтБ╛╬ж=0`) **proved** (axiom-free, `PosSemidef/Kernel.lean`, over the existing `RayleighPosSemidefKernel`). **Theorem A.12** (`─д_v=─дтВА+vV╠В`, `V╠ВтЙе0`: `vтЖСтИЮ` finite-energy eigenstate families converging to nonzero `╬ж` satisfy the effective Schr├╢dinger eq `P╠ВтВА─дтВА╬ж=E╬ж` on `HтВА=ker V╠В` тАФ documented axiom, `EffectiveLimit.lean`, weak-form + Tendsto, non-vacuous finite-energyтЗТeffective direction). **Appendix A.2 complete.** **┬зA.3 angular momentum**: **Lemma A.14** (su(2) ladder) тАФ operator identity `─┤тБ╗─┤тБ║=─┤┬▓тИТ─┤┬│(─┤┬│+1)` (eq A.3.7) + ladder non-vanishing (`тИТJтЙдM<J`, `╬жтЙа0` тЗТ `─┤тБ║╬жтЙа0`) + raising **membership** (`─┤тБ║╬жтИИH_{J,M+1}`, via `[─┤┬│,─┤тБ║]=─┤тБ║` + `[─┤┬▓,─┤тБ║]=0`) + **lowering** direction (`─┤тБ║─┤тБ╗=─┤┬▓тИТ─┤┬│(─┤┬│тИТ1)` eq A.3.8, `─┤тБ╗╬жтЙа0` for `тИТJ<MтЙдJ`, `─┤тБ╗╬жтИИH_{J,MтИТ1}`) **proved** тАФ A.14 fully done (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Ladder.lean`). **Lemma A.15 (spin bound, part 1)**: norm identities `тАЦ─┤┬▒╬жтАЦ┬▓={J(J+1)тИТM(M┬▒1)}тАЦ╬жтАЦ┬▓` (eq A.3.9, Hermitian ─┤^╬▒) `angRaise/angLower_normSq` + `angMom_abs_le_J` (`тИТJтЙдMтЙдJ`, i.e. `JтИТM,J+MтЙе0`) **proved** (axiom-free). **Lemma A.15 (integrality)** + **Theorem A.13 (`J = n/2`)**: ladder-termination `raiseIter` (iterated `─┤тБ║`) with `raiseIter_eigenspace`/`raiseIter_ne_zero` gives `JтИТM тИИ тДдтЙе0` (`angMom_sub_mem_nat`); the gauge-reflected `(─┤┬╣,тИТ─┤┬▓,тИТ─┤┬│)` gives `J+M тИИ тДдтЙе0`, so `2J=(JтИТM)+(J+M)тИИтДдтЙе0`, i.e. `J=n/2` (`angMom_J_eq_half_nat`) **proved** (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Quantization.lean`). **Theorem A.16 (SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy)**: for SU(2)-invariant `─д` (`[─д,─┤с╡Е]=0`), a joint energy eigenstate in `H_{J,M0}` yields nonzero same-energy companions in every `H_{J,JтИТk}` (`kтЙд2J`) тАФ `ham_su2_multiplet` (energy `E` is тЙе`(2J+1)`-fold degenerate), proved via raise-to-top + reflected-lowering ladder + `ham_mulVec_raiseIter` (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Multiplet.lean`). **Theorem A.17 (spin-0/1-2 sector suffices)**: every energy eigenvalue of an SU(2)-invariant `─д` has an eigenstate with `─┤┬│=0` or `─┤┬│=1/2` тАФ `ham_eigenstate_spin_zero_or_half` (corollary of A.16+A.13; the lone simultaneous-diagonalization step `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` is **now proved (axiom-free)** via the generic common eigenvector of two commuting Hermitian operators on an invariant subspace (`Math/CommutingHermitianEigenvector.lean`: `exists_common_eigenvector_of_isHermitian_commute`), the Casimir `─┤┬▓` being PSD and commuting with `─┤┬│`/`─д`, `AngularMomentum/SpinHalfSector.lean`). **Theorem A.18 (PerronтАУFrobenius, real symmetric)**: a real symmetric `M` with `(c┬╖1тИТM)` irreducible (off-diag `тЙд0` + connectivity) has a nondegenerate lowest eigenvalue with a strictly positive eigenvector тАФ `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` (reuses the project's CollatzтАУWielandt PF + eigenspace simplicity; the lowest-eigenvalue identification is the variational `|w|`-argument eq A.4.1), proved (axiom-free, `PerronFrobeniusSymmetric.lean`). **Theorems A.19/A.20 (polar + SVD)**: `matrix_polar_decomposition` (`A = W C`, `W` unitary, `C` PSD) and `matrix_singular_value_decomposition` (`A = U D VтАа`, `U,V` unitary, `D = diagonal d` with `d тЙе 0`) тАФ **now proved (axiom-free)**: SVD built from the spectral theorem of `Aс┤┤A` (`eigenvectorUnitary` `V`, eigenvalues `╬╗_i тЙе 0`, `d_i = тИЪ╬╗_i`), with the normalised images `u_i = d_iтБ╗┬╣┬╖A v_i` extended to an orthonormal basis (`exists_orthonormalBasis_extension_of_card_eq`) giving `U`, and `A V = U D` columnwise; polar follows as `W = U Vс┤┤`, `C = V D Vс┤┤` (`MatrixAnalysis/Decomposition.lean`). **Theorems A.21/A.22 (Wigner)**: `wignerAutomorphism_unitary` (linear тИЧ-automorphism of `M(H)` тЯ╣ `╬У(├В)=├ЫтАа├В├Ы`) + `wignerAutomorphism_antiunitary` (antilinear тЯ╣ `├ЫтАа├В╠Д├Ы`) + `wignerProjection` (rank-1-projection map preserving `Tr[P PтА▓]` тЯ╣ (anti)unitary `V╠В`) тАФ documented axioms over `Matrix D D тДВ` (`WignerTheorem.lean`, Issue #4224). **Definition A.23 + Theorem A.24 (states / BanachтАУAlaoglu)**: `IsState` (weak-тИЧ-continuous `╧Ж : WeakDual тДВ A` with `╧Ж 1 = 1`, `0 тЙд ╧Ж(aтАаa)`) over an abstract unital C*-algebra + `stateSpace_isCompact` (state space weak-тИЧ compact, documented axiom) тАФ `CStarAlgebra/State.lean`. **Definitions A.25/A.27 + Theorem A.26 (ground states)**: dynamics modelled by a derivation `╬┤=[─д,┬╖]`; `IsGroundState` (`0 тЙд ╧Й(aтАа┬╖╬┤a)`) + `HasNonzeroGap` (`╬│>0`, `╧Й(aтАа╬┤a) тЙе ╬│╧Й(aтАаa)` on `╧Й(a)=0`) + `groundState_variational` (ground state тЯ║ `╧Й(─д_L)` least over states agreeing outside ╬Ы_L, documented axiom) тАФ `CStarAlgebra/GroundState.lean`. **Theorem A.28 (GNS construction)**: `gns_construction` тАФ every state `╧Б` on a C*-algebra has a GNS triple `(H_╧Б, ╧А_╧Б, ╬й_╧Б)` (`тИЧ`-representation `╧А : A тЖТтЛЖтВР[тДВ] (HтЖТL H)` + cyclic `╬й`) with `╧Б(├В)=тЯи╬й,╧А(├В)╬йтЯй` and `{╧А(├В)╬й}` dense; documented axiom (mathlib has the GNS machinery), `CStarAlgebra/GNS.lean`. **┬зA.6тАУ┬зA.7 (Wigner / states / ground states / GNS) complete тЖТ Appendix A complete.** Prove-first where mathlib supports, axiomatize-first for the heavy analytic results, strict book order (see the status & axiomatization-policy note below). |

### Appendix A: status and axiomatization policy

Tasaki's **Appendix A is fully formalized in book order (A.1тАУA.28)**, and the
entire Tasaki text up to and including Chapter 11 (┬з11.5) plus this appendix is
now covered. The appendix splits into two kinds of items:

- **Proved (axiom-free)** тАФ the linear-algebra and angular-momentum core that
  `mathlib` supports directly: **A.1** (the Lie product / Trotter formula, proved
  from scratch in a complete normed `тДЭ`-algebra), **A.4тАУA.6**
  (positive-semidefinite basics + unique
  PSD square root), **A.7тАУA.8** (Weyl eigenvalue monotonicity via CourantтАУFischer,
  and the trace-exp monotonicity through the spectral mapping for `exp`),
  **A.9тАУA.11** (frustration-free Hamiltonians, energy-form
  kernels), **A.13тАУA.16** (angular-momentum quantization `J = n/2`, the ladder,
  and SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy), **A.17** (spin-0/half sector via the common
  eigenvector of commuting Hermitians), **A.18** (PerronтАУFrobenius for a real
  symmetric matrix, via the project's CollatzтАУWielandt development and the
  variational `|w|`-argument), and **A.19тАУA.20** (polar + singular-value
  decompositions from the spectral theorem).
- **Documented axioms (faithful statements, deferred proofs)** тАФ **A.12**
  (strong-coupling effective Hamiltonian limit) and the
  operator-algebraic **A.21тАУA.28** (Wigner's
  theorem, states, BanachтАУAlaoglu, ground states of infinite systems, the GNS
  construction).

**Value judgment / policy.** The documented axioms above are kept as *faithful,
book-order statements* тАФ they record exactly what Tasaki proves тАФ but they are
**not active proof targets** of this project. The heavy functional-analytic and
operator-algebraic results (states on the quasi-local C\*-algebra, weak-тИЧ
compactness, ground states, GNS, Wigner) belong to a dedicated operator-algebra /
functional-analysis development; such a development may well be carried out
**separately** (for instance contributed to `mathlib`), and these axioms simply
**wait for that implementation**. Accordingly the project's policy is to
**axiomatize only the appendix results that Tasaki's formalized main development
actually uses**, to **prove** the remaining ones where `mathlib` provides the
tools, and otherwise to leave a faithful axiom in place rather than invest in a
large bespoke operator-algebra build whose natural home is elsewhere. The
`#print axioms` of every theorem in the repository makes the precise dependency
on these documented axioms auditable.

## Formalized theorems

All items below are formally proved with **zero `sorry`**. Full
mathematical statements and proof sketches are in
[`tex/proof-guide.tex`](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/blob/main/tex/proof-guide.tex).

### Single-site Pauli operators

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬з2.1 eq. (2.1.8), p. 15. Cross-checked with Nielsen-Chuang
┬з2.1.3 Figure 2.2 (pp. 65-66, definitions), Ex. 2.19 (p. 70,
Hermiticity), Ex. 2.41 (p. 78, `(╧Г^╬▒)┬▓ = I` and anticommutation),
Ex. 2.40 (p. 77, commutator, whence the cyclic products).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `pauliX/Y/Z_isHermitian` | `(╧Г^╬▒)тАа = ╧Г^╬▒` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX/Y/Z_mul_self` | `(╧Г^╬▒)┬▓ = I` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_mul_pauliY` etc. | `╧Г^x ╧Г^y = i┬╖╧Г^z` (cyclic) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_anticomm_pauliY` etc. | `╧Г^╬▒ ╧Г^╬▓ + ╧Г^╬▓ ╧Г^╬▒ = 0` (╬▒ тЙа ╬▓) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |

### Spin-1/2 rotation operators (Tasaki ┬з2.1 eq. (2.1.26))

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬з2.1 eq. (2.1.26), p. 17 (closed form) and eq. (2.1.23),
p. 16 (`├Ы_{2╧А} = -1` for half-odd-integer spin).

| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_pi` | `├Ы^(╬▒)_╧А = -2i ┬╖ ┼Ь^(╬▒)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_pi_sq` | `(├Ы^(╬▒)_╧А)┬▓ = -1` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_pi_anticomm_spinHalfRot2_pi` (and cyclic) | `{├Ы^(╬▒)_╧А, ├Ы^(╬▓)_╧А} = 0` for `╬▒ тЙа ╬▓` (Tasaki (2.1.25)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_pi_conjTranspose` | `(├Ы^(╬▒)_╧А)тАа = 2i ┬╖ ┼Ь^(╬▒)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_pi_mul_spinHalfRot2_pi` (and cyclic) | `├Ы^(╬▒)_╧А ┬╖ ├Ы^(╬▓)_╧А = ├Ы^(╬│)_╧А` (Tasaki (2.1.29), S=1/2) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_pi_conj_spinHalfOp_*` | axis invariance and sign flip at ╬╕=╧А (Tasaki (2.1.15)/(2.1.21)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_spinHalfOp{2,3,1}` | `(├Ы^(╬▒)_╬╕)тАа ┼Ь^(╬▓) ├Ы^(╬▒)_╬╕ = cos ╬╕ ┬╖ ┼Ь^(╬▓) - sin ╬╕ ┬╖ ┼Ь^(╬│)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.16), even-╬╡ cyclic triple) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_spinHalfOp{3,1,2}` | `(├Ы^(╬▒)_╬╕)тАа ┼Ь^(╬▓) ├Ы^(╬▒)_╬╕ = cos ╬╕ ┬╖ ┼Ь^(╬▓) + sin ╬╕ ┬╖ ┼Ь^(╬│)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.16), odd-╬╡ triple) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | same-axis invariance `(├Ы^(╬▒)_╬╕)тАа ┼Ь^(╬▒) ├Ы^(╬▒)_╬╕ = ┼Ь^(╬▒)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.15)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_half_pi_conj_spinHalfOp_*` | `╧А/2`-rotation conjugation `(├Ы^(╬▒)_{╧А/2})тАа ┼Ь^(╬▓) ├Ы^(╬▒)_{╧А/2} = -╬╡^{╬▒╬▓╬│} ┼Ь^(╬│)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.22), 6 cases) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_eq_exp` | `├Ы^(3)_╬╕ = exp(-i╬╕ ┼Ь^(3))` via `Matrix.exp_diagonal` + Euler (Problem 2.1.b, axis 3) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_mul_spinHalfRot2_mulVec_spinHalfUp` | `├Ы^(3)_╧Ж ├Ы^(2)_╬╕ |╧И^тЖСтЯй = e^{-i╧Ж/2} cos(╬╕/2) |╧И^тЖСтЯй + e^{i╧Ж/2} sin(╬╕/2) |╧И^тЖУтЯй` (coherent state, Problem 2.1.d) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_mul_spinHalfRot2_mulVec_spinHalfDown` | `├Ы^(3)_╧Ж ├Ы^(2)_╬╕ |╧И^тЖУтЯй = -e^{-i╧Ж/2} sin(╬╕/2) |╧И^тЖСтЯй + e^{i╧Ж/2} cos(╬╕/2) |╧И^тЖУтЯй` (rotation of spin-down, Problem 2.2.c auxiliary) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_half_pi_mul_spinHalfRot2_half_pi_mulVec_spinHalfUp` | specialization at ╬╕ = ╧Ж = ╧А/2 (Problem 2.1.e) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfDotVec` / `spinHalfDotVec_isHermitian` | vector inner product `┼Ь ┬╖ v := ╬г_╬▒ v_╬▒ ┼Ь^(╬▒)` and its Hermiticity (cf. (2.1.19)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_commute_spinHalfOp3_smul` | same-axis rotation commutes with `v ┬╖ ┼Ь^(3)` (cf. (2.1.20) along axis) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `hadamard` / `hadamard_mul_self` | the Hadamard basis-change matrix `W = (1/тИЪ2)┬╖!![1,1;1,-1]` and `W┬╖W = 1` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `hadamard_mul_spinHalfOp1_mul_hadamard` | `W ┬╖ ┼Ь^(1) ┬╖ W = ┼Ь^(3)` (basis change between ╧Г^x and ╧Г^z) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `hadamard_mul_spinHalfOp3_mul_hadamard` | `W ┬╖ ┼Ь^(3) ┬╖ W = ┼Ь^(1)` (inverse basis change) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_eq_hadamard_conj` | `├Ы^(1)_╬╕ = W ┬╖ ├Ы^(3)_╬╕ ┬╖ W` (axis 1 rotation as Hadamard conjugate of axis 3) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_eq_exp` | `├Ы^(1)_╬╕ = exp(-i╬╕ ┼Ь^(1))` via Hadamard conjugation + `Matrix.exp_conj` (Problem 2.1.b, axis 1) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `yDiag` / `yDiagAdj` / `yDiag_mul_yDiagAdj` / `yDiag_mul_spinHalfOp3_mul_yDiagAdj` | y-axis basis-change unitary `V` with `V┬╖VтАа = 1` and `V┬╖┼Ь^(3)┬╖VтАа = ┼Ь^(2)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot2_eq_yDiag_conj` / `spinHalfRot2_eq_exp` | `├Ы^(2)_╬╕ = V┬╖├Ы^(3)_╬╕┬╖VтАа` and `├Ы^(2)_╬╕ = exp(-i╬╕ ┼Ь^(2))` (Problem 2.1.b, axis 2) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |

### 3D rotation matrices `R^(╬▒)_╬╕` (general ╬╕, Tasaki ┬з2.1 eq. (2.1.11))

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `rot3D{1,2,3} ╬╕` | 3├Ч3 real rotation matrices by angle ╬╕ about each axis | `Quantum/Rotation3D.lean` |
| `rot3D{1,2,3}_zero` | `R^(╬▒)_0 = 1` | `Quantum/Rotation3D.lean` |
| `rot3D{1,2,3}_pi` | `R^(╬▒)_╧А` from general formula matches explicit ╧А-rotation | `Quantum/Rotation3D.lean` |

### ZтВВ ├Ч ZтВВ representation (Tasaki ┬з2.1 eqs. (2.1.27)-(2.1.34))

The ZтВВ ├Ч ZтВВ structure is proved across files:
- S = 1/2 (projective, eq. (2.1.31)): `spinHalfRot*_pi_sq = -1`, anticommutation, products.
- S = 1 (genuine, eq. (2.1.27)): `spinOnePiRot*_sq = 1`, commutation.

See `Quantum/Z2Z2.lean` for the unified documentation.

### Polynomial-basis decomposition for S = 1 (Tasaki ┬з2.1 Problem 2.1.a, S = 1)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum
Many-Body Systems*, ┬з2.1 Problem 2.1.a, p. 15 + solution S.1, p. 493.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinOneProj{Plus,Zero,Minus}` | the three diagonal projectors `\|╧И^╧ГтЯйтЯи╧И^╧Г\|` (╧Г тИИ {+1, 0, -1}) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneProj{Plus,Zero,Minus}_eq_polynomial` | each diagonal projector equals a polynomial in `┼Ь^(3)` (Lagrange interpolation) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{01,02,10,12,20,21}` | the six off-diagonal matrix units `\|╧И^╧ДтЯйтЯи╧И^╧Г\|` (╧Д тЙа ╧Г) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{01,12}_eq_polynomial` | `(1/тИЪ2) ┼Ь^- ┬╖ P_╧Г` for the two single-step lowering units | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{10,21}_eq_polynomial` | `(1/тИЪ2) ┼Ь^+ ┬╖ P_╧Г` for the two single-step raising units | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit02_eq_polynomial` | `(1/2) (┼Ь^-)┬▓ ┬╖ P_+` for the double-step lowering unit | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit20_eq_polynomial` | `(1/2) (┼Ь^+)┬▓ ┬╖ P_-` for the double-step raising unit | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOne_decomposition` | every 3├Ч3 complex matrix is a linear combination of the 9 matrix units (entry-wise); combined with the polynomial expressions above this gives Tasaki Problem 2.1.a for `S = 1` | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |

### Spin-`S` operators (general S тЙе 0, parameterised by `N = 2S : тДХ`)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body Systems*, ┬з2.1 Problem 2.1.a (p. 15) and solution S.1 (p. 493).

Generic spin-`S` operators live on `Matrix (Fin (N + 1)) (Fin (N + 1)) тДВ`, with `N = 2S : тДХ` (so `N = 1` тЗФ `S = 1/2`, `N = 2` тЗФ `S = 1`). Tracking issue #458 (Tasaki ┬з2.1 P1d''' general-S generalisation).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinSOp3` | `┼Ь^(3) := diag(N/2, N/2 - 1, тАж, -N/2)` for `N : тДХ` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus` / `spinSOpMinus` | raising/lowering operators with matrix entries `тИЪ(k┬╖(N тИТ k + 1))` (resp. `тИЪ((N тИТ k)(k + 1))`) on the immediate sub/super-diagonal | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp1` / `spinSOp2` | `┼Ь^(1) := (┼Ь^+ + ┼Ь^-) / 2`, `┼Ь^(2) := (┼Ь^+ тИТ ┼Ь^-) / (2 i)` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_apply_top` / `_bottom` | `┼Ь^+` annihilates `\|NтЯй` (highest weight); `┼Ь^-` annihilates `\|0тЯй` (lowest weight) тАФ the ladder boundaries | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp{Plus,Minus}` | **Cartan relations** `[┼Ь^{(3)}, ┼Ь^+] = ┼Ь^+` and `[┼Ь^{(3)}, ┼Ь^-] = -┼Ь^-`: `┼Ь^┬▒` shift the magnetic quantum number by `┬▒1`. Proved entry-wise via `Matrix.diagonal_mul` / `mul_diagonal` (╬▓-2 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Algebra.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_conjTranspose` / `spinSOp{1,2,3}_isHermitian` | adjointness `(┼Ь^+)с┤┤ = ┼Ь^-`, `(┼Ь^-)с┤┤ = ┼Ь^+`, and Hermiticity of `┼Ь^{(╬▒)}` for `╬▒ тИИ {1, 2, 3}` (╬▓-3 of Issue #458). The ladder adjointness follows from the matrix-entry symmetry; `┼Ь^{(1)}` and `┼Ь^{(2)}` use the `1/2` and `1/(2i)` self-conjugacy facts | `Quantum/SpinS/Hermitian.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj` / `spinSOp3_sub_smul_mul_diagProj` / `_self_mul_diagProj` | the **diagonal projector** `P_k := diag(╬┤_{i,k})` and the eigenvalue-action lemma `(┼Ь^{(3)} тИТ ╬╗_j тАв 1) ┬╖ P_k = (╬╗_k тИТ ╬╗_j) ┬╖ P_k`, with the annihilation specialisation at `j = k` (╬▓-4 of Issue #458). Foundation for the Lagrange-interpolation polynomial decomposition: each `P_k` will be expressed as `тИП_{j тЙа k} (┼Ь^{(3)} тИТ ╬╗_j тАв 1) / (╬╗_k тИТ ╬╗_j)` in a follow-up PR | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProj.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mul_diagProj` / `diagProj_mul_spinSOp3` / `spinSOp3_commute_diagProj` | the **eigenvalue equation** `┼Ь^{(3)} ┬╖ P_k = ╬╗_k ┬╖ P_k` (where `╬╗_k = (N : тДВ)/2 тИТ k`), with the symmetric `P_k ┬╖ ┼Ь^{(3)} = ╬╗_k ┬╖ P_k` and the resulting commutativity. Both are diagonal-times-diagonal computations (╬▓-5 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Lagrange.lean` |
| `mul_diagProj_apply` / `diagProj_mul_apply` / `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_mul_diagProj_apply` | **Column/row selection** by the diagonal projector: `(A ┬╖ P_k)[i, j] = A[i, k]` if `j = k` else `0` (and dually for `P_k ┬╖ A`). Specialised to `┼Ь^┬▒` produces off-diagonal matrix units (╬▓-6 of Issue #458) тАФ the building blocks of the polynomial decomposition theorem | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderProj.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_mul_spinSOpMinus` | **Ladder recursion**: `┼Ь^+ ┬╖ P_{k+1} ┬╖ ┼Ь^- = (k + 1)(N тИТ k) ┬╖ P_k`. The triple product collapses to a non-zero scalar multiple of `P_k`. Iterating from the lowest-weight projector `P_N` (itself a polynomial in `┼Ь^{(3)}`) yields every diagonal projector as a polynomial in `{1╠В, ┼Ь^{(╬▒)}}` (╬▓-7 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderRecursion.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_first` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_last` | **Ladder boundaries**: `┼Ь^+ ┬╖ P_0 = 0` (top of ladder) and `┼Ь^- ┬╖ P_N = 0` (bottom). The first/last columns of `┼Ь^┬▒` vanish, so multiplication by `P_{0/N}` (which selects that column) gives `0`. Terminate the recursion at the highest- and lowest-weight projectors (╬▓-8 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderBoundary.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_isHermitian` / `sum_spinSDiagProj_eq_one` | **Hermiticity** of each `P_k`, and the **resolution of the identity** `╬г_k P_k = 1`. The latter is the cornerstone for the matrix-unit decomposition: combined with the off-diagonal matrix units (╬▓-6, ╬▓-7), gives every matrix in `M_{N+1}(тДВ)` (╬▓-9 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjProperties.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_eq_single` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_eq_single` | **Off-diagonal matrix-unit decomposition**: `┼Ь^+ ┬╖ P_{i+1} = тИЪ((i+1)(NтИТi)) ┬╖ E_{i, i+1}` and `┼Ь^- ┬╖ P_i = тИЪ((NтИТi)(i+1)) ┬╖ E_{i+1, i}`, where `E_{a, b} := Matrix.single a b 1` is the (a, b) matrix unit (╬▓-10 of Issue #458). These are the simplest off-diagonal matrix units expressible via `┼Ь^┬▒` ladder action on diagonal projectors | `Quantum/SpinS/OffDiagUnit.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_sq_eq_diagonal` | `(┼Ь^{(3)})┬▓ = diag((N/2 тИТ i)┬▓)`. Step toward the **Casimir identity** `(┼Ь^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼Ь^{(2)})┬▓ + (┼Ь^{(3)})┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ 1` for general spin (╬▓-11 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Op3Square.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_spinSOpMinus_eq_diagonal` | `┼Ь^+ ┬╖ ┼Ь^- = diag((i + 1)(N тИТ i))`. The product is diagonal because `┼Ь^+[i, l] ┬╖ ┼Ь^-[l, j]` is non-zero only when `l = i + 1 = j + 1`, forcing `i = j`. Combined with the analogous `┼Ь^- ┬╖ ┼Ь^+` and `(┼Ь^{(3)})┬▓`, this assembles the Casimir identity (╬▓-12 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PlusMinusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOpMinus_mul_spinSOpPlus_eq_diagonal` | `┼Ь^- ┬╖ ┼Ь^+ = diag(i ┬╖ (N тИТ i + 1))`. Symmetric to ╬▓-12 with `┼Ь^┬▒` swapped (╬▓-13 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/MinusPlusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_sq_add_spinSOp2_sq` / `spinSOp_total_squared` | **Casimir identity** for general spin-`S`: `(┼Ь^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼Ь^{(2)})┬▓ + (┼Ь^{(3)})┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ 1`, equivalent to `S(S+1) ┬╖ 1` for `S = N/2`. Proves the irreducible spin-`S` representation has Casimir eigenvalue `S(S+1)` (Schur's lemma). The intermediate identity `(┼Ь^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼Ь^{(2)})┬▓ = (1/2)(┼Ь^+ ┬╖ ┼Ь^- + ┼Ь^- ┬╖ ┼Ь^+)` is proved using `module` (╬▓-14 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Casimir.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mulVec_basis` | spin-`S` eigenstate equation `┼Ь^{(3)} ┬╖ \|kтЯй = (N/2 тИТ k) ┬╖ \|kтЯй`, where `\|kтЯй := Pi.single k 1` is the `k`-th unit basis vector of `(Fin (N + 1) тЖТ тДВ)` (╬▓-15 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Eigenstates.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mulVec_basis` / `spinSOpMinus_mulVec_basis` | **Ladder action on basis vectors**: `┼Ь^+ ┬╖ \|kтЯй = тИЪ(k(N тИТ k + 1)) ┬╖ \|k тИТ 1тЯй` for `k тЙе 1`, and `┼Ь^- ┬╖ \|kтЯй = тИЪ((N тИТ k)(k + 1)) ┬╖ \|k + 1тЯй` for `k тЙд N тИТ 1`. The standard SU(2) ladder relations on the magnetic-quantum-number basis (╬▓-16 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderStates.lean` |
| `spinSOp_total_squared_mulVec_basis` | **Casimir eigenvalue on basis**: `(┼Ь)┬▓ ┬╖ \|kтЯй = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ \|kтЯй`. Direct consequence of `(┼Ь)┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ 1` (╬▓-14) тАФ every basis vector has the universal Casimir eigenvalue, reflecting that the spin-`S` representation is a single irreducible (Schur) (╬▓-17 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirEigenvalue.lean` |
| `spinSOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}_commute_total_squared` | **Casimir invariance**: each `┼Ь^{(╬▒)}` and `┼Ь^┬▒` commutes with the Casimir `(┼Ь)┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ 1`. Direct consequence of the scalar nature of the Casimir (╬▓-18 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirInvariance.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_commutator_spinSOpMinus` | **Third Cartan relation**: `[┼Ь^+, ┼Ь^-] = 2 ┬╖ ┼Ь^{(3)}`. Combines ╬▓-12 (`┼Ь^+ ┬╖ ┼Ь^- = diag((i + 1)(N тИТ i))`) and ╬▓-13 (`┼Ь^- ┬╖ ┼Ь^+ = diag(i (N тИТ i + 1))`); the difference `(i+1)(N-i) тИТ i(N-i+1) = N тИТ 2i = 2(N/2 тИТ i)` matches `2 ┬╖ ┼Ь^{(3)}` (╬▓-19 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Cartan3.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_commutator_spinSOp2` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼Ь^{(1)}, ┼Ь^{(2)}] = i ┬╖ ┼Ь^{(3)}`. Derived from the Cartan relations by algebraic manipulation through `┼Ь^{(1)} = (┼Ь^+ + ┼Ь^-)/2` and `┼Ь^{(2)} = (┼Ь^+ тИТ ┼Ь^-)/(2i)`: `(P+Q)(P-Q) тИТ (P-Q)(P+Q) = -2[P, Q] = -4 ┼Ь^{(3)}`, then scalar simplification `-1/I = I` (╬▓-20 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator.lean` |
| `spinSOp2_commutator_spinSOp3` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼Ь^{(2)}, ┼Ь^{(3)}] = i ┬╖ ┼Ь^{(1)}`. Derived from Cartan ╬▓-2 (`[┼Ь^{(3)}, ┼Ь^┬▒] = ┬▒┼Ь^┬▒`) via `┼Ь^{(2)} = (┼Ь^+ тИТ ┼Ь^-)/(2i)`: the commutator reduces to `(1/(2i)) (-┼Ь^+ тИТ ┼Ь^-) = (-1/(2i)) ┬╖ 2 ┼Ь^{(1)} = i ┬╖ ┼Ь^{(1)}` (╬▓-21 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator23.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp1` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼Ь^{(3)}, ┼Ь^{(1)}] = i ┬╖ ┼Ь^{(2)}`. Derived from Cartan ╬▓-2 via `┼Ь^{(1)} = (┼Ь^+ + ┼Ь^-)/2`: the commutator reduces to `(1/2) (┼Ь^+ тИТ ┼Ь^-) = (1/2) ┬╖ (2i) ┬╖ ┼Ь^{(2)} = i ┬╖ ┼Ь^{(2)}`. Together with ╬▓-20 and ╬▓-21 this completes the standard SU(2) commutator algebra (Tasaki eq. (2.1.1)) for spin-`S` operators (╬▓-22 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator31.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mul_self` / `spinSDiagProj_mul_of_ne` | **Idempotence and orthogonality** of the diagonal projectors `P_k = |kтЯйтЯиk|`: `P_k ┬╖ P_k = P_k` and `P_i ┬╖ P_j = 0` for `i тЙа j`. Combined with ╬▓-9 (`тИС_k P_k = 1`) this gives the spectral decomposition of the identity for `┼Ь^{(3)}` (╬▓-23 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjOrtho.lean` |
| `aeval_diagonal` | **Polynomial evaluation at a diagonal matrix**: `aeval (Matrix.diagonal v) p = Matrix.diagonal (fun i => p.eval (v i))`. Foundational lemma for the Lagrange-interpolation step (╬▓-25+) тАФ lets us pull a polynomial in `┼Ь^{(3)}` (a diagonal matrix) through to its scalar action on each diagonal entry. Proof by `Polynomial.induction_on'` on monomials and addition (╬▓-24 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AevalDiagonal.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_eq_lagrange_aeval` | **Lagrange-interpolation formula for `P_k`**: `P_k = aeval (┼Ь^{(3)}) (Lagrange.basis Finset.univ (spinSOp3Eigen N) k)`, equivalently `P_k = тИП_{j тЙа k} (┼Ь^{(3)} тИТ ╬╗_j тАв 1)/(╬╗_k тИТ ╬╗_j)` with `╬╗_j = (N : тДВ)/2 тИТ j.val`. Combines ╬▓-24 (`aeval` of diagonal) with mathlib's `Lagrange.eval_basis_self` / `eval_basis_of_ne`. Each diagonal projector is therefore a **polynomial in `┼Ь^{(3)}`**, which combined with ╬▓-9 (`тИС P_k = 1`) gives an explicit polynomial decomposition of `1╠В` (╬▓-25 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LagrangeFormula.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_eq_one_add_I_smul_two` / `spinSOpMinus_eq_one_sub_I_smul_two` | **Inversion of the Cartesian definition**: `┼Ь^+ = ┼Ь^{(1)} + i ┬╖ ┼Ь^{(2)}` and `┼Ь^- = ┼Ь^{(1)} тИТ i ┬╖ ┼Ь^{(2)}`. The defining identities `┼Ь^{(1)} = (1/2)(┼Ь^+ + ┼Ь^-)`, `┼Ь^{(2)} = (1/(2i))(┼Ь^+ тИТ ┼Ь^-)` invert to express the ladder operators as **linear combinations** of the Hermitian Cartesian spin operators (╬▓-26 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMAsOneTwo.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin_spinSOp3` / `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin` | **Diagonal projectors live in the algebra generated by the spin operators**: `P_k тИИ Algebra.adjoin тДВ {┼Ь^{(3)}}` (and a fortiori `P_k тИИ Algebra.adjoin тДВ {┼Ь^{(1)}, ┼Ь^{(2)}, ┼Ь^{(3)}}`). Promotes the Lagrange-interpolation formula (╬▓-25) from "polynomial-evaluation" form to "subalgebra-membership" form via `Algebra.adjoin_singleton_eq_range_aeval` (╬▓-27 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/ProjMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mem_adjoin` / `spinSOpMinus_mem_adjoin` | **Ladder operators live in `Algebra.adjoin тДВ {┼Ь^{(╬▒)}}`**: `┼Ь^+, ┼Ь^- тИИ Algebra.adjoin тДВ {┼Ь^{(1)}, ┼Ь^{(2)}, ┼Ь^{(3)}}`. Direct consequence of ╬▓-26 (`┼Ь^┬▒ = ┼Ь^{(1)} ┬▒ i ┬╖ ┼Ь^{(2)}`) and the fact that subalgebras are closed under `тДВ`-linear combinations (╬▓-28 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Immediate-neighbor matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin тДВ {┼Ь^{(╬▒)}}`**: `E_{i, i+1}, E_{i+1, i} тИИ Algebra.adjoin тДВ {┼Ь^{(1)}, ┼Ь^{(2)}, ┼Ь^{(3)}}`. Combines ╬▓-10 (`┼Ь^+ ┬╖ P_{i+1} = тИЪ((i+1)(N-i)) ┬╖ E_{i, i+1}`) with ╬▓-27 (`P_k тИИ adjoin`) and ╬▓-28 (`┼Ь^┬▒ тИИ adjoin`); the ladder coefficient is non-zero on the valid range and the subalgebra is closed under multiplication and scalar `smul`. Step toward expressing every matrix unit as a polynomial in `{┼Ь^{(╬▒)}}` (╬▓-29 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/NeighborUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_offset_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_offset_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Arbitrary stride-(k+1) matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin тДВ {┼Ь^{(╬▒)}}`**: `E_{i, i+(k+1)}, E_{i+(k+1), i} тИИ Algebra.adjoin тДВ {┼Ь^{(╬▒)}}` for any `k`. Induction on `k`: base case is ╬▓-29; inductive step chains via `Matrix.single_mul_single_same` (`E_{i,j} ┬╖ E_{j,k} = E_{i,k}`) (╬▓-30 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/OffsetUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_single_mem_adjoin` | **Every matrix unit `E_{i,j}` lives in `Algebra.adjoin тДВ {┼Ь^{(╬▒)}}`**. Three-case split: `i = j` reduces to ╬▓-27 via `Matrix.diagonal_single` (`E_{i,i} = P_i`); `i.val < j.val` is ╬▓-30 upper; `j.val < i.val` is ╬▓-30 lower. Last building block before the spanning theorem (╬▓-31 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllUnitsAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_mem_adjoin` / **`spinS_adjoin_eq_top`** | **ЁЯОп Tasaki ┬з2.1 Problem 2.1.a (P1d''') general-`S`**: every operator on the `(2S+1)`-dimensional spin-`S` Hilbert space `тДВ^{N+1}` is a polynomial in `{1╠В, ┼Ь^{(1)}, ┼Ь^{(2)}, ┼Ь^{(3)}}`. Equivalently, `Algebra.adjoin тДВ {┼Ь^{(1)}, ┼Ь^{(2)}, ┼Ь^{(3)}} = тКд`. Proof: every matrix unit `E_{i,j} тИИ adjoin` (╬▓-31), so by the entry-wise decomposition `M = тИС_{i,j} M_{i,j} тАв E_{i,j}` (`Matrix.matrix_eq_sum_single`) and the linearity of the subalgebra, every matrix is in the adjoin (╬▓-32 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/SpanningTheorem.lean` |
| `ManyBodyOpS` / `onSiteS` / `spinSSiteOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}` | **Multi-site spin-`S` operator space** indexed by configurations `╧Г : ╬Ы тЖТ Fin (N + 1)`, with the site-embedded operator `onSiteS i A` acting as `A` on site `i` and as the identity elsewhere; site-specialised `┼Ь_i^{(╬▒)}, ┼Ь_i^┬▒`. Hermiticity preservation `onSiteS_isHermitian` lifts from single-site to multi-site (Tasaki ┬з2.5 Phase B-╬▓ ╬▓-3a, Issue #412) | `Quantum/SpinS/MultiSite.lean` |

### Time-reversal map for `S = 1/2` (Tasaki ┬з2.3)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬з2.3 eqs. (2.3.4)тАУ(2.3.8), pp. 26тАУ27.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `timeReversalSpinHalf` | `╬Ш╠В(v) = (-conj(v 1), conj(v 0))` (Tasaki eq. (2.3.6), `S = 1/2`); the antilinear unitary `├╗_2 ┬╖ K╠В` of ┬з2.3 | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_spinHalfUp` / `_spinHalfDown` | `╬Ш╠В|╧И^тЖСтЯй = |╧И^тЖУтЯй` and `╬Ш╠В|╧И^тЖУтЯй = -|╧И^тЖСтЯй` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_add` | additivity `╬Ш╠В(v + w) = ╬Ш╠В(v) + ╬Ш╠В(w)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_smul` | **antilinearity** in the scalar: `╬Ш╠В(c тАв v) = (conj c) тАв ╬Ш╠В(v)` (Tasaki ┬з2.3 antilinearity warning, pp. 26тАУ27) | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_sq` | **Kramers degeneracy at `S = 1/2`** (Tasaki eq. (2.3.8), half-odd-integer): `╬Ш╠В┬▓ = -1╠В` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_mulVec` | spin sign flip (Tasaki eq. (2.3.14)): `╬Ш╠В(┼Ь^(╬▒) ┬╖ v) = (-┼Ь^(╬▒)) ┬╖ (╬Ш╠В v)` for `╬▒ = 1, 2, 3` тАФ equivariance form of `╬Ш╠В ┼Ь^(╬▒) ╬Ш╠ВтБ╗┬╣ = -┼Ь^(╬▒)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `complexConjugationSpinHalf` | the antilinear complex-conjugation map `K╠В` of Tasaki ┬з2.3 eq. (2.3.4) at `S = 1/2`: `K╠В(v) i := conj(v i)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `complexConjugationSpinHalf_sq` / `_add` / `_smul` | `K╠В` is involutive (`K╠В┬▓ = id`), additive, and antilinear in the scalar | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_eq_spinHalfRot2_pi_mulVec` | the **factorisation identity** of Tasaki ┬з2.3: `╬Ш╠В = ├╗_2 ┬╖ K╠В` where `├╗_2 = spinHalfRot2 ╧А` is the ╧А rotation about the `2`-axis | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `flipConfig` / `flipConfig_apply` / `flipConfig_involutive` | the spin-flip on a many-body configuration `╧Г : ╬Ы тЖТ Fin 2`: `flipConfig ╧Г x := 1 - ╧Г x`; involutive | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign` / `timeReversalSign_zero` / `_one` / `_mul_flip` | per-site sign factor `╬╡(0) = 1`, `╬╡(1) = -1` for the multi-spin time-reversal, with `╬╡(s) ┬╖ ╬╡(1 - s) = -1` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti` | multi-spin time-reversal map (Tasaki ┬з2.3 lattice extension, `S = 1/2`): `(╬Ш╠В_tot v) ╧Д := (тИП_x ╬╡(╧Д x)) ┬╖ conj(v (flip ╧Д))` for finite `╬Ы` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign_prod_conj` / `timeReversalSign_prod_mul_flip` | the product `тИП_x ╬╡(╧Д x)` is real (conjugation invariant); the cross product equals `(-1)^|╬Ы|` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_sq` | **Multi-spin Kramers degeneracy at `S = 1/2`** (Tasaki ┬з2.3 half-odd-integer extension): `╬Ш╠В_tot┬▓ = (-1)^|╬Ы| ┬╖ 1╠В` тАФ `+1╠В` when `|╬Ы|` is even, `-1╠В` when odd | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_basisVec` | action of `╬Ш╠В_tot` on a many-body basis state: `╬Ш╠В_tot |╬и_╧ГтЯй = (тИП_x ╬╡(flip ╧Г x)) ┬╖ |╬и_{flip ╧Г}тЯй` тАФ natural many-body generalisation of `╬Ш╠В|тЖСтЯй = |тЖУтЯй` and `╬Ш╠В|тЖУтЯй = -|тЖСтЯй` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliZ_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `╧Г^z` (Tasaki ┬з2.3 (2.3.14) lifted to many-body): `╬Ш╠В_tot ((onSite x ╧Г^z) v) = (-(onSite x ╧Г^z))(╬Ш╠В_tot v)`. Diagonal-action case; `╧Г^x`, `╧Г^y` deferred | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `siteFlipAt` / `siteFlipAt_self` / `siteFlipAt_of_ne` / `flipConfig_siteFlipAt_comm` / `siteFlipAt_involutive` | per-site flip helpers: `siteFlipAt ╧Д x` flips slot `x` only; commutes with `flipConfig`; involutive. The combinatorial primitive underlying off-diagonal `╧Г^x_x` / `╧Г^y_x` action (deferred) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `onSite_pauliX_mulVec_basisVec` | basis-state action of the off-diagonal site Pauli: `(onSite x ╧Г^x).mulVec |╬и_╧ГтЯй = |╬и_{siteFlipAt ╧Г x}тЯй` (the spin at site `x` is swapped) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `pauliX_eq_indicator` / `onSite_pauliX_mulVec_apply` | closed-form `pauliX a b = if b = 1 - a then 1 else 0`, lifted to `((onSite x ╧Г^x).mulVec v) ╧Д = v (siteFlipAt ╧Д x)` for any state `v` (general extension of the basis-state action) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign_prod_siteFlipAt` | `тИП_y ╬╡((siteFlipAt ╧Д x) y) = -(тИП_y ╬╡(╧Д y))` тАФ the per-site flip swaps `╬╡(╧Д x)` with `╬╡(1 - ╧Д x) = -╬╡(╧Д x)`, flipping the total sign | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliX_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `╧Г^x` (Tasaki ┬з2.3 (2.3.14) at ╬▒ = 1): `╬Ш╠В_tot ((onSite x ╧Г^x) v) = (-(onSite x ╧Г^x))(╬Ш╠В_tot v)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliY_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `╧Г^y` (Tasaki ┬з2.3 (2.3.14) at ╬▒ = 2): `╬Ш╠В_tot ((onSite x ╧Г^y) v) = (-(onSite x ╧Г^y))(╬Ш╠В_tot v)`. The proof handles the per-site `┬▒i` factor via `conj(pauliY_sign(1 - s)) = pauliY_sign(s)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_add` / `_smul` / `_real_smul` | multi-spin `╬Ш╠В_tot` is additive, antilinear in the scalar (`╬Ш╠В_tot(c тАв v) = conj(c) тАв ╬Ш╠В_tot v`), and real-linear (special case of antilinearity at real `r`) тАФ foundational for lifting Pauli-axis equivariance to bilinear / Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian forms | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_mulVec` | Tasaki ┬з2.3 (2.3.14) for spin-1/2 ops `┼Ь^(╬▒) = ╧Г^(╬▒) / 2`: `╬Ш╠В_tot ((onSite x ┼Ь^(╬▒)) v) = (-(onSite x ┼Ь^(╬▒)))(╬Ш╠В_tot v)` for ╬▒ = 1, 2, 3 тАФ direct corollaries of the Pauli versions by scalar (1/2) multiplication | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_spinHalfDot_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the bilinear `┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y`** (Tasaki ┬з2.3): `╬Ш╠В_tot ((┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y) v) = (┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y)(╬Ш╠В_tot v)` тАФ two equivariance `-1` factors cancel; sums per-axis | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki ┬з2.3): for real coupling `J` (`conj(J(x,y)) = J(x,y)`), `╬Ш╠В_tot ((H J) v) = (H J)(╬Ш╠В_tot v)`. Combines per-bond invariance + ╬Ш╠В_tot antilinearity (J reality) + additivity (over double-sum) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_conj` / `periodicChainCoupling_conj` | every entry of `openChainCoupling N J` (resp. `periodicChainCoupling N J`) is real (under complex conjugation), since `J : тДЭ` makes `(-(J : тДВ))` real-valued | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_openChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_periodicChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareTorusHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_cubicLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` | concrete time-reversal invariance: the open / periodic chain, the 2D open square / torus, and the 3D cubic Heisenberg Hamiltonians all commute with `╬Ш╠В_tot` for any real coupling `J : тДЭ`. Backed by `*Coupling_conj` reality lemmas in `HeisenbergChain.lean` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_basisVec_upDown` / `_basisVec_basisSwap_upDown` | `╬Ш╠В_tot |тЖСтЖУтЯй = -|тЖУтЖСтЯй` and `╬Ш╠В_tot |тЖУтЖСтЯй = -|тЖСтЖУтЯй` on `Fin 2` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_singlet` | the two-site spin singlet `|тЖСтЖУтЯй - |тЖУтЖСтЯй` is **time-reversal invariant** (Tasaki ┬з2.3 / ┬зA.3): being the SU(2) `S = 0` representation, it survives `╬Ш╠В_tot` unchanged | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_triplet_zero` | the triplet `m = 0` state `|тЖСтЖУтЯй + |тЖУтЖСтЯй` is **anti-invariant** under `╬Ш╠В_tot`: `╬Ш╠В_tot (|тЖСтЖУтЯй + |тЖУтЖСтЯй) = -(|тЖСтЖУтЯй + |тЖУтЖСтЯй)` (the symmetric combination picks up a minus sign from the per-basis-vector flip) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |

### Multi-body operator space (abstract lattice)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬з2.2, pp. 21-26 (tensor-product Hilbert space and site-local
operators). The lattice `╬Ы` is an arbitrary finite set with decidable
equality; specializing to `╬Ы = Fin N` recovers an `N`-site chain.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `ManyBodyOp ╬Ы` | `Matrix (╬Ы тЖТ Fin 2) (╬Ы тЖТ Fin 2) тДВ` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite i A` | site-embedded operator at `i : ╬Ы` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_isHermitian` | `A.IsHermitian тЖТ (onSite i A).IsHermitian` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_add`, `onSite_sub`, `onSite_zero`, `onSite_smul`, `onSite_one` | linearity of the site embedding and `onSite i 1 = 1` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne` | distinct-site commutation (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x тЙа y`, S = 1/2) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisVec` / `onSite_mulVec_basisVec` | tensor-product basis states and their action under site operators (Tasaki (2.2.1)/(2.2.4)) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_same` | same-site product `onSite i A ┬╖ onSite i B = onSite i (A ┬╖ B)` (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x = y`) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_same` | same-site commutator `[onSite i A, onSite i B] = onSite i [A, B]` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | commuting Hermitians multiply Hermitian (generic; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4342) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |

### Generic matrix-analysis helpers (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/`)

Topic-organized generic linear-algebra facts extracted from the physics files where they had been
re-proved as private helpers (Issue [#4339](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4339)).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.isHermitian_sum` | a finite sum of Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | the product of two commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_isHermitian` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_sq_of_sq_one` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting involutions is an involution | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mul_self_of_idempotent` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting idempotents is idempotent | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mulVec_eq_self` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of matrices each fixing `╧И` also fixes `╧И` | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | the trace of a Hermitian matrix is real | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `Tr(A┬╖B)` of two Hermitian matrices is conjugation-invariant | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |

(The two trace helpers above are also listed in the Gibbs-state section where they are consumed; the
canonical definitions live in `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean`.)

These are consumed by the JordanтАУWigner string / Hubbard hard-core projection layers (PR #4342),
replacing the per-file private copies in `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean`,
`Fermion/JordanWigner/Operators.lean`, and `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean`.

### Total spin operator (Tasaki ┬з2.2 eq. (2.2.7), (2.2.8))

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬з2.2 eqs. (2.2.7) and (2.2.8), p. 22.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `totalSpinHalfOp1/2/3 ╬Ы` | `┼Ь_tot^(╬▒) := ╬г_{x тИИ ╬Ы} onSite x ┼Ь^(╬▒)` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp1/2/3_isHermitian` | `┼Ь_tot^(╬▒)` is Hermitian | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `spinHalfOp_onSite_comm_of_ne` | S = 1/2 named wrapper of `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_onSite_commutator_spinHalfOp{2,3,1}_onSite` | same-site commutator `[┼Ь_x^(╬▒), ┼Ь_x^(╬▓)] = i ┬╖ ┼Ь_x^(╬│)` (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x = y`) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus`, `totalSpinHalfOpMinus` | `┼Ь^┬▒_tot := ╬г_{x тИИ ╬Ы} onSite x ┼Ь^┬▒` (Tasaki (2.2.8)) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus_eq_add`, `totalSpinHalfOpMinus_eq_sub` | `┼Ь^┬▒_tot = ┼Ь^(1)_tot ┬▒ i ┬╖ ┼Ь^(2)_tot` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus/Minus_conjTranspose` | `(┼Ь^┬▒_tot)тАа = ┼Ь^тИУ_tot` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{2,3,1}` | `[┼Ь_tot^(╬▒), ┼Ь_tot^(╬▓)] = i ┬╖ ┼Ь_tot^(╬│)` (total spin commutation) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_commutator_totalSpinHalfOpPlus/Minus` | `[┼Ь_tot^(3), ┼Ь^┬▒_tot] = ┬▒┼Ь^┬▒_tot` (Cartan ladder relations) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared` / `_isHermitian` | Casimir operator `(┼Ь_tot)┬▓ := ╬г_╬▒ (┼Ь_tot^(╬▒))┬▓` and its Hermiticity | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | `[(┼Ь_tot)┬▓, ┼Ь_tot^(╬▒)] = 0` (Casimir invariance, cf. Tasaki (2.2.12)) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_commutator_totalSpinHalfOpPlus/Minus` | `[(┼Ь_tot)┬▓, ┼Ь^┬▒_tot] = 0` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus_commutator_totalSpinHalfOpMinus` | `[┼Ь^+_tot, ┼Ь^-_tot] = 2 ┬╖ ┼Ь_tot^(3)` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `magnetization`, `spinSign` | total magnetization `|╧Г| := ╬г_x spinSign(╧Г_x)` (Tasaki (2.2.2)) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `spinHalfSign` | half-integer eigenvalue of `┼Ь^(3)` on `Fin 2` basis | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `onSite_spinHalfOp3_mulVec_basisVec` | `┼Ь_x^(3) ┬╖ |╧ГтЯй = ┬▒(1/2) ┬╖ |╧ГтЯй` (single-site eigenvalue) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_mulVec_basisVec` | `┼Ь_tot^(3) ┬╖ |╧ГтЯй = (╬г_x spinHalfSign(╧Г_x)) ┬╖ |╧ГтЯй`, partial (2.2.10) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_mulVec_basisVec_eq_magnetization` | `┼Ь_tot^(3) ┬╖ |╧ГтЯй = (|╧Г| / 2) ┬╖ |╧ГтЯй` (full Tasaki eq. (2.2.10)) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `onSite_spinHalfOpPlus/Minus_mulVec_basisVec` | raising/lowering action `┼Ь_x^┬▒ ┬╖ |╧ГтЯй` on a basis state at site `x` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus/Minus_mulVec_basisVec` | total `┼Ь^┬▒_tot ┬╖ |╧ГтЯй` as a sum of site-wise actions | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}Pi` | global ╧А-rotation `├Ы^(╬▒)_╧А_tot := тИП_x ├Ы^(╬▒)_╧А_x` (Tasaki eq. (2.2.11) at ╬╕ = ╧А) via `Finset.noncommProd` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3} ╬╕` | general-╬╕ global rotation `├Ы^(╬▒)_╬╕_tot := тИП_x ├Ы^(╬▒)_╬╕_x` (Tasaki eq. (2.2.11)) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_zero` | `├Ы^(╬▒)_0_tot = 1` (identity rotation) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}Pi_eq` | ╧А-rotation matches the general-╬╕ form at `╬╕ = ╧А` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}Pi_mul_totalSpinHalfRot{2,3,1}Pi` | `├Ы^(╬▒)_╧А_tot ┬╖ ├Ы^(╬▓)_╧А_tot = ├Ы^(╬│)_╧А_tot` (cyclic, Tasaki Problem 2.2.a) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `onSiteRingHom x` / `onSiteLinearMap x` / `continuous_onSite x` | `onSite x` packaged as a `RingHom`, тДВ-linear map, and continuous function | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `onSite_pow` | `(onSite x A)^k = onSite x (A^k)` (powers commute with `onSite`) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}Pi_two_site` | for `╬Ы = Fin 2`, the global ╧А-rotation factors as `onSite 0 (├Ы^(╬▒)_╧А) * onSite 1 (├Ы^(╬▒)_╧А)` (Tasaki Problem 2.2.b) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_all_up` | for any `k : тДХ`, `┼Ьtot^(3) ┬╖ (┼Ьtot^-)^k ┬╖ |тЖС..тЖСтЯй = (|╬Ы|/2 - k) ┬╖ (┼Ьtot^-)^k ┬╖ |тЖС..тЖСтЯй` тАФ the magnetic-quantum-number `M = Smax - k` labelling of the unnormalised iterates `(┼Ьtot^-)^k ┬╖ |╬жтЖСтЯй` (Tasaki's `|╬ж_MтЯй` of eq. (2.4.9), p. 33, up to normalisation). Proof via Nat induction using the Cartan ladder `[┼Ьtot^(3), ┼Ьtot^-] = -┼Ьtot^-` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `mulVec_preserves_eigenvalue_of_commute` | generic abstract pattern: for any `A B : ManyBodyOp ╬Ы` with `Commute A B`, if `A ┬╖ v = ╬╗ ┬╖ v` then `A ┬╖ (B ┬╖ v) = ╬╗ ┬╖ (B ┬╖ v)` тАФ the backbone of all commutator-based eigenvalue propagation | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_all_down` | dual ladder: for any `k : тДХ`, `┼Ьtot^(3) ┬╖ (┼Ьtot^+)^k ┬╖ |тЖУ..тЖУтЯй = (-|╬Ы|/2 + k) ┬╖ (┼Ьtot^+)^k ┬╖ |тЖУ..тЖУтЯй` тАФ same Tasaki ┬з2.4 (2.4.9) ladder parameterised from the lowest weight `M = -Smax`, raised by `┼Ьtot^+`. Proof: Nat induction using `[┼Ьtot^(3), ┼Ьtot^+] = +┼Ьtot^+` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_mulVec_basisVec_const` / `_all_up` / `_all_down` | constant-config ┼Ьtot^(3) eigenvalue: `┼Ьtot^(3) ┬╖ |s..sтЯй = (|╬Ы| ┬╖ spinHalfSign s) ┬╖ |s..sтЯй`; `s = 0` gives eigenvalue `|╬Ы|/2 = Smax`, `s = 1` gives `-|╬Ы|/2 = -Smax` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpMinus_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `┼Ьtot^- ┬╖ |тЖУ..тЖУтЯй = 0`: lowering annihilates the bottom of the ladder | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `┼Ьtot^+ ┬╖ |тЖС..тЖСтЯй = 0`: raising annihilates the top of the ladder | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp{Minus,Plus}_pow_basisVec_all_{up,down}_mem_magnetizationSubspace` | Submodule-form: `(┼Ьtot^-)^k ┬╖ |тЖС..тЖСтЯй тИИ H_{Smax - k}` and `(┼Ьtot^+)^k ┬╖ |тЖУ..тЖУтЯй тИИ H_{-Smax + k}` тАФ Tasaki ┬з2.4 eq. (2.4.9) ladder iterates explicitly placed in the magnetisation sectors of Tasaki eq. (2.2.10) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `basisVec_{upDown,basisSwap_upDown}_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | two-site antiparallel states `|тЖСтЖУтЯй`, `|тЖУтЖСтЯй` lie in `H_0` (Tasaki ┬з2.5 (2.5.2), p. 37, N├йel state for the spin-1/2 Fin 2 instance) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `singlet_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` / `triplet_zero_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | singlet `|тЖСтЖУтЯй - |тЖУтЖСтЯй` and triplet-`m=0` state `|тЖСтЖУтЯй + |тЖУтЖСтЯй` lie in `H_0` (Tasaki ┬зA.3 decomposition at the `M = 0` sector) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `neelChainConfig` / `neelChainState` | Tasaki ┬з2.5 eq. (2.5.2) N├йel state at `S = 1/2` on the parity-coloured chain `Fin (2 * K)`: `╧Г(i) = тЖС` if `i.val` even, `тЖУ` if odd | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelChainConfig_magnetization_zero` / `neelChainState_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | the N├йel chain configuration has total magnetisation `0`, so the corresponding basis state lies in the `┼Ь_tot^(3) = 0` eigenspace `H_0` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_neelChainState_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | for any coupling `J`, `H ┬╖ |╬ж_N├йelтЯй` again lies in `H_0` тАФ immediate corollary of SU(2) invariance applied to the N├йel state. The N├йel state is *not* an H-eigenstate (Tasaki ┬з2.5 (2.5.3)), but it cannot leak into other magnetisation sectors | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelChainState_adjacent` | Tasaki ┬з2.5 eq. (2.5.3) per-bond action: for every adjacent pair `(i, i+1)` of the chain `Fin (2 * K)`, `┼Ь_тЯиiтЯй ┬╖ ┼Ь_тЯиi+1тЯй ┬╖ |╬ж_N├йelтЯй = (1/2) ┬╖ |swap_{i,i+1} ╬ж_N├йelтЯй - (1/4) ┬╖ |╬ж_N├йelтЯй` (antiparallel case, parity-derived) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelChainState_wrap` | wrap-around bond `(2K + 1, 0)` action on the periodic chain `Fin (2 * (K + 1))`: same `(1/2) swap - (1/4) N├йel` decomposition as the open-bond case (parities `1` and `0` differ since the cycle length is even). Together with the adjacent lemma, covers every bond of the periodic chain | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_openChainCoupling_one_mulVec_neelChainState_one` | `K = 1` instance: `H_open(N=1, J) ┬╖ |╬ж_N├йelтЯй = -J ┬╖ |тЖУтЖСтЯй + (J/2) ┬╖ |╬ж_N├йelтЯй`. Lifts the per-bond `spinHalfDot` calculation through `H_open(N=1, J) = -2J ┬╖ spinHalfDot 0 1`. The non-eigenstate character of the N├йel state is plain | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelChainConfig_one_eq_upDown` / `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelChainState_one` | bridges the `K = 1` N├йel chain configuration to the existing `upDown` config and computes `╬Ш╠В_tot (neelChainState 1) = -basisVec (basisSwap upDown 0 1)` (the per-down sign convention of `╬Ш╠В` flips the antiparallel pair) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelSquareConfig` / `neelSquareState` | 2D checkerboard N├йel state on `Fin (2K) ├Ч Fin (2L)` (Tasaki ┬з2.5 (2.5.2) bipartite case): `╧Г(i, j) = тЖС` if `(i.val + j.val) % 2 = 0`, `тЖУ` otherwise | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelSquareConfig_magnetization_zero` / `neelSquareState_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | the 2D N├йel configuration has total magnetisation `0` and the corresponding state lies in the `┼Ь_tot^(3) = 0` eigenspace `H_0`. Proof: row-by-row column-sum vanishes (helper `sum_alternating_sign_offset` for the 1D parity sum with offset) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelSquareState_horizontal_adjacent` / `_vertical_adjacent` | Tasaki ┬з2.5 (2.5.3) per-bond action on the 2D N├йel state for the horizontal (`(i,j)~(i+1,j)`) and vertical (`(i,j)~(i,j+1)`) nearest-neighbour bonds: same `(1/2) ┬╖ |swapтЯй - (1/4) ┬╖ |╬ж_N├йelтЯй` decomposition as the 1D chain | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelSquareState_horizontal_wrap` / `_vertical_wrap` | wrap-around bond actions on the 2D torus N├йel state: horizontal `((2K+1, j), (0, j))` on `Fin (2(K+1)) ├Ч Fin (2L)` and vertical `((i, 2L+1), (i, 0))` on `Fin (2K) ├Ч Fin (2(L+1))` are antiparallel (parities differ by an odd shift); both inherit the same `(1/2)┬╖|swapтЯй - (1/4)┬╖|╬ж_N├йelтЯй` decomposition. With `_horizontal_adjacent` / `_vertical_adjacent`, covers every nearest-neighbour bond of the 2D torus N├йel state | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelCubicConfig` / `neelCubicState` / `neelCubicConfig_magnetization_zero` / `neelCubicState_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | 3D cubic checkerboard N├йel state on `(Fin (2K) ├Ч Fin (2L)) ├Ч Fin (2M)`: `╧Г((i,j),k) = тЖС` if `(i+j+k) % 2 = 0`, magnetisation = 0, lies in `H_0` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelCubicState_x_adjacent` / `_y_adjacent` / `_z_adjacent` | Tasaki ┬з2.5 (2.5.3) per-bond actions on the 3D cubic N├йel state for the three nearest-neighbour bond axes (x, y, z): same `(1/2)┬╖|swapтЯй - (1/4)┬╖|╬ж_N├йelтЯй` decomposition | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelCubicState_x_wrap` / `_y_wrap` / `_z_wrap` | wrap-around bond actions on the 3D cubic-torus N├йel state: each axis-wrap (`((2K+1, j), k) ~ ((0, j), k)`, `((i, 2L+1), k) ~ ((i, 0), k)`, `((i, j), 2M+1) ~ ((i, j), 0)`) is antiparallel (one coordinate shifts by an odd amount). With `_x_adjacent` / `_y_adjacent` / `_z_adjacent`, covers every nearest-neighbour bond of the 3D cubic torus N├йel state | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelSquareState_one_one` | concrete `K = L = 1` (2├Ч2 = 4-site) instance: `╬Ш╠В_tot (neelSquareState 1 1) = basisVec (flipConfig (neelSquareConfig 1 1))` (the equal up/down counts make `(-1)^|A| = 1`, so no overall sign) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelCubicState_one_one_one` | concrete `K = L = M = 1` (2├Ч2├Ч2 = 8-site) instance: `╬Ш╠В_tot (neelCubicState 1 1 1) = basisVec (flipConfig (neelCubicConfig 1 1 1))` (4 down spins after flipping тЖТ `(-1)^4 = 1`, so no overall sign) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelChainState` | general-`K` 1D chain: `╬Ш╠В_tot (neelChainState K) = (-1)^K ┬╖ basisVec (flipConfig (neelChainConfig K))` (helper `prod_alternating_neg_one` collapses the per-site sign product). | `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelSquareState` | general-`K, L` 2D checkerboard: `╬Ш╠В_tot (neelSquareState K L) = basisVec (flipConfig (neelSquareConfig K L))` (no sign because `(-1)^(2KL) = 1`). | `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelCubicState` | general-`K, L, M` 3D cubic checkerboard: `╬Ш╠В_tot (neelCubicState K L M) = basisVec (flipConfig (neelCubicConfig K L M))` (no sign because `(-1)^(4KLM) = 1`). | `sum_mul_basisVec` / `basisVec_sum_mul` | selector-sum identities `тИС ╧Д, f ╧Д ┬╖ basisVec ╧Г ╧Д = f ╧Г` (and the symmetric form), the workhorses for inner-product computations on the spin Hilbert space | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisVec_inner` | basis-vector orthonormality `тИС ╧Д, basisVec ╧Г ╧Д ┬╖ basisVec ╧Б ╧Д = if ╧Б = ╧Г then 1 else 0`. Real bilinear pairing (no complex conjugation needed since `basisVec` values are 0 or 1) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |

| `neelChainState_inner_basisVec_basisSwap_adjacent_eq_zero` | the N├йel chain state is orthogonal to the swapped basis vector at any adjacent (antiparallel) bond: `тИС ╧Д, ╬ж_N├йel(╧Д) ┬╖ basisVec(swap)(╧Д) = 0`. | `inner_basisVec_spinHalfDot_basisVec_antiparallel` | generic lemma: for any antiparallel `(x, y)` configuration `╧Г`, `тЯиbasisVec ╧Г, ┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y ┬╖ basisVec ╧ГтЯй = -1/4`. The 1-line foundation for every N├йel-bond expectation | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `inner_basisVec_spinHalfDot_basisVec_parallel` | parallel companion: for `╧Г x = ╧Г y` (and `x тЙа y`), `тЯиbasisVec ╧Г, ┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y ┬╖ basisVec ╧ГтЯй = +1/4`. Both basis vectors at the parallel pair are eigenvectors of `┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y` (eigenvalue `+1/4`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `neelChainState_inner_spinHalfDot_parallel_eq_one_quarter` | N├йel chain same-sublattice (parallel) bond expectation `+1/4`: for any `x тЙа y` with `x.val % 2 = y.val % 2` (e.g., `(0, 2)`, `(1, 3)`), `тЯи╬ж_N├йel, ┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y ┬╖ ╬ж_N├йelтЯй = +1/4` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `onSite_spinHalfOp3_mul_onSite_spinHalfOp3_mulVec_basisVec` | `(┼Ь^(3)_x ┬╖ ┼Ь^(3)_y) ┬╖ basisVec ╧Г = (spinHalfSign ╧Г x ┬╖ spinHalfSign ╧Г y) ┬╖ basisVec ╧Г`: every basis vector is an eigenvector of the diagonal `┼Ь^z┬╖┼Ь^z` correlator. | `neelChainState_energy_expectation_K1` | `K = 1` (2-site) open-chain Heisenberg energy expectation `тЯи╬ж_N├йel, H_open ┬╖ ╬ж_N├йelтЯй = J/2`. | `neelConfigOf` / `neelStateOf` | generic graph-centric N├йel state from a sublattice indicator `A : V тЖТ Bool`: `neelConfigOf A x := if A x then тЖС else тЖУ` and `neelStateOf A := basisVec (neelConfigOf A)`. The chain / 2D / 3D `neelXyzConfig` / `neelXyzState` definitions are bridged via `_eq_neelConfigOf` / `_eq_neelStateOf`. Tasaki ┬з2.5 eq. (2.5.2) graph-centric form | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
