| `inner_neelStateOf_spinHalfDot_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y` expectation on the canonical N├йel state: for any `x тЙа y` with `A x тЙа A y`, `тЯи╬ж_N├йel(A), ┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y ┬╖ ╬ж_N├йel(A)тЯй = -(1/4)`. Tasaki ┬з2.5 (2.5.4) ingredient (graph-centric form). The chain / 2D / 3D `_eq_neg_one_quarter` companions reduce to this via the `_eq_neelStateOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `inner_neelStateOf_szsz_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `┼Ь^z_x ┬╖ ┼Ь^z_y` correlation on the canonical N├йel state: for any `A x тЙа A y`, `тЯи╬ж_N├йel(A), ┼Ь^z_x ┬╖ ┼Ь^z_y ┬╖ ╬ж_N├йel(A)тЯй = -(1/4)`. Diagonal half of Tasaki ┬з2.5 (2.5.4) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf` | generic graph-centric Marshall sign `тИП_{x тИИ A} (-1)^(╧Г x)` for any finite vertex type `V`, sublattice indicator `A : V тЖТ Bool`, and configuration `╧Г : V тЖТ Fin 2`. Aligns with the project-wide graph-centric design (CLAUDE.local.md) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_const_zero` | for any sublattice indicator `A`, the all-up Marshall sign is `marshallSignOf A (const 0) = 1`. Generic counterpart of `marshallSignChainConfig_const_zero` etc.; those are now 1-line corollaries via the `_eq_marshallSignOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig` / `marshallSignChainConfig_neelChainConfig` | the Marshall sign `(-1)^(N_A^тЖУ)` for spin-1/2 configurations on the parity-coloured chain `Fin (2K)`, encoded as `тИП_{x even} (-1)^(╧Г x)`; specialisation to the N├йel configuration gives sign `+1` (no down spins on sublattice `A`). Foundational definition for the Marshall basis change underpinning the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki ┬з2.5). **Deprecated** as of 2026-04-22 in favour of the generic `marshallSignOf` (the chain / 2D / 3D Marshall sign defs are kept for backward compatibility but new code should prefer the generic form) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_eq_marshallSignOf` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | the chain / 2D / 3D parity-coloured Marshall signs are precisely `marshallSignOf` instantiated at the corresponding parity colouring | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig` / `marshallSignSquareConfig_neelSquareConfig` | 2D analogue: Marshall sign `тИП_{(i,j) with i+j even} (-1)^(╧Г (i,j))` on `Fin (2K) ├Ч Fin (2L)`; equals `+1` on the 2D checkerboard N├йel configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignCubicConfig` / `marshallSignCubicConfig_neelCubicConfig` | 3D analogue: Marshall sign `тИП_{((i,j),k) with i+j+k even} (-1)^(╧Г ((i,j),k))` on `(Fin (2K) ├Ч Fin (2L)) ├Ч Fin (2M)`; equals `+1` on the 3D cubic checkerboard N├йel configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down chain configurations: `marshallSignChainConfig K (const 0) = 1` and `marshallSignChainConfig K (const 1) = (-1)^K` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | 2D Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down checkerboard: both equal `+1` (the all-down case has `2KL` down spins on `A`, so `(-1)^(2KL) = 1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignCubicConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | 3D Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down cubic: both equal `+1` (the all-down case has `4KLM` down spins on `A`, so `(-1)^(4KLM) = 1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_flipConfig` | Marshall sign under the global spin-flip on the chain: `marshallSignChainConfig K (flipConfig ╧Г) = (-1)^K ┬╖ marshallSignChainConfig K ╧Г`. Each of the K even-indexed sites contributes `-1`. Proof: `Finset.prod_mul_distrib` + helper `prod_alternating_neg_one` collapses the contributing factor product to `(-1)^K`, with the per-site identity `(-1)^((1-s).val) = (-1)┬╖(-1)^(s.val)` closed by `fin_cases` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig_flipConfig` / `marshallSignCubicConfig_flipConfig` | 2D / 3D Marshall sign invariant under the global spin-flip (the contributing factor product `(-1)^(2KL)` resp. `(-1)^(4KLM)` equals `+1` for all K, L, M) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallChainState` / `_neelChainConfig` | Marshall-rotated chain basis state `marshallSignChainConfig K ╧Г тАв basisVec ╧Г`; specialisation at the N├йel configuration coincides with `neelChainState K` (since the Marshall sign of the N├йel state is `+1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSquareState` / `_neelSquareConfig` | 2D Marshall-rotated checkerboard state; coincides with `neelSquareState K L` at the N├йel configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallCubicState` / `_neelCubicConfig` | 3D cubic Marshall-rotated checkerboard state; coincides with `neelCubicState K L M` at the N├йel configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_flipConfig_neelChainConfig` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | Marshall sign on the *flipped* N├йel configuration: `(-1)^K` (1D), `+1` (2D), `+1` (3D) тАФ direct compositions of `_flipConfig` and `_neelChainConfig` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallChainState_flipConfig_eq_timeReversalSpinHalfMulti` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | the Marshall-rotated *flipped* N├йel state coincides with the time-reversed N├йel state in 1D, 2D, 3D тАФ both sides equal the same explicit `(-1)^K` (1D) or `+1` (2D, 3D) scaled basis vector. Establishes a direct bridge between the Marshall basis change (Tasaki ┬з2.5 / Marshall-Lieb-Mattis) and the time-reversal operator (Tasaki ┬з2.3) on the N├йel ground-state ansatz | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis A ╧Г` | **Marshall-dressed standard basis state** `:= marshallSignOf A ╧Г тАв basisVec ╧Г` on a generic finite vertex type `V` with sublattice indicator `A : V тЖТ Bool` (Tasaki ┬з2.5 eq. (2.5.8), p. 41). The dressing produces a basis in which the spin-1/2 antiferromagnetic Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a connected bipartite graph has all off-diagonal matrix elements `тЙд 0` (Marshall sign trick), the input to the PerronтАУFrobenius proof of the MLM theorem | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_self` / `_of_ne` / `_apply` | pointwise rules: `╬и╠Г^╧Г ╧Г = marshallSignOf A ╧Г`; `╬и╠Г^╧Г ╧Д = 0` for `╧Д тЙа ╧Г`; explicit `╬и╠Г^╧Г ╧Д = marshallSignOf A ╧Г ┬╖ basisVec ╧Г ╧Д` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_sq_eq_one` | each factor of `marshallSignOf` is `┬▒1`, so the sign squares to `1`: `(marshallSignOf A ╧Г)┬▓ = 1` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_inner` | orthonormality of the Marshall-dressed basis under the real bilinear pairing: `╬г_╧Д ╬и╠Г^╧Г ╧Д ┬╖ ╬и╠Г^╧Б ╧Д = if ╧Б = ╧Г then 1 else 0` (combines `basisVec_inner` with `marshallSignOf_sq_eq_one`) | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_mem_magnetizationSubspace` / `_zero` | the dressed basis state lies in the same magnetisation-`M` subspace `H_M = H_{╧Г╠Д/2}` as the underlying `basisVec ╧Г` (Tasaki eq. (2.2.10)); the `_zero` specialisation places it in `H_0` when `╬г_x ╧Г_x = 0` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_im_eq_zero` | every matrix entry of the two-site spin product `┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y` is **real**: `((spinHalfDot x y) ╧Г ╧Г').im = 0` for all `x, y, ╧Г, ╧Г'`. Case analysis on `x = y` / parallel / antiparallel via the existing `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_{parallel,antiparallel}` action lemmas. **Property (i) ingredient** for the MarshallтАУLiebтАУMattis theorem (Tasaki ┬з2.5, p. 41) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_im_eq_zero` | for **real** coupling `J : ╬Ы тЖТ ╬Ы тЖТ тДВ` (`(J x y).im = 0` for all `x, y`), every matrix entry of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `H = ╬г_{x,y} J(x,y) ┬╖ spinHalfDot x y` is real: `((heisenbergHamiltonian J) ╧Г ╧Г').im = 0`. тДЭ-linearity + `spinHalfDot_apply_im_eq_zero` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_im_eq_zero` | the Marshall sign `marshallSignOf A ╧Г` is **real**: `(marshallSignOf A ╧Г).im = 0`. Each factor of the product is `┬▒1 тИИ тДЭ` (either `1` or `(-1 : тДВ)^(╧Г x : тДХ)` with `(╧Г x : тДХ) тИИ {0, 1}`); products of reals are real | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_heisenbergHamiltonian_marshallDressed_im_eq_zero` | **MLM Property (i)**: for real coupling `J`, the dressed Heisenberg bilinear pairing `╬г_╧Д \|╬и╠Г^╧ГтЯй ╧Д ┬╖ (H ┬╖ \|╬и╠Г^{╧Г'}тЯй) ╧Д` is real (Tasaki ┬з2.5, p. 41 in the proof of Theorem 2.2). Reduces to `marshallSignOf A ╧Г ┬╖ marshallSignOf A ╧Г' ┬╖ H ╧Г ╧Г'` (each factor real) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_mulVec_marshallDressed_eq` | for any operator `M`, the dressed bilinear pairing factorises: `╬г_╧Д \|╬и╠Г^╧ГтЯй ╧Д ┬╖ (M ┬╖ \|╬и╠Г^{╧Г'}тЯй) ╧Д = marshallSignOf A ╧Г ┬╖ marshallSignOf A ╧Г' ┬╖ M ╧Г ╧Г'`. Generalises the inner-product computation used in Property (i) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_mul_marshallSignOf_basisSwap_of_bipartite_antiparallel` | **Marshall sign relation**: for a bond `{x, y}` crossing the bipartition (`A x тЙа A y`) with `╧Г` antiparallel at `{x, y}` (`╧Г x тЙа ╧Г y`), `marshallSignOf A ╧Г * marshallSignOf A (basisSwap ╧Г x y) = -1`. The combined product over `╬Ы` of pairwise factors collapses: outside `{x, y}` each pairwise factor is `(┬▒1)┬▓ = 1`; at the unique site in `A тИй {x, y}` the pair contributes `(-1)^(╧Г x + ╧Г y) = -1` since `╧Г x тЙа ╧Г y`; the other site of `{x, y}` lies outside `A` and contributes `1` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `bond_dressed_contribution_re_nonpos` | per-bond non-positivity: for `╧Г тЙа ╧Г'` and any bond `(x, y)` with real non-negative `J(x, y)` supported on bipartite bonds, the contribution `marshallSignOf A ╧Г ┬╖ marshallSignOf A ╧Г' ┬╖ J(x,y) ┬╖ (spinHalfDot x y) ╧Г ╧Г'` to the dressed off-diagonal element has non-positive real part. Case analysis on `(spinHalfDot x y) ╧Г ╧Г'` (zero off-diagonal except at `╧Г = basisSwap ╧Г' x y`, antiparallel ╧Г', `x тЙа y`) combined with the Marshall sign relation | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_heisenbergHamiltonian_marshallDressed_re_nonpos_of_ne` | **MLM Property (ii)** (Tasaki ┬з2.5, p. 41): for real non-negative `J` supported on bipartite bonds and `╧Г тЙа ╧Г'`, the dressed off-diagonal Heisenberg pairing `╬г_╧Д \|╬и╠Г^╧ГтЯй ╧Д ┬╖ (H ┬╖ \|╬и╠Г^{╧Г'}тЯй) ╧Д` has non-positive real part. Sum bond-by-bond using `bond_dressed_contribution_re_nonpos`. The **Marshall sign trick** at the heart of the MarshallтАУLiebтАУMattis Theorem 2.2 proof | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `SwapStep`, `SwapReachable` | one-step swap relation `╧Г тЖж basisSwap ╧Г x y` along a graph edge `(x, y)` with `╧Г x тЙа ╧Г y`; reflexive transitive closure for multi-step reachability | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_walk_of_ne` | for any `G`-walk from `x` to `y` and `╧Г x тЙа ╧Г y`, `SwapReachable G ╧Г (basisSwap ╧Г x y)`. Walk induction with case analysis on `╧Г z` at intermediate vertex (Tasaki p. 41 "Proof of Property (iii)" Lemma) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_reachable_of_ne` / `_preconnected_` | for any `x, y` reachable in `G` (or any `x, y` if `G` preconnected) with `╧Г x тЙа ╧Г y`, the swap is reachable. **MLM Property (iii) ingredient** (Tasaki ┬з2.5 p. 41) тАФ combined with iteration over the magnetisation-difference, gives PerronтАУFrobenius irreducibility on `H_M` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `HтВАIndex ╬Ы` | index type `{╧Г : ╬Ы тЖТ Fin 2 // magnetization ╬Ы ╧Г = 0}` for the zero-magnetisation subspace `H_0`; `Fintype` and `DecidableEq` instances | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0` | real-valued matrix on `HтВАIndex ╬Ы` with entries `Re (marshallSignOf A ╧Г ┬╖ marshallSignOf A ╧Д ┬╖ (H_J)_{╧Г,╧Д})` тАФ the matrix to which Tasaki's PerronтАУFrobenius proof of MLM applies | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_isSymm` | the matrix is **symmetric** for real symmetric `J` (Hermiticity of Heisenberg + realness of entries) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_offdiag_nonpos` | **off-diagonal entries are non-positive** for real non-negative bipartite `J` and distinct `╧Г тЙа ╧Д`, packaged from PR ╬▒-3's Property (ii) via `dot_marshallDressed_mulVec_marshallDressed_eq` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `magnetization_basisSwap` | `basisSwap ╧Г x y` preserves total magnetisation. Proof uses the identification `basisSwap ╧Г x y = ╧Г тИШ Equiv.swap x y` (the swap is a permutation of `╬Ы`); the magnetisation `тИС_z spinSign(╧Г z)` is invariant under such reindexing (`Equiv.sum_comp`). Key ingredient for Tasaki ┬з2.5 p. 42 Proposition (equal-magnetisation reachability) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetization.lean` |
| `disagreementSet` / `configDist` | the set / count of sites where `╧Г` and `╧Г'` disagree; `configDist_eq_zero_iff` characterises configuration equality | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `exists_swap_pair_of_eq_magnetization` | for `╧Г тЙа ╧Г'` with equal magnetisation, there exist sites `x` (with `╧Г x = 0, ╧Г' x = 1`) and `y` (with `╧Г y = 1, ╧Г' y = 0`). Pigeonhole/cardinality argument: the `(0, 1)`-disagreement and `(1, 0)`-disagreement sets have equal cardinality from magnetisation equality, and the disagreement set is non-empty for `╧Г тЙа ╧Г'` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `configDist_basisSwap_lt` | swapping at sites `x тИИ D01`, `y тИИ D10` strictly decreases the configuration distance to `╧Г'`. The disagreement set strictly shrinks (`x` newly agrees with `╧Г'` after swap) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_eq_magnetization` | **Tasaki ┬з2.5 p. 42 Proposition**: any two configurations `╧Г`, `╧Г'` with the same total magnetisation are connected by a chain of single-edge bond swaps, on a connected graph. Strong induction on `configDist`, reducing by `тЙе 2` per step via the swap pair from `exists_swap_pair_of_eq_magnetization`. **Final ingredient** for PerronтАУFrobenius irreducibility on `H_M` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted` | the shifted matrix `B := c┬╖I тИТ M` on `HтВАIndex ╬Ы`. Used as input to PerronтАУFrobenius: `B` is symmetric, has non-negative off-diagonal (sign flip of `M`'s non-positive off-diagonal), and non-negative diagonal when `c тЙе M ╧Г ╧Г` for all `╧Г`. The maximum eigenvalue of `B` corresponds to the minimum eigenvalue of `M` (the H_0 ground state of the AFM Heisenberg) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Shifted.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` / `_nonneg` (`_offdiag_nonneg`, `_diag_nonneg`) | symmetry and (off-diagonal / full) non-negativity of `B` under the appropriate hypotheses on `J` and `c` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Shifted.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_basisSwap` | the off-diagonal matrix entry `(spinHalfDot x y) ╧Г (basisSwap ╧Г x y) = 1/2` for `x тЙа y` and antiparallel `╧Г_x тЙа ╧Г_y`. Building block for the explicit Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related configurations needed for PerronтАУFrobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SpinDotSwapEntry.lean` |
| `basisSwap_basisSwap_ne_self_of_ne_bond` | combinatorial helper: for `x тЙа y`, `╧Г_x тЙа ╧Г_y`, and `(u, v) тИЙ {(x, y), (y, x)}`, the configuration `basisSwap (basisSwap ╧Г x y) u v тЙа ╧Г`. Site analysis: `╧Г` and `╧Г' = basisSwap ╧Г x y` differ at exactly `{x, y}`, so for the iterated swap to return to `╧Г`, the swap sites `{u, v}` must coincide with `{x, y}`. Used for off-bond vanishing in the Heisenberg matrix entry computation | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/HeisenbergSwapEntry.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_basisSwap_off_bond_eq_zero` | for `╧Г' = basisSwap ╧Г x y` (with `x тЙа y`, `╧Г_x тЙа ╧Г_y`) and any `(u, v) тИЙ {(x, y), (y, x)}`, the matrix entry `(spinHalfDot u v) ╧Г ╧Г' = 0`. Three cases: `u = v` (diagonal), `u тЙа v` parallel ╧Г' (constant action), `u тЙа v` antiparallel + off-bond (combinatorial helper) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SpinDotOffBond.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_basisSwap` | the Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related configurations: `(heisenbergHamiltonian J) ╧Г (basisSwap ╧Г x y) = (J x y + J y x) / 2`. Decomposes the double sum and uses ╬▒-5e (active bond = 1/2) + ╬▒-5g (off-bond = 0). For symmetric `J`, simplifies to `J x y` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/HeisenbergSwapValue.lean` |
| `dressed_pairing_basisSwap_eq` / `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_apply_basisSwap` | the dressed Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related H_0 configurations: complex-level value `-J(x, y)` (Marshall sign trick ├Ч Heisenberg formula ├Ч symmetric `J`), real-part value `-(J x y).re`. Combined with `J(x, y).re > 0` on graph edges gives strict negativity of M off-diagonal at swap pairs, hence strict positivity of `B = c┬╖I тИТ M` тАФ the input for PerronтАУFrobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/DressedSwapValue.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pos_of_basisSwap` | strict positivity `0 < B ╧Г ╧Д` on swap-related H_0 configurations with positive symmetric bipartite `J`. Combines the dressed matrix value `-J(x, y).re` (PR ╬▒-5i) with the off-diagonal definition `B ╧Г ╧Д = -M ╧Г ╧Д` (PR ╬▒-5d). Single-step strict positivity for PerronтАУFrobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedSwap.lean` |
| `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_path` | generic matrix-power positivity from a positive path: for non-negative matrix `B` and a path `p_0, ..., p_{k+1}` with `B(p_i, p_{i+1}) > 0` on every consecutive pair, `(B^(k+1))(p_0)(p_{k+1}) > 0`. Induction on `k` using `pow_succ` + `Finset.sum_pos'`. Used to lift single-step swap positivity (╬▒-5j) to multi-step matrix-power positivity for PF irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowPath.lean` |
| `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_pow_pos_step` | one-step extension: if `(B^m) ╧Г ╧Д > 0` and `B ╧Д ╧Б > 0` for non-negative `B`, then `(B^(m+1)) ╧Г ╧Б > 0`. Inductive building block for ReflTransGen-style matrix-power lifting | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowExtend.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pow_pos_of_swapReachable` | for `╧Г : HтВАIndex ╬Ы` and any `╬╛` with `Relation.ReflTransGen (SwapStep G) ╧Г.val ╬╛`, there exists `m` with `(B^m) ╧Г тЯи╬╛, h_magтЯй > 0`. Induction on `ReflTransGen`: refl gives `m = 0`, tail extends by one swap using ╬▒-5j (single-step swap positivity) and ╬▒-5l (one-step matrix-power extension). Key bridge from combinatorial reachability to PF irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedReachable.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isIrreducible` | **`B = c ┬╖ I тИТ M` is irreducible** on `H_0` for connected bipartite `G` with positive symmetric real coupling supported on `G`-edges and shift constant `c > M ╧Г ╧Г` (strict). Cases on `╧Г = ╧Д` (use diagonal positivity) vs `╧Г тЙа ╧Д` (use ╬▒-5c reachability + ╬▒-5m matrix-power lift). Final input for PerronтАУFrobenius application | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedIrreducible.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isHermitian` | the shifted matrix is Hermitian (= symmetric for real matrices). Wraps `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` (PR ╬▒-5d) into the IsHermitian form needed by PerronтАУFrobenius | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0PFApplication.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_exists_pos_eigenvec_max` / `_pos_eigenvec_unique` | **PerronтАУFrobenius applied to `B = c ┬╖ I тИТ M` on `H_0`**: existence of a strictly positive eigenvector `v` for some real eigenvalue `╬╝`, and uniqueness up to positive scalar. Translating back to `M`, `v` is the eigenvector for the **minimum** eigenvalue (the H_0 ground state of the AFM Heisenberg). This is the matrix-level Tasaki (2.5.4): the H_0 ground-state expansion `╬г_╧Г c_╧Г \|╬и╠Г^╧ГтЯй` with `c_╧Г = v ╧Г > 0` is unique up to positive scalar | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0PFApplication.lean` |
| `bipartiteCoupling` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian` | the Tasaki ┬з2.5 p. 40 toy Hamiltonian setup: `bipartiteCoupling A x y := if A x тЙа A y then 1 else 0` (the unnormalised bipartite coupling), and `heisenbergToyHamiltonian A := heisenbergHamiltonian (bipartiteCoupling A)`. Real symmetric, non-negative, supported on bipartite bonds, positive on inter-sublattice pairs. Hermitian. Used in subsequent PRs to derive `S_tot = 0` for the AFM Heisenberg ground state via inner-product comparison | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonian.lean` |
| `bipartiteGraphFromA` | the complete bipartite graph on `╬Ы` from sublattice indicator `A : ╬Ы тЖТ Bool`: vertices `x, y` are adjacent iff `A x тЙа A y`. The natural bond graph for the toy Hamiltonian (every edge of `bipartiteCoupling A` is a `bipartiteGraphFromA A`-edge and vice versa) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/BipartiteGraph.lean` |
| `bipartiteGraphFromA_preconnected` | `bipartiteGraphFromA A` is preconnected when both sublattices are non-empty. Cases on `A x = A y` (length-2 path via opposite sublattice) vs `A x тЙа A y` (direct edge). Provides the `G.Preconnected` hypothesis needed for MLM application to the toy Hamiltonian | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/BipartiteGraph.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_toy_exists_pos_eigenvec_max` / `_pos_eigenvec_unique` | **Matrix-level Tasaki (2.5.4) for the toy Hamiltonian**: the shifted toy matrix `B_toy = c ┬╖ I тИТ M_toy` (under both-sublattices-nonempty + diagonal-shift hypothesis) has a unique-up-to-positive-scalar strictly positive eigenvector. Specialises ╬▒-5o to the toy via ╬▒-6a + ╬▒-6b | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyPF.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOp1/2/3` | sublattice spin operators `┼Ь_A^(╬▒) := ╬г_{x тИИ A} onSite x ┼Ь^(╬▒)` for `╬▒ тИИ {1, 2, 3}`. Foundation for the Casimir identity `─д_toy = (1/(2|╬Ы|))((┼Ь_tot)┬▓ тИТ (┼Ь_A)┬▓ тИТ (┼Ь_B)┬▓)` (Tasaki ┬з2.5 (2.5.11)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp1/2/3` / `totalSpinSOp{1,2,3}_eq_sublattice_sum` | **Spin-`S` analogues**: `┼Ь_A^(╬▒) := ╬г_{x : A x} onSiteS x (spinSOp_╬▒ N)` for `╬▒ тИИ {1, 2, 3}`, with decomposition `┼Ь_tot^(╬▒) = ┼Ь_A^(╬▒) + ┼Ь_┬мA^(╬▒)`. | `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_isHermitian` | Spin-`S` sublattice operator Hermiticity (╬│-4 step 2). Sum of Hermitian summands is Hermitian | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1043) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_isHermitian` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir `(┼Ь_A)┬▓ := ╬г_╬▒ (┼Ь_A^(╬▒))┬▓` plus Hermiticity. Foundation for the Casimir identity in Tasaki ┬з2.5 (2.5.11) at general spin-S (╬│-4 step 3) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1044) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute` | spin-`S` cross-sublattice same-axis commutativity `Commute (┼Ь_A^(╬▒)) (┼Ь_┬мA^(╬▒))` for ╬▒ тИИ {1, 2, 3}. Sites in A vs ┬мA are distinct, so per-site operators commute via `onSiteS_commute_of_ne` (╬│-4 step 4) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1045) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpGeneric_cross_commute` / `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute_op{1,2,3}` | spin-`S` mixed-axes cross-sublattice commutativity: `Commute (┼Ь_A^(╬▒)) (┼Ь_┬мA^(╬▓))` for any ╬▒, ╬▓ тИИ {1, 2, 3}. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_cross_commute` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir cross-commute: `Commute (┼Ь_A)┬▓ (┼Ь_┬мA)┬▓`. | `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_commutator_sublatticeSpinSOp{2,3,1}` | spin-`S` sublattice SU(2) algebra: `[┼Ь_A^╬▒, ┼Ь_A^╬▓] = i ╬╡^╬▒╬▓╬│ ┼Ь_A^╬│`. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commutator_sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir self-invariance: `[(┼Ь_A)┬▓, ┼Ь_A^(╬▒)] = 0`, equivalently `Commute (┼Ь_A)┬▓ ┼Ь_A^(╬▒)`. Uses the SU(2) algebra (PR #1048) plus the Leibniz identity (╬│-4 step 8) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1049) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_complement` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir vs complement axis-╬▒: `Commute (┼Ь_A)┬▓ ┼Ь_┬мA^(╬▒)` for ╬▒ тИИ {1, 2, 3}. Each axis-╬▓ square `(┼Ь_A^(╬▓))┬▓` commutes with `┼Ь_┬мA^(╬▒)` by `Commute.mul_left` applied to the mixed-axes cross-commute (╬│-4 step 9) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1050) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_totalSpinSOp{1,2,3}` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir vs total spin axis-╬▒: `Commute (┼Ь_A)┬▓ ┼Ь_tot^(╬▒)` for ╬▒ тИИ {1, 2, 3}. Combines self-invariance (PR #1049) with the complement-axis result (PR #1050) via `Commute.add_right` after rewriting `┼Ь_tot^(╬▒) = ┼Ь_A^(╬▒) + ┼Ь_┬мA^(╬▒)` (╬│-4 step 10) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1051) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_totalSpinSSquared` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir vs total Casimir: `Commute (┼Ь_A)┬▓ (┼Ь_tot)┬▓`. Third pairwise commutativity needed for the joint eigenbasis of `(┼Ь_tot)┬▓`, `(┼Ь_A)┬▓`, `(┼Ь_┬мA)┬▓` (Tasaki ┬з2.5 toy-Hamiltonian eigenvalue analysis). Combines the three axis results from PR #1051 via `Commute.mul_right` and `Commute.add_right` (╬│-4 step 11) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1052) |

(2.5.10) without `1/\|╬Ы\|`): `─д_toy_S A := heisenbergHamiltonianS (bipartiteCoupling A) N`. Reuses spin-independent `bipartiteCoupling` from `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonian.lean`. Hermitian via `heisenbergHamiltonianS_isHermitian_of_real` (╬│-4 step 12) | `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1053) |
| `sublatticeSpinSDot` / `sublatticeSpinSDot_def` / `sublatticeSpinSDot_eq_sum_sum` / `sublatticeSpinSDot_complement_isHermitian` | spin-`S` cross-sublattice spin dot product `┼Ь_A ┬╖ ┼Ь_B := ╬г_╬▒ ┼Ь_A^(╬▒) ┼Ь_B^(╬▒)`, definitional unfolding, bilinear expansion `┼Ь_A ┬╖ ┼Ь_B = ╬г_{x : A x} ╬г_{y : B y} ┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y`, and Hermiticity for `B = ┬мA` (each axis-╬▒ summand is a product of two commuting Hermitian operators) (╬│-4 step 13) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` (PR #1054) |
| `sublatticeSpinSDot_complement_comm` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_eq_sublatticeSpinSDot_sum` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_eq_two_sublatticeSpinSDot` | spin-`S` toy Hamiltonian decomposes as oriented cross-sublattice spin dot products: `─д_toy_S = ┼Ь_A ┬╖ ┼Ь_┬мA + ┼Ь_┬мA ┬╖ ┼Ь_A`, and via cross-sublattice symmetry the closed form `─д_toy_S = 2 тАв ┼Ь_A ┬╖ ┼Ь_┬мA`. Bridges the bipartite-bond sum (Tasaki ┬з2.5 (2.5.10)) to the operator-level Casimir form (╬│-4 step 14) | `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` (PR #1055) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_eq_sublattice_casimir` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_eq_casimir_diff` | **spin-`S` Casimir identity** (Tasaki ┬з2.5 (2.5.11)): `(┼Ь_tot)┬▓ = (┼Ь_A)┬▓ + 2 тАв (┼Ь_A ┬╖ ┼Ь_┬мA) + (┼Ь_┬мA)┬▓` (per-axis `(a + b)┬▓ = a┬▓ + 2ab + b┬▓` via cross-commute), and the closed form (without `1/\|╬Ы\|`) `─д_toy_S = (┼Ь_tot)┬▓ тИТ (┼Ь_A)┬▓ тИТ (┼Ь_┬мA)┬▓` (╬│-4 step 15) | `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` (PR #1056) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_commute_totalSpinSSquared` / `_commute_sublatticeSpinSquaredS` / `_complement` | spin-`S` toy Hamiltonian commutes with the three Casimirs `(┼Ь_tot)┬▓`, `(┼Ь_A)┬▓`, `(┼Ь_┬мA)┬▓`. The first via SU(2) invariance of any spin-`S` Heisenberg Hamiltonian; the other two via the closed form (PR #1056) and the three pairwise Casimir commutativities (PRs #1047, #1052). All four Casimir-style commutators of `─д_toy_S`, prerequisite for the joint eigenbasis analysis (╬│-4 step 16) | `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` (PR #1057) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_of_jointCasimirEigenvector` / `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_via_joint` | **spin-`S` toy Hamiltonian eigenvalue on simultaneous Casimir eigenvectors**: for `v` a joint eigenvector of `(┼Ь_tot)┬▓`, `(┼Ь_A)┬▓`, `(┼Ь_┬мA)┬▓` at eigenvalues `╬▒, ╬▓_A, ╬▓_B`, `─д_toy_S ┬╖ v = (╬▒ тИТ ╬▓_A тИТ ╬▓_B) тАв v` and `тЯиv, ─д_toy_S vтЯй = (╬▒ тИТ ╬▓_A тИТ ╬▓_B) ┬╖ тЯиv, vтЯй`. Direct application of PR #1056's closed form `─д_toy_S = (┼Ь_tot)┬▓ тИТ (┼Ь_A)┬▓ тИТ (┼Ь_┬мA)┬▓` and linearity of `mulVec`. Sets up the eigenvalue analysis underpinning Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3 (╬│-4): minimising `(╬▒ тИТ ╬▓_A тИТ ╬▓_B)` over admissible joint eigenvalues gives the predicted `S_tot = ||A| тИТ |┬мA||┬╖S` ground state (PR #2774, file `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianJointEigenvalue.lean`) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_jointCasimirEigenspace_le_eigenspace` | **subspace-level form of PR #2774**: the meet of the three Casimir eigenspaces at `(╬▒, ╬▓_A, ╬▓_B)` is contained in the `─д_toy_S`-eigenspace at `(╬▒ тИТ ╬▓_A тИТ ╬▓_B)`, i.e. `eigenspace(┼Ь_tot┬▓)_╬▒ тКУ eigenspace(┼Ь_A┬▓)_╬▓_A тКУ eigenspace(┼Ь_┬мA┬▓)_╬▓_B тЙд eigenspace(─д_toy_S)_{╬▒тИТ╬▓_AтИТ╬▓_B}`. | `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_eq_total_minus_sublattice_casimirs` | **N├йel toy-Hamiltonian expectation via Casimir decomposition**: `тЯи╬ж_N├йel \| ─д_toy_S \| ╬ж_N├йelтЯй = тЯи╬ж_N├йel \| (┼Ь_tot)┬▓ \| ╬ж_N├йelтЯй тИТ s_A(s_A+1) ┬╖ тЯи╬ж_N├йel,╬ж_N├йelтЯй тИТ s_B(s_B+1) ┬╖ тЯи╬ж_N├йel,╬ж_N├йelтЯй`, where `s_A = \|A\|┬╖N/2, s_B = \|┬мA\|┬╖N/2`. Bridges PR #2774's joint-Casimir framework to the existing direct expectation `тИТ\|A\|┬╖\|┬мA\|┬╖N┬▓/2` (PR #1070); useful for subsequent ╬│-4 variational comparisons against the predicted toy-Hamiltonian ground state (PR #2776, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelToyHamiltonianViaCasimir.lean`) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_apply_diag_neel_complement` / `neelStateOfS_complement_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation` | **complement-orientation N├йel toy-Hamiltonian expectation**: `тЯи╬ж_N├йel(┬мA) \| ─д_toy_S(A) \| ╬ж_N├йel(┬мA)тЯй = тИТ\|A\|┬╖\|┬мA\|┬╖N┬▓/2`. Same value as the original-orientation expectation (PR #1093): the toy Hamiltonian is sublattice-swap invariant (via `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_eq_two_sublatticeSpinSDot` + `sublatticeSpinSDot_complement_comm`), and the complement N├йel basis configuration produces the same per-bond `m_x ┬╖ m_y = -N┬▓/4` contribution. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_sub_complement` | **complement-orientation gap identity**: `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N тИТ bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted (┬мA) N = (\|A\| тИТ \|┬мA\|)┬╖N`. Direct ring computation from PR #2779's simplified form via `(┬м┬мA) = A` at the filter level (PR #2780, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyComplement.lean`) |

Bridges: equals `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N` when `\|┬мA\| тЙд \|A\|`, equals `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted (┬мA) N` when `\|A\| тЙд \|┬мA\|`. This is the first concrete realisation of PR #2778's predicted ground-state subspace. The predicted Theorem 2.3 spin magnitude is bounded above by the maximum total spin `m_max = \|╬Ы\|┬╖S`. Direct from `\|\|A\| тИТ \|┬мA\|\| тЙд \|A\| + \|┬мA\| = \|╬Ы\|` (PR #2870) (PR #2874, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightNormLeMMax.lean`) |

| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_add_min_eq_two_norm_sq` | **`max + min` of canonical/complement predicted (┼Ь_tot)┬▓ eigenvalues = `2┬╖тАЦbiwтАЦ┬▓`** unconditionally: companion of PR #2968 (max тИТ min = `2┬╖тАЦbiwтАЦ`). Direct from PR #2966 + PR #2967 via `ring`. Equivalent to PR #2963 (canonical + complement sum) since `max + min = canonical + complement` (PR #2976, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxAddMin.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_sub_min_pos_iff_unbalanced` | **Iff: `max тИТ min > 0 тЖФ unbalanced`** at `N тЙе 1`: the canonical-complement eigenvalue gap (proportional to `тАЦbiwтАЦ`) vanishes iff balanced. Direct from PR #2968 (gap = `2┬╖тАЦbiwтАЦ`) + PR #2855 (`тАЦbiwтАЦ = 0 тЖФ balanced`). Companion of PR #2971 (`max > 0 тЖФ unbalanced`) (PR #2977, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxSubMinPosIffUnbalanced.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_abs_canonical_sub_complement_eq_two_norm` | **`\|canonical тИТ complement\| = 2┬╖тАЦbiwтАЦ`** unconditionally: unsigned version of PR #2960. Equivalent to PR #2968 (`max тИТ min = 2┬╖тАЦbiwтАЦ`) since `max тИТ min = \|canonical тИТ complement\|` (PR #2978, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueAbsDiff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_canonical_complement_eq_norm_sq_add` | **`max(canonical, complement) = тАЦbiwтАЦ┬▓ + тАЦbiwтАЦ`** unconditionally: expanded polynomial form of PR #2966. Convenient for `nlinarith` and arithmetic manipulations on the physical max eigenvalue (PR #2979, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxEqNormSqAdd.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_canonical_complement_eq_norm_sq_sub` | **`min(canonical, complement) = тАЦbiwтАЦ┬▓ тИТ тАЦbiwтАЦ`** unconditionally: expanded polynomial form of PR #2967. Companion of PR #2979 тАФ together makes the affine parameterisation `max = тАЦbiwтАЦ┬▓ + тАЦbiwтАЦ`, `min = тАЦbiwтАЦ┬▓ тИТ тАЦbiwтАЦ` explicit (PR #2980, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMinEqNormSqSub.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_canonical_complement_eq_canonical_of_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **`max(canonical, complement) = canonical`** at proper orientation `\|┬мA\| тЙд \|A\|`: the canonical orientation gives the physical predicted eigenvalue exactly at proper orientation. Direct from PR #2964 + PR #2965 + `biw.re тЙе 0` at proper orientation (PR #2981, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxEqCanonical.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_canonical_complement_lt_mMax_quad_iff_non_saturated` | **Iff: `max(canonical, complement) < m_max┬╖(m_max+1) тЖФ non-saturated`** at `N тЙе 1`: the iff form of PR #2975. Direct combine of PR #2970 (`max = m_max(m_max+1) тЖФ saturated`) + PR #2972 (`max тЙд m_max(m_max+1)`) (PR #2983, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxLtMMaxQuadIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_canonical_complement_eq_complement_of_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **`min(canonical, complement) = complement`** at proper orientation `\|┬мA\| тЙд \|A\|`: complement is the "wrong" (min) eigenvalue at proper orientation. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_canonical_complement_eq_complement_of_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **`max(canonical, complement) = complement`** at complement orientation `\|A\| тЙд \|┬мA\|`: complement is the physical (max) eigenvalue at complement orientation. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_canonical_complement_eq_canonical_of_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **`min(canonical, complement) = canonical`** at complement orientation `\|A\| тЙд \|┬мA\|`: canonical is the "wrong" (min) eigenvalue at complement orientation. | `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_norm_pos_iff_card_ne` | **Iff: `тАЦbiwтАЦ > 0 тЖФ unbalanced`** at `N тЙе 1`: iff form combining `тАЦbiwтАЦ тЙе 0` and PR #2855 (`тАЦbiwтАЦ = 0 тЖФ balanced`). | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_canonical_le_max` | **`canonical тЙд max(canonical, complement)`** unconditionally: trivial corollary of `le_max_left`. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_le_canonical` | **`min(canonical, complement) тЙд canonical`** unconditionally: trivial corollary of `min_le_left` (PR #2992, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMinLeCanonical.lean`) |

| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_canonical_eigenvalue_re_sub_sublattice_casimir` | **Predicted min energy decomposition**: `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N).re = canonical_eigenvalue.re тИТ Re(s_A┬╖(s_A+1)) тИТ Re(s_B┬╖(s_B+1))`. Direct from the definition of `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted` via `Complex.sub_re`. Makes explicit the relationship between predicted min energy of `H_toy` and the canonical predicted `(┼Ь_tot)┬▓` eigenvalue (PR #2994, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReViaEigenvalue.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_max_eigenvalue_re_sub_sublattice_casimir_of_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **Predicted min energy via max (physical) eigenvalue at proper orientation**: at `\|┬мA\| тЙд \|A\|`, `(predicted_min_energy).re = max(canonical, complement) тИТ Re(s_A┬╖(s_A+1)) тИТ Re(s_B┬╖(s_B+1))`. Combines PR #2994 + PR #2981 тАФ uses the physical predicted `(┼Ь_tot)┬▓` eigenvalue (PR #2995, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReAtProperOrientation.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_min_eigenvalue_re_sub_sublattice_casimir_of_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **Predicted min energy via min (wrong-orientation) eigenvalue at complement orientation**: at `\|A\| тЙд \|┬мA\|`, `(predicted_min_energy).re = min(canonical, complement) тИТ Re(s_A┬╖(s_A+1)) тИТ Re(s_B┬╖(s_B+1))`. Companion of PR #2995 тАФ at complement orientation, the canonical-formula uses the smaller `min` eigenvalue, underestimating the physical min energy. Combines PR #2994 + PR #2986 (PR #2996, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReAtComplementOrientation.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_add_complement` | **Complement-orientation sum identity**: `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N + bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted (┬мA) N = тИТ\|A\|┬╖\|┬мA\|┬╖N┬▓ тИТ \|╬Ы\|┬╖N`. Companion of the existing `_sub_complement` (gap identity). Together they give the affine sandwich: `predicted_min A = тИТ\|A\|┬╖\|┬мA\|┬╖N┬▓/2 тИТ \|┬мA\|┬╖N` and `predicted_min (┬мA) = тИТ\|A\|┬╖\|┬мA\|┬╖N┬▓/2 тИТ \|A\|┬╖N` (PR #2997, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedAddComplement.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_eq_complement_iff_card_eq` | **Iff: `predicted_min_energy A = predicted_min_energy (┬мA) тЖФ balanced`** at `N тЙе 1`: the two orientations give the same predicted min energy **iff** balanced sublattices. Direct from the existing `_sub_complement` gap identity `= (\|A\| тИТ \|┬мA\|)┬╖N` + `N тЙе 1` cancellation (PR #2998, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedEqComplementIffBalanced.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_le_predictedSymm_re` | **`(predicted_min A).re тЙд (predictedSymm A).re`** unconditionally: canonical-orientation predicted min energy is bounded above by the symmetric (min-aware) form. Difference: `(predictedSymm тИТ predicted_min A).re = (\|┬мA\| тИТ min(\|A\|, \|┬мA\|))┬╖N тЙе 0`. Equality at `\|┬мA\| тЙд \|A\|` (where `min = \|┬мA\|`); strict at `\|A\| < \|┬мA\|` and `N тЙе 1` (PR #2999, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReLeSymmRe.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_le_predictedSymm_re` | **`(predicted_min ┬мA).re тЙд (predictedSymm A).re`** unconditionally: mirror of PR #2999 тАФ complement-orientation predicted min energy bounded above by symmetric form. Difference: `(predictedSymm тИТ predicted_min ┬мA).re = (\|A\| тИТ min(\|A\|, \|┬мA\|))┬╖N тЙе 0`. Equality at `\|A\| тЙд \|┬мA\|`; strict at `\|┬мA\| < \|A\|` and `N тЙе 1`. Together with PR #2999 confirms `predictedSymm` as upper bound on both orientations (PR #3000, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedComplementReLeSymmRe.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_complement_re_eq_predictedSymm_re` | **`max((predicted_min A).re, (predicted_min ┬мA).re) = (predictedSymm A).re`** unconditionally: identifies `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm` as the **max** of orientation-specific predicted min energies. Direct from PR #2999/#3000 (upper bounds) plus equality at the orientation matching `min(\|A\|, \|┬мA\|)`. The symmetric form is the physical Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3 prediction (PR #3001, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMaxEqPredictedSymm.lean`) |

| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_eq_predictedSymm_re_of_balanced` | **At balanced: `(predicted_min ┬мA).re = (predictedSymm A).re`**. Mirror of PR #3003. At proper orientation (`\|┬мA\| тЙд \|A\|`) the difference vanishes; at complement orientation (`\|A\| тЙд \|┬мA\|`) the difference is `(\|┬мA\| тИТ \|A\|)┬╖N тЙе 0` (PR #3019, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymmSubPredictedReEq.lean`) |

Mirror of PR #3019. At complement orientation (`\|A\| тЙд \|┬мA\|`) the difference vanishes; at proper orientation the difference is `(\|A\| тИТ \|┬мA\|)┬╖N тЙе 0` (PR #3020, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymmSubComplementPredictedReEq.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_sub_complement_re_eq` | **Signed difference: `(predicted_min A).re тИТ (predicted_min ┬мA).re = (\|A\| тИТ \|┬мA\|)┬╖N`** unconditionally. Matches PR #3012's spread formula `max тИТ min = \|\|A\| тИТ \|┬мA\|\|┬╖N` тАФ gives alternative spread characterisation via absolute signed difference (PR #3022, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedAbsSubComplementEq.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_sub_min_complement_re_eq_zero_of_balanced` | **Spread vanishes at balanced (unconditional in `N`)**: `\|A\| = \|┬мA\| тЖТ spread = 0`. Since `\|A\| = \|╬Ы\|` at this edge, gives `spread = \|╬Ы\|┬╖N` тАФ the maximum possible spread (PR #3026, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSpreadAtSaturated.lean`) |

| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_norm_eq_zero_iff_card_zero` | **norm-zero characterisation** at `N тЙе 1`: `тАЦbipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A NтАЦ = 0 тЖФ \|A\| = 0 тИи \|┬мA\| = 0`. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_im_zero` / `_re_eq` | **`bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm` is real**: imaginary part is `0` and real part is `-\|A\|┬╖\|┬мA\|┬╖N┬▓/2 - min(\|A\|, \|┬мA\|)┬╖N` (with `min` taken in `тДЭ` after `Nat.cast_min`). Mirrors PR #2825 for the asymmetric form (PR #2843, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmReal.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_nonpos` | **non-positivity of the predicted symmetric min energy**: `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re тЙд 0`. Both terms `-\|A\|┬╖\|┬мA\|┬╖N┬▓/2` and `-min(\|A\|, \|┬мA\|)┬╖N` are non-positive. Symmetric-form mirror of PR #2791 (asymmetric `_re_nonpos`) (PR #2844, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmNonpos.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_neg_of_nondegenerate` | **strict negativity of the predicted symmetric min energy** (non-degenerate case): `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re < 0` when both `\|A\| тЙе 1`, `\|┬мA\| тЙе 1`, and `N тЙе 1`. Symmetric-form mirror of PR #2817 (PR #2845, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmStrictNeg.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_norm_eq` | **norm of the predicted symmetric min energy**: `тАЦbipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A NтАЦ = \|A\|┬╖\|┬мA\|┬╖N┬▓/2 + min(\|A\|, \|┬мA\|)┬╖N`. Combines PR #2843's `_re_eq` + `_im_zero` with PR #2844's `_re_nonpos`: norm = `|.re| = -.re` for a non-positive real value, then expand (PR #2848, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmNorm.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_eq_zero_of_cardNotA_zero` / `_of_cardA_zero` | **symmetric predicted min energy vanishes at saturated edges**: at `\|┬мA\| = 0` or `\|A\| = 0`, `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N = 0`. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_norm_eq_zero_of_cardNotA_zero` / `_of_cardA_zero` | **symm-energy norm vanishes at saturated edges**: `тАЦbipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A NтАЦ = 0` at `\|┬мA\| = 0` or `\|A\| = 0`. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_eq_balanced_form_of_card_eq` | **balanced-case symm-predicted min energy**: when `\|A\| = \|┬мA\|`, `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N = -\|A\|┬▓┬╖N┬▓/2 - \|A\|┬╖N`. | `bipartiteImbalanceWeight` / `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_eq_bipartiteImbalanceWeight` / `neelStateOfS_mem_magSubspaceS_bipartiteImbalanceWeight` / `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_eq_zero_of_card_eq` / `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_eq_mMax_of_cardNotA_zero` / `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_re_nonneg_of_cardA_ge_cardNotA` | **bipartite imbalance weight foundation** for Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3 (╬│-4 / `\|A\| тЙа \|┬мA\|` case): definition `bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N := (\|A\| тИТ \|┬мA\|) ┬╖ N / 2` тАФ the **signed** `┼Ь_tot^(3)` magnetization weight of the N├йel orientation `neelStateOfS A N`. (In the `\|A\| тЙе \|┬мA\|` orientation, this signed weight equals the predicted Theorem 2.3 total spin `\|\|A\| тИТ \|┬мA\|\|┬╖S`; in the opposite orientation it is its negative.) Packages: bridge `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS A N) = bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N`; membership `neelStateOfS A N тИИ magSubspaceS ╬Ы N (bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N)`; two edge cases (balanced bipartite тЖТ weight 0; saturated bipartite тЖТ weight m_max); non-negativity of the real part when `\|A\| тЙе \|┬мA\|`. First foundational step toward formalising Theorem 2.3's prediction at the magnetization-sector level (PR #2773, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelBipartiteWeight.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSDot_apply_diag_neel` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_apply_diag_neel` | **spin-`S` toy Hamiltonian N├йel-state expectation value**: `тЯи╬ж_N├йel \| ─д_toy_S \| ╬ж_N├йelтЯй = -\|A\|┬╖\|┬мA\|┬╖N┬▓/2`. Proof via PR #1055's `─д_toy_S = 2 тАв (┼Ь_A ┬╖ ┼Ь_┬мA)` and per-pair diagonal `(spinSDot x y) (neel) (neel) = m_x ┬╖ m_y = -N┬▓/4` for `x тИИ A, y тИИ ┬мA` (╬│-4 step 28) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1070) |

| `totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_of_mem` / `totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_of_mem` | spin-`S` SU(2) raising/lowering ladder operators shift the magnetization subspace by ┬▒1: `┼Ь^тИУ_tot` maps `magSubspaceS ╬Ы N M` to `magSubspaceS ╬Ы N (M тИУ 1)`. | `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` / `_complement_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | spin-`1/2` sublattice Casimir preserves each magnetisation subspace `H_M`. | `totalSpinSOpPlus_eq_sublattice_sum` / `totalSpinSOpMinus_eq_sublattice_sum` | spin-`S` total raising/lowering operators decompose across the bipartition: `┼Ь^┬▒_tot = ┼Ь_A^┬▒ + ┼Ь_┬мA^┬▒`. Mirror of axis decompositions (╬│-4 step 44) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1086) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` | spin-`S` sublattice ladder operators annihilate the appropriate extremal all-aligned state: `┼Ь_A^+ ┬╖ \|╧Г_тКдтЯй = 0` and `┼Ь_A^- ┬╖ \|╧Г_тКетЯй = 0`. | `totalSpinSOp3_commutator_sublatticeSpinSOpPlus` / `_OpMinus` | total Cartan relation for sublattice ladders: `[┼Ь_tot^(3), ┼Ь_A^┬▒] = ┬▒┼Ь_A^┬▒`. Trivial direct computation since `╧Гx = c` constant. Bridges ╬│-4 step 218 to existing `allAlignedStateS_zero_/_last_expectation_totalSpinSSquared` (╬│-4 step 222) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` (PR #1269) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS` | **N├йel `┼Ь_tot^(3)` eigenvalue**: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS A N) = (\|A\| тИТ \|┬мA\|)┬╖N/2`. Direct from `magSumS_neelConfigOfS` (= `\|┬мA\|┬╖N`) and the filter-card decomposition `\|V\| = \|A\| + \|┬мA\|`. Named lemma extracted from inlined computation in `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_card_Lambda` (╬│-4 step 224) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeelBasisVecS.lean` (PR #1271) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_via_general` | **Spin-`S` N├йel Casimir re-derived via the general formula**: `<╬ж_N├йel \| (┼Ь_tot)┬▓ \| ╬ж_N├йel> = ((\|A\|тИТ\|┬мA\|)┬╖N/2)┬▓ + \|V\|┬╖N/2` derived from ╬│-4 step 218 + ╬│-4 step 221 + ╬│-4 step 224 + `ring`. Unification check against the existing `_card_Lambda` form (╬│-4 step 127). Validates that the general formula correctly specializes to the N├йel-specific result (╬│-4 step 225) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeelBasisVecS.lean` (PR #1272) |
| `allAlignedStateS_zero/_last_totalSpinSSquared_via_general` | **Spin-`S` allAligned-up/down Casimir re-derived via general formula**: `<╬ж_тКд \| (┼Ь_tot)┬▓ \| ╬ж_тКд> = m_max┬╖(m_max+1)` (and same for `╬ж_тКе`) where `m_max = \|V\|┬╖N/2`. Composition of ╬│-4 step 218 + ╬│-4 step 222 + `magEigenvalueS_allAlignedConfigS` + `ring`. Recovers existing `allAlignedStateS_zero_expectation_totalSpinSSquared` (╬│-4 step 226) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1273) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfSquared_expectation_via_general` | **Spin-`1/2` N├йel Casimir re-derived via general formula**: `<╬ж_N├йel \| (┼Ь_tot)┬▓ \| ╬ж_N├йel> = ((\|A\|тИТ\|┬мA\|)/2)┬▓ + \|╬Ы\|/2`. Composition of ╬│-4 step 216 + `magnetization_neelConfigOf` + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 225 (╬│-4 step 227) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1274) |
| `basisVec_const_totalSpinHalfSquared_expectation_via_general` | **Spin-`1/2` const-state Casimir re-derived via general formula**: `<basisVec(const s) \| (┼Ь_tot)┬▓ \| basisVec(const s)> = \|╬Ы\|┬╖(\|╬Ы\|+2)/4` for any `s : Fin 2`. Composition of ╬│-4 step 216 + `magnetization (const s) = ┬▒\|╬Ы\|` + `(spinSign s)┬▓ = 1` + `ring`. Recovers existing ╬│-4 step 201 (╬│-4 step 228) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1275) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement` | **Complement N├йel `┼Ь_tot^(3)` eigenvalue**: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (┬мA) N) = (\|┬мA\|тИТ\|A\|)┬╖N/2`. Sublattice-swap symmetry; complement of ╬│-4 step 224. Direct corollary by applying ╬│-4 step 224 to `(┬мA)` (╬│-4 step 229) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1276) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement_simplified` | **Cleaner form** of ╬│-4 step 229 with double negation `!!A` simplified to `A` via `Finset.filter_congr` + `Bool.not_not`: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (┬мA) N) = (\|┬мA\|тИТ\|A\|)┬╖N/2` (╬│-4 step 230) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1277) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement_eq_neg` | **Negation relation**: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (┬мA) N) = тИТmagEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS A N)`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 224 + ╬│-4 step 230 + `ring`. The complement N├йel sits at the opposite `┼Ь_tot^(3)` eigenvalue (╬│-4 step 231) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1278) |
| `magnetization_neelConfigOf_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` negation relation**: `magnetization ╬Ы (neelConfigOf (┬мA)) = тИТmagnetization ╬Ы (neelConfigOf A)`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 231; direct from existing `magnetization_neelConfigOf{,_complement}` + `ring` (╬│-4 step 232) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1279) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfSquared_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` Casimir is sublattice-swap invariant on N├йel**: `<╬ж_N├йel(┬мA)\|(┼Ь_tot)┬▓\|╬ж_N├йel(┬мA)> = <╬ж_N├йel(A)\|(┼Ь_tot)┬▓\|╬ж_N├йel(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 227 (Casimir = `(M/2)┬▓+\|╬Ы\|/2`) + ╬│-4 step 232 (`M(┬мA) = тИТM(A)`): `(M)┬▓` is sign-flip invariant (╬│-4 step 233) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1280) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` Casimir is sublattice-swap invariant on N├йel**: `<╬ж_N├йel(┬мA)\|(┼Ь_tot)┬▓\|╬ж_N├йel(┬мA)> = <╬ж_N├йel(A)\|(┼Ь_tot)┬▓\|╬ж_N├йel(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 218 + 221 + 231 + `ring`: `magEigenvalueS┬▓` is sign-flip invariant; `╬г_x m_x┬▓` evaluates to `\|V\|┬╖(N/2)┬▓` for both A and ┬мA. Spin-`S` mirror of ╬│-4 step 233 (╬│-4 step 234) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1281) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` `(┼Ь_tot^(3))┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├йel**: `<╬ж_N├йel(┬мA)\|(┼Ь_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬ж_N├йel(┬мA)> = <╬ж_N├йel(A)\|(┼Ь_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬ж_N├йel(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 205 + 231 + `ring`: squared eigenvalue is sign-flip invariant (╬│-4 step 235) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1282) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` `(┼Ь_tot^(3))┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├йel**: `<╬ж_N├йel(┬мA)\|(┼Ь_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬ж_N├йel(┬мA)> = <╬ж_N├йel(A)\|(┼Ь_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬ж_N├йel(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 204 + 232 + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 235 (╬│-4 step 236) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1283) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` transverse Casimir `(┼Ь^1)┬▓+(┼Ь^2)┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├йel**: equal expectations on `╬ж_N├йel(A)` and `╬ж_N├йel(┬мA)`. Both reduce to `\|V\|┬╖N/2` via ╬│-4 step 220 + 221 (╬│-4 step 237) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1284) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse Casimir invariance on N├йel**: both expectations equal `\|╬Ы\|/2`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 219 (╧Г-independent constant). Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 237 (╬│-4 step 238) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1285) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`S` linear `┼Ь_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on N├йel**: `<╬ж_N├йel(┬мA)\|┼Ь_tot^(3)\|╬ж_N├йel(┬мA)> = тИТ<╬ж_N├йel(A)\|┼Ь_tot^(3)\|╬ж_N├йel(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 206 + 231 (╬│-4 step 239) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1286) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` linear `┼Ь_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on N├йel**. Direct from ╬│-4 step 207 + 232 + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 239 (╬│-4 step 240) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1287) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`S` N├йel transverse linear expectations vanish**: `<╬ж_N├йel\|┼Ь_tot^(1,2)\|╬ж_N├йel> = 0`. Trivial corollary of ╬│-4 step 214 applied to `╧Г = neelConfigOfS A N` (╬│-4 step 241). Together with ╬│-4 step 91 (`<┼Ь_tot^(3)> = magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS`), gives the complete per-axis profile `(0, 0, (\|A\|тИТ\|┬мA\|)┬╖N/2)` | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1288) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`1/2` N├йel transverse linear expectations vanish**: same as ╬│-4 step 241 for spin-`1/2`, derived from ╬│-4 step 215 (╬│-4 step 242) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1289) |

The single-cluster Problem 2.5.a modules below are imported from the
build root again after the 2026-05-30 orphan-module sweep had removed
the earlier implementation.  The restored concrete tip is
`Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean`, and the
minimum-eigenvalue bridge in `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianMin.lean`
keeps the abstract Hamiltonian, energy hygiene, concrete
dimer/trimer/quartet/pentamer eigenvalue formulas, and variational
upper/lower-bound consumers live in the default Lean build
(PR #4032; PR #4033; PR #4034; PR #4035; PR #4036).

| `singleClusterHamiltonianS` | **Single-cluster (star-graph) Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki Problem 2.5.a, p. 38): `H = ╬г_{j=1}^z ┼Ь_0 ┬╖ ┼Ь_j` on `Fin (z + 1)` with central vertex `0` and `z` leaves. Ground-state energy `тИТS(1 + zS)` (╬│-5 step 243) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1290) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_isHermitian` | **Hermiticity** of the single-cluster Heisenberg Hamiltonian. Sum of Hermitian `spinSDot 0 j N` over `j тИИ univ.erase 0` (╬│-5 step 244) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1291) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_zero_z` | **Edge case `z = 0`**: `singleClusterHamiltonianS 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = тИЕ` in `Fin 1`. Tasaki's formula `тИТS(1 + zS)` is intended for `z тЙе 1` (╬│-5 step 245) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1292) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<╬ж_тКд\|H\|╬ж_тКд> = z┬╖(N/2)┬▓` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian. Far above Tasaki's GS energy `тИТS(1+zS)` since all-up is in the maximum-`s_tot` Casimir sector, not the minimum (╬│-5 step 246) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1293) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allAligned_expectation` | **AllAligned-c expectation**: `<╬ж_aligned(c)\|H\|╬ж_aligned(c)> = z┬╖(N/2 тИТ c.val)┬▓`. Generalises ╬│-5 step 246 (the `c = 0` case). For `c = Fin.last N` (all-down) gives same `z┬╖(N/2)┬▓` (╬│-5 step 247) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1294) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allDown_expectation` | **All-down expectation**: `<╬ж_тКе\|H\|╬ж_тКе> = z┬╖(N/2)┬▓`. Direct specialisation of ╬│-5 step 247 at `c = Fin.last N` (╬│-5 step 248) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1295) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Leaf-spin operators on `Fin (z+1)`**: `┼Ь_R^(╬▒) = ╬г_{j тЙа 0} onSiteS j ┼Ь^(╬▒)` for axes `╬▒ = 1, 2, 3`. Sum over leaves of single-site `┼Ь^(╬▒)` (excludes central vertex `0`). Building blocks for the upcoming Casimir decomposition `H = (┼Ь_tot┬▓ тИТ ┼Ь_0┬▓ тИТ ┼Ь_R┬▓)/2` (╬│-5 step 249) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1296) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eq_dot_leaves` | **`┼Ь_0 ┬╖ ┼Ь_R` decomposition**: `H = ╬г_╬▒ onSiteS 0 (┼Ь^(╬▒)) ┬╖ leafSpinSOp_╬▒`. Direct distribution of left multiplication over the sum `╬г_j (A * B_j) = A * (╬г_j B_j)` via `Finset.mul_sum` (╬│-5 step 250) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1297) |
| `totalSpinSOp1/2/3_eq_onSite_zero_add_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Total = central + leaves** (per axis): `totalSpinSOp_╬▒ (Fin (z+1)) N = onSiteS 0 (┼Ь^(╬▒)) + leafSpinSOp_╬▒ z N`. Direct from `Finset.sum_erase_add` + `add_comm` (╬│-5 step 251) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1298) |
| `leafSpinSSquared` | **Leaf-spin Casimir**: `┼Ь_R┬▓ := (┼Ь_R^(1))┬▓ + (┼Ь_R^(2))┬▓ + (┼Ь_R^(3))┬▓`, total-spin-squared restricted to leaves of `Fin (z+1)` (╬│-5 step 252) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1299) |
| `onSiteS_zero_commute_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Center-leaf commutativity**: `Commute (onSiteS 0 ┼Ь^(╬▒)) (leafSpinSOp_╬▒ z N)`. Direct from `Commute.sum_right` + `onSiteS_commute_of_ne` (disjoint sites) (╬│-5 step 253) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1300) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_eq_casimir_diff` | **Casimir decomposition**: `2┬╖H = (┼Ь_tot)┬▓ тИТ ┼Ь_0┬▓ тИТ ┼Ь_R┬▓` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian, where `┼Ь_0┬▓ = spinSDot 0 0 N` and `┼Ь_R┬▓ = leafSpinSSquared z N`. Proof: expand `╬г_╬▒ totalSpinSOp_╬▒┬▓ = ╬г_╬▒ (onSite 0 + leaf_╬▒)┬▓` via ╬│-5 step 251 + `(a+b)┬▓ = a┬▓ + 2ab + b┬▓` (using ╬│-5 step 253 commutativity) + ╬│-5 step 250 (cross term identification) + `noncomm_ring` (╬│-5 step 254) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1301) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_smul_eq_casimir_diff` | **тДВ-smul Casimir form**: `(2 : тДВ) тАв H = (┼Ь_tot)┬▓ тИТ ┼Ь_0┬▓ тИТ ┼Ь_R┬▓`. Direct corollary of ╬│-5 step 254 via `two_mul + two_smul` (╬│-5 step 255) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1302) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_expectation` | **Casimir decomposition expectation form**: `2 ┬╖ <v\|H\|v> = <v\|Stot┬▓\|v> тИТ <v\|S0┬▓\|v> тИТ <v\|SR┬▓\|v>` for any `v`. Direct corollary of ╬│-5 step 255 + linearity of `dotProduct + mulVec` over `smul` and subtraction (╬│-5 step 256) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1303) |
| `spinSDot_self_expectation_general` | **Single-site Casimir expectation**: `<v\|spinSDot x x N\|v> = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ <v\|v>` for any `v`. Direct from `spinSDot_self` (= scalar matrix) + linearity. Used to evaluate the `S0┬▓` term in the Casimir decomposition (╬│-5 step 257) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1304) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_expectation_simplified` | **Simplified Casimir expectation**: `2 ┬╖ <v\|H\|v> = <v\|Stot┬▓\|v> тИТ (N(N+2)/4)┬╖<v\|v> тИТ <v\|SR┬▓\|v>`. Combines ╬│-5 step 256 + 257; the `S0┬▓` term is now an explicit scalar multiple of the norm-squared (╬│-5 step 258) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1305) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_of_joint_casimir_eigenvec` | **H eigenvalue from joint Casimir eigenvalues**: if `Stot┬▓┬╖v = ╬▒┬╖v` and `SR┬▓┬╖v = ╬▓┬╖v`, then `H┬╖v = (╬▒ тИТ N(N+2)/4 тИТ ╬▓)/2 ┬╖ v`. Reduces the GS-energy problem to enumerating admissible `(╬▒, ╬▓)` joint eigenvalues (╬│-5 step 259) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1306) |
| `spinSDot_self_mulVec_eq_smul` | **Single-site Casimir as scalar action**: `spinSDot x x N ┬╖ v = (N(N+2)/4) тАв v` for any `v`. Direct from `spinSDot_self` (= scalar matrix). Standalone helper extracted from ╬│-5 step 259 (╬│-5 step 260) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1307) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3_zero_z` and `leafSpinSSquared_zero_z` | **Edge case `z=0`** (single vertex, no leaves): `leafSpinSOp_╬▒ 0 N = 0` and `leafSpinSSquared 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = тИЕ` in `Fin 1` (╬│-5 step 261) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1308) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_eq_sum_spinSDot` | **`leafSpinSSquared` as double sum**: `leafSpinSSquared z N = ╬г_{j,k тИИ univ.erase 0} spinSDot j k N`. Mirrors `totalSpinSSquared_eq_sum_spinSDot` for the leaf-only Casimir (╬│-5 step 262) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1309) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<╬ж_тКд\|leafSpinSSquared z N\|╬ж_тКд> = (zN/2)┬╖(zN/2 + 1) = s_R(s_R+1)` where `s_R = z┬╖(N/2)` is max leaf spin. Computed by rearranging ╬│-5 step 254 (Casimir decomposition) and applying closed forms for `<Stot┬▓>`, `<S0┬▓>`, `<H>` (╬│-5 step 263) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1310) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **Eigenvector form on allUp**: `H ┬╖ \|╬ж_тКдтЯй = z┬╖(N/2)┬▓ ┬╖ \|╬ж_тКдтЯй`. Proof: each leaf-pair `spinSDot 0 j` at `j тЙа 0` gives `(N┬▓/4)┬╖\|╬ж_тКдтЯй` (via `spinSDot_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero_of_ne`); sum over `z` leaves (╬│-5 step 264) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1311) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **Eigenvector form** of `┼Ь_R┬▓` on allUp: `┼Ь_R┬▓ ┬╖ \|╬ж_тКдтЯй = (zN/2)┬╖(zN/2+1) ┬╖ \|╬ж_тКдтЯй`. Witnesses that `\|╬ж_тКдтЯй` is in the maximum-leaf-spin Casimir sector `s_R = zN/2`. Combined with ╬│-5 step 264 + existing `Stot┬▓` eigenvector identity, confirms `\|╬ж_тКдтЯй` is a joint eigenstate of `H, Stot┬▓, ┼Ь_0┬▓, ┼Ь_R┬▓`. Proof: rearrange ╬│-5 step 255 + apply (╬│-5 step 265) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1312) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` | **Eigenvector form on allDown**: `H ┬╖ \|╬ж_тКетЯй = z┬╖(N/2)┬▓ ┬╖ \|╬ж_тКетЯй`. AllDown mirror of ╬│-5 step 264; same eigenvalue (spin-flip symmetry) (╬│-5 step 266) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1313) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` | **Eigenvector form** of `┼Ь_R┬▓` on allDown: `┼Ь_R┬▓ ┬╖ \|╬ж_тКетЯй = (zN/2)┬╖(zN/2+1) ┬╖ \|╬ж_тКетЯй`. AllDown mirror of ╬│-5 step 265; both `\|╬ж_тКдтЯй` and `\|╬ж_тКетЯй` lie in the maximum-leaf-spin Casimir sector `s_R = zN/2`, differing only by total `┼Ь_tot^(3)` magnetization (╬│-5 step 267) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1314) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_at_gs_casimir_sector` | **GS-sector eigenvalue specialization**: if `v` is a joint eigenvector of `┼Ь_tot┬▓` at `((zтИТ1)N/2)((zтИТ1)N/2+1)` and `┼Ь_R┬▓` at `(zN/2)(zN/2+1)`, then `H ┬╖ v = тИТ(N/2)┬╖(zN/2+1) ┬╖ v = тИТS(1+zS) ┬╖ v`. Specialization of ╬│-5 step 259 to the ground-state sector predicted by Tasaki Problem 2.5.a (╬│-5 step 268) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1315) |

| `exists_joint_eigenvector_in_invariant_submodule` | **Joint invariant-submodule eigenvector bridge**: over an algebraically closed field, any non-zero finite-dimensional submodule invariant under two commuting endomorphisms contains a non-zero simultaneous eigenvector for both. It is enough to produce a non-zero same-energy witness `w` whose total/leaf Casimir eigenvalues have Casimir energy at least `Re (singleClusterGSEnergyS z N)`. Together with cross-commute, gives `Commute (┼Ь_A)┬▓ (┼Ь_tot^(╬▒))`, hence `Commute (┼Ь_A)┬▓ (┼Ь_tot)┬▓` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` |
