| `totalSpinHalfOp{Plus,Minus}_exp_commute_of_commute` | ladder version: `Commute A (┼Ь^┬▒_tot) тЖТ Commute A (exp(c тАв ┼Ь^┬▒_tot))` for any `c : тДВ` (useful for U(1) symmetry) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conjTranspose_mul_self` | `(├Ы^(╬▒)_╬╕_tot)с┤┤ * ├Ы^(╬▒)_╬╕_tot = 1` (unitarity). Derived from `exp_mem_unitary_of_mem_skewAdjoint` after recognizing `-i╬╕ ┼Ь_tot^(╬▒)` as skew-adjoint | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_eq_self_of_commute` | Tasaki eq. (2.2.13) finite form: `Commute A (┼Ь_tot^(╬▒)) тЖТ (├Ы^(╬▒)_╬╕_tot)с┤┤ * A * ├Ы^(╬▒)_╬╕_tot = A`. Combines `_commute_of_commute` with unitarity | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `IsInMagnetizationSubspace` | predicate for the magnetization-`M` eigenspace `H_M` (Tasaki eq. (2.2.9)/(2.2.10)) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace M` | the magnetization-`M` eigenspace as a `Submodule тДВ ((╬Ы тЖТ Fin 2) тЖТ тДВ)` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `basisVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace` | `|╧ГтЯй тИИ H_{|╧Г|/2}` тАФ basis states lie in their magnetization subspace | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_disjoint` | distinct sectors `H_M тКУ H_{M'} = тКе` (`M тЙа M'`) тАФ eigenvalue uniqueness | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `iSup_magnetizationSubspace_eq_top` | `тиЖ_M H_M = тКд` тАФ every vector decomposes as a sum across sectors | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_eq_eigenspace` | bridge `H_M = (┼Ь_tot^(3) as End).eigenspace M` (used to inherit `iSupIndep`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_iSupIndep` | `iSupIndep`: each sector is disjoint from the supremum of all others | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_isInternal` | `DirectSum.IsInternal`: full direct-sum decomposition `H = тКХ_M H_M` (Tasaki eqs. (2.2.9)/(2.2.10)) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |

### Two-site spin inner product (Tasaki ┬з2.2 eq. (2.2.16))

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬з2.2 eq. (2.2.16), p. 24.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinHalfDot x y` | `┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y := ╬г_{╬▒} onSite x ┼Ь^(╬▒) ┬╖ onSite y ┼Ь^(╬▒)` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_eq_plus_minus` | `┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y = (1/2)(┼Ь_x^+ ┼Ь_y^- + ┼Ь_x^- ┼Ь_y^+) + ┼Ь_x^(3) ┼Ь_y^(3)` (Tasaki (2.2.16)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_comm` | `┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y = ┼Ь_y ┬╖ ┼Ь_x` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_self` | `┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_x = (3/4) ┬╖ 1` (the S(S+1) = 3/4 identity for S = 1/2) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_isHermitian` | `┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y` is Hermitian | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_eq_sum_dot` | `(┼Ь_tot)┬▓ = ╬г_{x,y} ┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfPairSpinSq` / `spinHalfPairSpinSq_eq` | `(┼Ь_x + ┼Ь_y)┬▓ = 2┬╖(┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y) + ┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_x + ┼Ь_y ┬╖ ┼Ь_y` (Tasaki (2.2.18)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | `[┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y, ┼Ь_tot^(╬▒)] = 0` for ╬▒ тИИ {1, 2, 3} (SU(2) invariance, Tasaki (2.2.17)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_commutator_totalSpinHalfOpPlus/Minus` | `[┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y, ┼Ь^┬▒_tot] = 0` (ladder-operator version of SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_parallel` | `┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y |╧ГтЯй = (1/4) |╧ГтЯй` when `╧Г x = ╧Г y` and `x тЙа y` (Tasaki (2.2.19) parallel case) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_both_up/down` | `┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y |тЖСтЖСтЯй = (1/4) |тЖСтЖСтЯй`, `┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y |тЖУтЖУтЯй = (1/4) |тЖУтЖУтЯй` (Tasaki (2.2.19) triplet `m = ┬▒1`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `basisSwap` / `basisSwap_involutive` / `basisSwap_antiparallel` | site-swap of `╧Г` at `x, y`, involutive and preserves anti-parallelism | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_antiparallel` | `┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y |╧ГтЯй = (1/2) |swap ╧ГтЯй - (1/4) |╧ГтЯй` when `╧Г x тЙа ╧Г y` (anti-parallel case) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_singlet` | singlet eigenvalue `┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y (|╧ГтЯй - |swap ╧ГтЯй) = -(3/4) (|╧ГтЯй - |swap ╧ГтЯй)` (Tasaki (2.2.19) singlet `S = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_triplet_anti` | triplet `m = 0` eigenvalue `┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y (|╧ГтЯй + |swap ╧ГтЯй) = (1/4) (|╧ГтЯй + |swap ╧ГтЯй)` (Tasaki (2.2.19) triplet `m = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian` | `H = ╬г_{x,y} J(x,y) ┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y` (general Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | `[H, ┼Ь_tot^(╬▒)] = 0` for all axes (Tasaki (2.2.13) SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{Plus,Minus}` | `[H, ┼Ь^┬▒_tot] = 0` (ladder form of SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | `Commute H ┼Ьtot┬▓` тАФ the Casimir operator-level form of SU(2) invariance (consequence of `[H, ┼Ьtot^╬▒] = 0` for each ╬▒, via `Commute.mul_right` and `.add_right`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_preserves_totalSpinHalfSquared_eigenvalue` | if `┼Ьtot┬▓ ┬╖ v = S ┬╖ v` then `┼Ьtot┬▓ ┬╖ (H ┬╖ v) = S ┬╖ (H ┬╖ v)` тАФ operator-level simultaneous diagonalisation of `H` and the SU(2) Casimir | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfOpPlus_mul_pauliZ` / `pauliZ_mul_spinHalfOpPlus` | `╧Г^+ ┬╖ ╧Г^z = -╧Г^+` and `╧Г^z ┬╖ ╧Г^+ = ╧Г^+` тАФ the (anti)commutation at the single-site Pauli algebra level, used for the Jordan-Wigner cross-site CAR | `Quantum/SpinHalfBasis.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_const` | `┼Ь_tot┬▓ |s s тАж sтЯй = (N(N+2)/4) |s s тАж sтЯй` for any constant `s : Fin 2` (Casimir eigenvalue at maximum total spin `S = N/2`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_all_up/down` | specializations of the above to `s = 0` (all-up) and `s = 1` (all-down) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOp{Minus,Plus}_pow_basisVec_const` | for any `s : Fin 2` and `k : тДХ`, `┼Ьtot┬▓ ┬╖ (┼Ьtot^тИУ)^k ┬╖ |sтАжsтЯй = (|╬Ы|┬╖(|╬Ы|+2)/4) ┬╖ (┼Ьtot^тИУ)^k ┬╖ |sтАжsтЯй` тАФ the iterated ladder iterates remain in the maximum-total-spin SU(2) representation `S = Smax = |╬Ы|/2` (Casimir invariance, Tasaki ┬з2.4) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | `H |sтАжsтЯй = (╬г_{x,y} J(x,y)┬╖(if x=y then 3/4 else 1/4)) ┬╖ |sтАжsтЯй` тАФ every Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian acts on a uniformly-aligned basis state as a scalar; bilinear-sum lift of Tasaki ┬з2.4 eq. (2.4.5), p. 32 (`-┼Ь_x┬╖┼Ь_y |╬жтЖСтЯй = -S┬▓ |╬жтЖСтЯй` for `S = 1/2`, `x тЙа y`), with the diagonal `S(S+1) = 3/4` contribution recorded explicitly | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up/down` | specialisations of the above to `s = 0` (all-up) / `s = 1` (all-down) тАФ the eigenvector property of the fully-polarised states; ground-state status (Tasaki's `E_GS = -|B|┬╖S┬▓`) requires extra ferromagnetic structure on `J` and is not asserted here | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_basisVec_const` | `H ┬╖ (┼Ь_tot^+ ┬╖ |sтАжsтЯй) = c_J ┬╖ (┼Ь_tot^+ ┬╖ |sтАжsтЯй)` тАФ `┼Ь_tot^+` preserves the H-eigenvalue on a constant-spin basis state (corollary of SU(2) invariance, Tasaki ┬з2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_basisVec_const` | same with `┼Ь_tot^-` тАФ the canonical lowering ladder Tasaki uses to enumerate the ferromagnetic ground states `|╬ж_MтЯй` (eq. (2.4.9), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_const` | iterated form: for any constant `s : Fin 2` and any `k : тДХ`, `H ┬╖ ((┼Ь_tot^-)^k ┬╖ |sтАжsтЯй) = c_J ┬╖ ((┼Ь_tot^-)^k ┬╖ |sтАжsтЯй)`; specialised at `s = 0` this gives the unnormalised Tasaki ┬з2.4 (2.4.9), p. 33 тАФ every iterate `(┼Ь_tot^-)^k ┬╖ |╬жтЖСтЯй` lies in the same H-eigenspace as `|╬жтЖСтЯй` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_const` | companion iterated form for `┼Ь_tot^+`: for any constant `s : Fin 2` and any `k : тДХ`, `H ┬╖ ((┼Ь_tot^+)^k ┬╖ |sтАжsтЯй) = c_J ┬╖ ((┼Ь_tot^+)^k ┬╖ |sтАжsтЯй)` (corollary of SU(2) invariance, Tasaki ┬з2.4 (2.4.7), iterated) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}` | for any `J` and `╬╕ : тДЭ`, `H` commutes with the global rotation `├Ы^(╬▒)_╬╕ = exp(-i╬╕ ┼Ь_tot^╬▒)` (composes `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{╬▒}` with `totalSpinHalfRot{╬▒}_commute_of_commute`; the operator-level form of Tasaki ┬з2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_basisVec_const` | for any `J`, `╬╕`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H ┬╖ (├Ы^(╬▒)_╬╕ ┬╖ |sтАжsтЯй) = c_J ┬╖ (├Ы^(╬▒)_╬╕ ┬╖ |sтАжsтЯй)` тАФ the rotated (single-axis) constant-spin state shares the H-eigenvalue (Tasaki ┬з2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfRot32_basisVec_const` | for any `J`, `╬╕`, `╧Х`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H ┬╖ (├Ы^(3)_╧Х ┬╖ ├Ы^(2)_╬╕ ┬╖ |sтАжsтЯй) = c_J ┬╖ (├Ы^(3)_╧Х ┬╖ ├Ы^(2)_╬╕ ┬╖ |sтАжsтЯй)` тАФ the two-step spin-coherent state of Tasaki eq. (2.4.6) (`|╬Ю_╬╕,╧ХтЯй` for `s = 0`) is an H-eigenvector with the same eigenvalue as the constant configuration (Tasaki eq. (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | `┼Ь_tot┬▓ (|тЖСтЖУтЯй - |тЖУтЖСтЯй) = 0` for `╬Ы = Fin 2` (singlet, `S = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_triplet_zero` | `┼Ь_tot┬▓ (|тЖСтЖУтЯй + |тЖУтЖСтЯй) = 2(|тЖСтЖУтЯй + |тЖУтЖСтЯй)` for `╬Ы = Fin 2` (triplet `m = 0`, `S = 1`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | the two-site singlet has zero `┼Ь_tot^(3)` magnetization | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_totalOnSite` | `[onSite x S╬▒, ╬г_z onSite z S╬▓] = onSite x [S╬▒, S╬▓]` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

### One-dimensional open-chain quantum Ising

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬з3.3 eq. (3.3.1), p. 55 (transverse-field Ising on an open
chain). Our formalization uses the Pauli convention `╧Г = 2┬╖S` and an
explicit bond coupling `J`.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h` | `H = -J ╬г ╧Г^z_i ╧Г^z_{i+1} - h ╬г ╧Г^x_i` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_isHermitian` | `H` is Hermitian for real `J`, `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot x y` | the Ising bond operator `╧Г^z_x ┬╖ ╧Г^z_y` (generic in `╬Ы`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot_isHermitian` | each bond operator is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric (J : ╬Ы тЖТ ╬Ы тЖТ тДВ) (h : тДВ)` | **graph-friendly** Ising Hamiltonian `╬г J(x,y) ╧Г^z_x ╧Г^z_y тИТ h ╬г ╧Г^x_x` for any finite `╬Ы` and pairwise coupling `J`; specialises to chain / graph / lattice via the choice of `J` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric_isHermitian` | Hermitian for entry-wise real `J` and real `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph G J h` | graph wrapper: `isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf G J) h`; double-sum convention matches Heisenberg-on-graph | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` | Hermitian for real `J, h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph G ╬▓ J h` | Gibbs state of the graph-built Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity (real `J, h`) and commute with the Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.sum_pathGraph_forward` / `sum_pathGraph_backward` / `sum_pathGraph_adj` | sum-decomposition helpers for `pathGraph (N+1)` adjacency: `╬г_{x,y}` over ordered adjacent pairs = `╬г_{i:Fin N} (f i.cs i.s + f i.s i.cs)` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `pathGraphParityColoring` / `pathGraph_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of `pathGraph (N + 1)` (`i тЖж i.val % 2`) and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Underpins the N├йel state (`Quantum/NeelState.lean`) and the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki ┬з2.5) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `cycleGraphEvenParityColoring` / `cycleGraph_even_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of the even cycle `cycleGraph (2 * K + 2)` and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Wrap-around case `(2K+1) + 1 тЙб 0` still flips parity because the cycle length is even (odd cycles are not bipartite) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_eq_isingHamiltonianGeneric` | **generic-N bridge**: `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h = isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J/2)) h`. The proof itself is the robust regression test | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

### Testing infrastructure

| Lean name / location | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `LatticeSystem/Tests/Sanity.lean` | sanity-check `example` blocks for concrete small-N instances (Pauli arithmetic, spin-1/2 ladder actions, Heisenberg / Ising Hermiticity on small chains, graph-centric bridge identities) |
| decide-based property tests (in `Tests/Sanity.lean`) | universally-quantified properties verified by `decide` on small finite types (graph adjacency symmetry / irreflexivity / connectivity on `pathGraph n` and `cycleGraph n` for small `n`); real proofs, no `sorry` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState ╬▓ J h N` | `gibbsState ╬▓ (quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h)` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_isHermitian` | the Ising-chain Gibbs state `╧Б_╬▓` is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `[╧Б_╬▓, H_Ising] = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_zero` | infinite-temperature closed form `тЯиAтЯй_0 = (1/dim) ┬╖ Tr A` (independent of `J, h`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_im_of_isHermitian` | for Hermitian `O`, `(тЯиOтЯй_╬▓).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_hamiltonian` | conservation `тЯи[H_Ising, A]тЯй_╬▓ = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(тЯиH_IsingтЯй_╬▓).im = 0` (energy expectation is real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_im` | for Hermitian `O`, `(тЯиH_Ising ┬╖ OтЯй_╬▓).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_sq_im` | `(тЯиH_Ising^2тЯй_╬▓).im = 0` (energy-squared expectation real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_pow_im` | `(тЯиH_Ising^nтЯй_╬▓).im = 0` for any `n : тДХ` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_anticommutator_im` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(тЯиA┬╖B + B┬╖AтЯй_╬▓).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_re` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(тЯиA┬╖B тИТ B┬╖AтЯй_╬▓).re = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsHamiltonianVariance_im` | `(Var_╬▓(H_Ising)).im = 0` (energy variance real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsing_partitionFn_im` | `(partitionFn ╬▓ H_Ising).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq` | for Hermitian `O`, `((тЯиOтЯй_╬▓).re : тДВ) = тЯиOтЯй_╬▓` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(╧Б_╬▓^n) = Z(n╬▓) / Z(╬▓)^n` for the Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_self_eq` | `тЯиH_IsingтЯй_╬▓ = -J ┬╖ тИС тЯи╧Г^z ╧Г^zтЯй_╬▓ + (-h) ┬╖ тИС тЯи╧Г^xтЯй_╬▓` (energy as bond + transverse-field decomposition) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

### Gibbs state (Tasaki ┬з3.3)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬з3.3.

All theorems in this module are fully proved with **zero `sorry`**.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `gibbsExp ╬▓ H` | `exp(-╬▓H) := Matrix.exp (-╬▓ тАв H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isHermitian` | `exp(-╬▓H)` is Hermitian (when `H` is Hermitian) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_zero` | `exp(-0┬╖H) = 1` (Tasaki ┬з3.3, pp. 75тАУ78) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add` | `exp(-(╬▓тВБ+╬▓тВВ)H) = exp(-╬▓тВБH) ┬╖ exp(-╬▓тВВH)` (one-parameter group) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add_of_commute_hamiltonians` | `exp(-╬▓(HтВБ+HтВВ)) = exp(-╬▓HтВБ) ┬╖ exp(-╬▓HтВВ)` for commuting `HтВБ, HтВВ` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_neg_mul_self` | `exp(╬▓H) ┬╖ exp(-╬▓H) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_self_mul_neg` | `exp(-╬▓H) ┬╖ exp(╬▓H) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isUnit` | `exp(-╬▓H)` is invertible | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_ne_zero` | `exp(-╬▓H) тЙа 0` (corollary of `gibbsExp_isUnit`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_ne_zero` | `╧Б_╬▓ тЙа 0` when `Z(╬▓) тЙа 0` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_inv` | `(╧Б_╬▓)тБ╗┬╣ = Z(╬▓) ┬╖ e^{╬▓H}` when `Z(╬▓) тЙа 0` (general matrix inverse, generalises `gibbsState_zero_inv`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_smul_gibbsState_eq_gibbsExp` | `Z(╬▓) ┬╖ ╧Б_╬▓ = e^{-╬▓H}` when `Z(╬▓) тЙа 0` (canonical rescaled identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_mul_gibbsExpectation_eq` | `Z(╬▓) ┬╖ тЯиAтЯй_╬▓ = Tr(e^{-╬▓H} ┬╖ A)` when `Z(╬▓) тЙа 0` (canonical unnormalised expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_natCast_mul` | `exp(-(n┬╖╬▓)H) = (exp(-╬▓H))^n` for `n : тДХ` (exact discrete semigroup identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_two_mul` | `exp(-(2╬▓)H) = exp(-╬▓H) ┬╖ exp(-╬▓H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_inv` | `(exp(-╬▓H))тБ╗┬╣ = exp(╬▓H)` (matrix inverse made explicit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_intCast_mul` | `exp(-(n┬╖╬▓)H) = (exp(-╬▓H))^n` for `n : тДд` (integer-power extension) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn ╬▓ H` | `Z := Matrix.trace (exp(-╬▓H))` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_zero` | `Z(0) = Fintype.card (╬Ы тЖТ Fin 2)` (dimension of the Hilbert space) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_zero_ne_zero` | `Z(0) тЙа 0` (concrete sorry-free proof that the partition function is nonzero at ╬▓ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | for any Hermitian `A : Matrix n n тДВ`, `A.trace.im = 0` (generic helper; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4344) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `partitionFn_im_of_isHermitian` | for Hermitian `H`, `(partitionFn ╬▓ H).im = 0` (Z is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_mul_self_trace` | `Tr(╧Б_╬▓┬▓) = Z(2╬▓) / Z(╬▓)┬▓` (purity / R├йnyi-2 entropy precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(╧Б_╬▓^n) = Z(n╬▓) / Z(╬▓)^n` for any `n : тДХ` (R├йnyi-n entropy precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_zero` | `╧Б_0 = (1/dim) ┬╖ I` (maximally mixed state at infinite temperature) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_zero_inv` | `╧Б_0тБ╗┬╣ = dim ┬╖ I` (matrix inverse at ╬▓ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_zero` | `тЯиAтЯй_0 = (1/dim) ┬╖ Tr A` (high-temperature limit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState ╬▓ H` | `╧Б := (1/Z) тАв exp(-╬▓H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_trace` | `Tr(╧Б) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_isHermitian` | `╧Б` is Hermitian | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation ╬▓ H O` | `тЯиOтЯй := Matrix.trace (╧Б * O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_one` | `тЯи1тЯй = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_add` | `тЯиOтВБ + OтВВтЯй = тЯиOтВБтЯй + тЯиOтВВтЯй` (linearity in observable) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_smul` | `тЯиc ┬╖ OтЯй = c ┬╖ тЯиOтЯй` (scalar linearity, `c : тДВ`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_neg` | `тЯи-OтЯй = -тЯиOтЯй` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sub` | `тЯиA - BтЯй = тЯиAтЯй - тЯиBтЯй` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sum` | `тЯитИС i тИИ s, f iтЯй = тИС i тИИ s, тЯиf iтЯй` (finite-sum linearity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_commute_hamiltonian` | `[exp(-╬▓H), H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬з3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `[╧Б_╬▓, H] = 0`, i.e. `╧Б_╬▓` is stationary under the dynamics generated by `H` (Tasaki ┬з3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `star (Tr(A ┬╖ B)) = Tr(A ┬╖ B)` for Hermitian `A, B : Matrix n n тДВ` (algebraic core, Gibbs-independent; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4344) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_star_of_isHermitian` | `star тЯиOтЯй_╬▓ = тЯиOтЯй_╬▓` for Hermitian `H`, `O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_im_of_isHermitian` | `(тЯиOтЯй_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H`, `O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq_of_isHermitian` | `((тЯиOтЯй_╬▓).re : тДВ) = тЯиOтЯй_╬▓` for Hermitian `H`, `O` (real-cast equality) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_comm` | `тЯиH ┬╖ AтЯй_╬▓ = тЯиA ┬╖ HтЯй_╬▓` for any `A` (Tasaki ┬з3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_comm_of_commute_hamiltonian` | for any conserved `A` (`[A, H] = 0`), `тЯиA ┬╖ OтЯй_╬▓ = тЯиO ┬╖ AтЯй_╬▓` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_eq_zero_of_commute_hamiltonian` | for any conserved `A`, `тЯиA ┬╖ O тИТ O ┬╖ AтЯй_╬▓ = 0` (selection rule) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_hamiltonian` | `тЯи[H, A]тЯй_╬▓ = 0` (conservation law) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(тЯиHтЯй_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H` (real energy expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sq_im_of_isHermitian` | `(тЯиO ┬╖ OтЯй_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` (second-moment realness, variance precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_pow_im_of_isHermitian` | `(тЯиO^nтЯй_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O`, any `n : тДХ` (all natural-power moments real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance ╬▓ H O` | `Var_╬▓(O) := тЯиO ┬╖ OтЯй_╬▓ тИТ тЯиOтЯй_╬▓┬▓` (canonical-ensemble variance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_eq` | unfolding lemma for `gibbsVariance` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Var_╬▓(O)).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` (variance is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_zero` | at ╬▓ = 0, `Var_0(O) = (1/dim) ┬╖ Tr(O┬▓) тИТ ((1/dim) ┬╖ Tr O)┬▓` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_eq_centered_sq` | `Var_╬▓(O) = тЯи(O тИТ тЯиOтЯй_╬▓ ┬╖ 1) ┬╖ (O тИТ тЯиOтЯй_╬▓ ┬╖ 1)тЯй_╬▓` (centered-square form, `Z тЙа 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance ╬▓ H A B` | `Cov_╬▓(A, B) := тЯиA ┬╖ BтЯй_╬▓ тИТ тЯиAтЯй_╬▓ ┬╖ тЯиBтЯй_╬▓` (canonical-ensemble complex covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq` | unfolding lemma for `gibbsCovariance` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_self_eq_variance` | `Cov_╬▓(O, O) = Var_╬▓(O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_sub_swap_eq_commutator` | `Cov_╬▓(A, B) тИТ Cov_╬▓(B, A) = тЯиA ┬╖ B тИТ B ┬╖ AтЯй_╬▓` (antisymmetric part = commutator expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_add_left` | `Cov_╬▓(AтВБ + AтВВ, B) = Cov_╬▓(AтВБ, B) + Cov_╬▓(AтВВ, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_add_right` | `Cov_╬▓(A, BтВБ + BтВВ) = Cov_╬▓(A, BтВБ) + Cov_╬▓(A, BтВВ)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_smul_left` | `Cov_╬▓(c тАв A, B) = c ┬╖ Cov_╬▓(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_smul_right` | `Cov_╬▓(A, c тАв B) = c ┬╖ Cov_╬▓(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_const_smul_one_left/right_eq_zero` | `Cov_╬▓(c тАв 1, B) = 0` and `Cov_╬▓(A, c тАв 1) = 0` (when `Z тЙа 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_const_smul_one_left/right_eq_zero` | `Cov^s_╬▓(c тАв 1, B) = 0` and `Cov^s_╬▓(A, c тАв 1) = 0` (when `Z тЙа 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm ╬▓ H A B` | `Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) := (1/2) ┬╖ тЯиA ┬╖ B + B ┬╖ AтЯй_╬▓ тИТ тЯиAтЯй_╬▓ ┬╖ тЯиBтЯй_╬▓` (symmetric / real-valued covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_self_eq_variance` | `Cov^s_╬▓(O, O) = Var_╬▓(O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Cov^s_╬▓(A, B)).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, A, B` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_comm` | `Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) = Cov^s_╬▓(B, A)` (symmetric in observables) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_add_left/right` | additivity of `Cov^s_╬▓` in each argument | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_smul_left/right` | scalar pull-out from each argument of `Cov^s_╬▓` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_add` | `Var_╬▓(A + B) = Var_╬▓(A) + Var_╬▓(B) + 2 ┬╖ Cov^s_╬▓(A, B)` (sum-of-observables variance identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_one` | `Var_╬▓(1) = 0` (when `Z тЙа 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_smul` | `Var_╬▓(c тАв A) = c┬▓ ┬╖ Var_╬▓(A)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_smul_one` | `Var_╬▓(c тАв 1) = 0` (when `Z тЙа 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_neg` | `Var_╬▓(тИТA) = Var_╬▓(A)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_add_const_smul_one` | `Var_╬▓(A + c тАв 1) = Var_╬▓(A)` (when `Z тЙа 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq_symm_add_half_commutator` | `Cov_╬▓(A, B) = Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) + (1/2) ┬╖ тЯиA ┬╖ B тИТ B ┬╖ AтЯй_╬▓` (symmetric / antisymmetric decomposition) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_eq_half_add_swap` | `Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) = (1/2) ┬╖ (Cov_╬▓(A, B) + Cov_╬▓(B, A))` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq_symm_of_commute` | for commuting `A, B`, `Cov_╬▓(A, B) = Cov^s_╬▓(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_conjTranspose_swap_of_isHermitian` | `star Tr(╧Б ┬╖ X) = Tr(╧Б ┬╖ Xс┤┤)` for Hermitian `╧Б` (generic helper) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_star_swap_of_isHermitian` | `star тЯиA ┬╖ BтЯй_╬▓ = тЯиB ┬╖ AтЯй_╬▓` for Hermitian `H, A, B` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_anticommutator_im` | `(тЯиA┬╖B + B┬╖AтЯй_╬▓).im = 0` (anticommutator is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_re` | `(тЯиA┬╖B тИТ B┬╖AтЯй_╬▓).re = 0` (commutator is purely imaginary) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_im` | `(тЯиH ┬╖ OтЯй_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

### Heisenberg chain (Tasaki ┬з3.5)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬з3.5, p. 89.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf G J` | the canonical pairwise coupling `╬Ы тЖТ ╬Ы тЖТ тДВ` of a `SimpleGraph G` with uniform edge weight `J`: returns `J` on edges of `G`, zero otherwise (graph-centric bridge) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf_symm` / `_self` / `_real` | symmetry (from `G.Adj` symmetry), vanishing on the diagonal (from irreflexivity), and reality (for real edge weight) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.pathGraph_adj_iff` / `cycleGraph_adj_iff` | path / cycle graph adjacency in the explicit `x.val + 1 = y.val тИи ...` form used elsewhere in the codebase | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+1) тЖТ Fin (N+1) тЖТ тДВ`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds, zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+2) тЖТ Fin (N+2) тЖТ тДВ`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds (mod N+2), zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_eq_couplingOf` | the open-chain coupling is `couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainCoupling_eq_couplingOf` | the periodic-chain coupling is `couplingOf (cycleGraph (N+2)) (-J)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_isHermitian_of_real_symm` | for any real symmetric coupling `J` the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `H = ╬г_{x,y} J(x,y) ┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y` is Hermitian | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_couplingOf_isHermitian` | **graph-centric** Hermiticity: for any `SimpleGraph G` and real edge weight `J : тДВ`, the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `heisenbergHamiltonian (couplingOf G J)` is Hermitian. The chain instances are corollaries via the bridge theorems | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraph G J` | named wrapper = `heisenbergHamiltonian (couplingOf G J)` (parallel to `isingHamiltonianOnGraph`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` / `_commute_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` / `_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | corollaries re-exposed under the named wrapper | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergGibbsState_eq_onGraph` / `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsState_eq_onGraph` | rfl bridges: chain Gibbs = graph Gibbs on pathGraph/cycleGraph | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_eq_isingGibbsStateOnGraph` | chain Ising Gibbs = `isingGibbsStateOnGraph (pathGraph (N+1)) ╬▓ (-J/2) h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingCycleGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | the periodic Ising Gibbs state commutes with the periodic Ising Hamiltonian (free corollary of `gibbsState_commute_hamiltonian`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingCycleGibbsExpectation_zero` / `_im_of_isHermitian` / `_commutator_hamiltonian` / `_hamiltonian_im` / `_hamiltonian_pow_im` / `isingCycle_partitionFn_im` / `_ofReal_re_eq` / `isingCycleGibbsState_pow_trace` | periodic-Ising expectation companions of the open-chain `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation*` family: ╬▓ = 0 closed form, real-valuedness for Hermitian observables, conservation `тЯи[H, A]тЯй = 0`, energy / energy-power expectations real, partition-function real, real-cast `((тЯиOтЯй_╬▓).re : тДВ) = тЯиOтЯй_╬▓`, R├йnyi-n trace `Tr(╧Б_╬▓^n) = Z(n╬▓) / Z(╬▓)^n` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph N ╬▓ G J U` | Gibbs state of the graph-built Hubbard Hamiltonian | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity / commute corollaries | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardChainGibbsState_eq_onGraph` | rfl bridge: `hubbardChainGibbsState = hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J) U` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwStringAbstract i` | Jordan-Wigner string for any `[Fintype ╬Ы] [LinearOrder ╬Ы]` тАФ product of `╧Г^z_j` over `j < i`; generalises the Fin-specific `jwString` | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `jwStringAbstract_isHermitian` / `jwStringAbstract_sq` / `jwStringAbstract_commute_onSite` | basic structural identities | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilationAbstract i` / `fermionCreationAbstract i` / `fermionNumberAbstract i` | abstract-╬Ы annihilation / creation / number; rfl-bridges to the Fin-specific versions | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilationAbstract_conjTranspose` / `fermionCreationAbstract_conjTranspose` / `fermionNumberAbstract_isHermitian` | adjoint relations and Hermiticity in the abstract form | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilationAbstract_sq` / `fermionCreationAbstract_sq` | `c_i┬▓ = 0`, `c_iтАа┬▓ = 0` (Pauli exclusion) | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnticommAbstract_self` / `fermionNumberAbstract_sq` | `{c_i, c_iтАа} = 1` and `n_i┬▓ = n_i` (abstract same-site CAR + idempotency) | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.boxProd_decidableAdj` | `DecidableRel (G тЦб H).Adj` for the box product (mathlib provides only the bare definition); enables 2D / nD lattices via `couplingOf` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerChainGraph` | the **infinite** one-dimensional chain `тДд` as a `SimpleGraph` (= `hasse тДд`), the infinite-volume analogue of `pathGraph (N+1)` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerChainGraph_adj_iff` | adjacency: `a ~ b тЖФ b = a + 1 тИи a = b + 1` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerSquareLatticeGraph` | the **infinite** 2D square lattice on `тДд ├Ч тДд` as `integerChainGraph тЦб integerChainGraph`; infinite analogue of `squareLatticeCoupling` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerSquareLatticeGraph_adj_iff` | adjacency: nearest neighbour in one coordinate, equal in the other | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `squareLatticeCoupling N J`, `squareLatticeHeisenberg_isHermitian` | the 2D open-boundary square lattice on `Fin (N+1) ├Ч Fin (N+1)` realised as `couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1) тЦб pathGraph (N+1)) (-J)`; Hermiticity is a one-line corollary of the graph-generic theorem above | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `squareTorusCoupling N J`, `squareTorusHeisenberg_isHermitian` | the 2D periodic square lattice (discrete torus) on `Fin (N+2) ├Ч Fin (N+2)` realised as `couplingOf (cycleGraph (N+2) тЦб cycleGraph (N+2)) (-J)`; Hermiticity is a one-line corollary | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `cubicLatticeCoupling N J`, `cubicLatticeHeisenberg_isHermitian` | the 3D open-boundary cubic lattice on `Fin (N+1)^3` realised as `couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1) тЦб pathGraph (N+1) тЦб pathGraph (N+1)) (-J)`; Hermiticity is a one-line corollary | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `squareLatticeHeisenbergGibbsState` / `_isHermitian` / `_commute_hamiltonian` | Gibbs state of the 2D open-boundary square-lattice Heisenberg Hamiltonian + Hermiticity + commute pair | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `squareTorusHeisenbergGibbsState` / `_isHermitian` / `_commute_hamiltonian` | Gibbs state of the 2D torus Heisenberg Hamiltonian + companions | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `cubicLatticeHeisenbergGibbsState` / `_isHermitian` / `_commute_hamiltonian` | Gibbs state of the 3D open-boundary cubic-lattice Heisenberg Hamiltonian + companions | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `squareLatticeHeisenbergGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` / `squareLatticeHeisenberg_partitionFn_im` | 2D open square-lattice Heisenberg energy expectation real / partition function real | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |

| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_eq` | for `N = 1` (the 2-site open chain on `Fin 2`), `H_open = -2J ┬╖ spinHalfDot 0 1` (explicit one-bond reduction; Tasaki ┬з2.4 simplest concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=1) ┬╖ |тЖСтЖСтЯй = -(J/2) ┬╖ |тЖСтЖСтЯй` тАФ `|тЖСтЖСтЯй` lies in the `S = 1` triplet sector and is an exact eigenvector with eigenvalue `-J/2` (this is the ferromagnetic ground state for `J < 0`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_singlet` | `H_open(N=1) ┬╖ (|тЖСтЖУтЯй - |тЖУтЖСтЯй) = (3J/2) ┬╖ (|тЖСтЖУтЯй - |тЖУтЖСтЯй)` тАФ singlet eigenvalue, the antiferromagnetic ground state for `J > 0` (Tasaki ┬з2.5 concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `H_open(N=1) ┬╖ |тЖУтЖУтЯй = -(J/2) ┬╖ |тЖУтЖУтЯй` тАФ all-down state has the same eigenvalue as all-up (both are `S = 1` triplet states) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_triplet_zero` | `H_open(N=1) ┬╖ (|тЖСтЖУтЯй + |тЖУтЖСтЯй) = -(J/2) ┬╖ (|тЖСтЖУтЯй + |тЖУтЖСтЯй)` тАФ triplet `m = 0` state, completing the 3-fold degenerate triplet representation `S = 1` with eigenvalue `-J/2` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_eq` | for `N = 2` (the 3-site open chain on `Fin 3`, 2 bonds), `H_open = -2J ┬╖ (spinHalfDot 0 1 + spinHalfDot 1 2)` тАФ explicit two-bond reduction | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=2) ┬╖ |тЖСтЖСтЖСтЯй = -J ┬╖ |тЖСтЖСтЖСтЯй` тАФ confirming the linear scaling `E(|тЖС..тЖСтЯй) = -N┬╖J/2` (here `N = 2` bonds, `J = 1` per bond) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_sum_eq` | for any `N : тДХ`, `╬г_{x,y тИИ Fin (N+1)} openChainCoupling N J x y = -(2N ┬╖ J : тДВ)` (the bond-counting lemma: each of the `N` unordered nearest-neighbour bonds is counted in both orientations) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | for any `N : тДХ`, `J : тДЭ`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H_open ┬╖ |s..sтЯй = -(N┬╖J/2 : тДВ) ┬╖ |s..sтЯй` тАФ both `s = 0` (all-up) and `s = 1` (all-down) share the same eigenvalue by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `s = 0` specialisation of the above (Tasaki ┬з2.4 (2.4.5)/(2.4.1) ferromagnetic ground-state energy `E_GS = -|B|┬╖S┬▓` for `S = 1/2`, `|B| = N` bonds) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `s = 1` specialisation: same eigenvalue `-(N┬╖J/2)` for the all-down state by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_all_up` | for any `N : тДХ`, `J : тДЭ`, `k : тДХ`, `H_open ┬╖ ((┼Ьtot^-)^k ┬╖ |тЖС..тЖСтЯй) = -(N┬╖J/2 : тДВ) ┬╖ ((┼Ьtot^-)^k ┬╖ |тЖС..тЖСтЯй)` тАФ the unnormalised Tasaki ┬з2.4 (2.4.9) ferromagnetic ground states `|╬ж_MтЯй` made explicit on the chain (combines PRs #82 + #98) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_all_down` | dual ladder from `|тЖУ..тЖУтЯй`, same eigenvalue `-(N┬╖J/2)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_open` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_periodic` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |

### Perron-Frobenius theorem (`Math/PerronFrobenius.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean`, `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean`)

Perron-Frobenius theorem for nonneg irreducible/primitive matrices (Issue #405, closed).
The sorry in `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` is eliminated via the Collatz-Wielandt port (PRs AтАУC).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.IsPrimitive.of_irreducible_pos_diagonal` | irreducible nonneg + positive diagonal тЖТ primitive (Seneta ┬з1.1, Prop. 1.3, p. 17) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.collatzWielandtFn` | CW function `min_{i\|x_i>0} (Ax)_i/x_i` (Seneta ┬з1.2, p. 27) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_any_ratio` | `CW(x) тЙд (Ax)_i/x_i` for `x_i > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_mulVec` | fundamental inequality `CW(x)┬╖x тЙд Ax` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.smul_eq` | scale invariance `CW(cx) = CW(x)` for `c > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.upperSemicontinuousOn` | CW is upper-semicontinuous on stdSimplex | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.exists_maximizer` | CW attains its max on stdSimplex (EVT for USC, Seneta ┬з1.2, p. 28) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.eq_eigenvalue` | `CW(v) = r` when `Av = r┬╖v`, `v > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.lt_of_all_ratios_gt` | all ratios `> c` тЯ╣ `CW(x) > c` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.pos_of_nonneg_eigenvec` | irreducible nonneg + `Av = ╬╝v`, `v тЙе 0`, `v тЙа 0` тЯ╣ `v > 0` (standard propagation argument) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_primitive` | primitive nonneg тЯ╣ тИГ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ┬з1.2, pp. 27тАУ28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_irreducible` | irreducible nonneg тЯ╣ тИГ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ┬з1.2, pp. 27тАУ28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `exists_nonneg_eigenvec_max` | (**sorry**, retained for docs) symmetric nonneg max eigenvalue has nonneg eigenvector | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` | (**sorry-free**) irreducible nonneg Hermitian тЯ╣ max eigenvalue has strictly positive eigenvector | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `pos_eigenvec_unique` | strictly positive eigenvector unique up to positive scalar | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `PerronFrobenius.eigenvec_proportional_of_pos_eigenvec` | **geometric simplicity of the Perron eigenvalue**: irreducible nonneg with a strictly positive eigenvector `v` at `╬╝` тЯ╣ every real eigenvector at `╬╝` is `s тАв v` for some scalar `s` (the `╬╝`-eigenspace is 1-dimensional). Perturbation trick: `v + t тАв u > 0` for small `t > 0` is an eigenvector at `╬╝`, hence `тИЭ v` by `pos_eigenvec_unique`, forcing `u тИЭ v`. Used to show a Perron ground state is a joint eigenvector of any commuting operator (e.g. the total Casimir) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusSimple.lean` |

References: E. Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains* (3rd ed.), Springer 2006, ┬з1.2 (pp. 27тАУ28);
or4nge19/MCMC: `MCMC/PF/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/PerronFrobenius/`.

### Spin-`S` MarshallтАУLiebтАУMattis on the magnetization sector (Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.2 generic S, sector form)

> **Route correction (2026-05-26).** The earlier "saturated-ladder-iterate"
> route toward `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` was found **unsound**: it reduced the
> theorem to a leaf hypothesis (lowerable attach-sum dominance) that is **false**
> тАФ the ferromagnetic ladder iterate `(┼ЬтБ╗_tot)^M|all-upтЯй` is *not* the
> Marshall-positive antiferromagnetic ground state (concrete counterexample:
> spin-1/2, `V={a,b}`, `A={a}`, `B={b}`, `M=0тЖТ1`). The 40 conditional-wrapper
> modules of that route were removed; their detailed rows below were deleted with
> them. The **sound** route keeps the per-sector PerronтАУFrobenius ground state
> (`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence`, sorry-free) and chains sectors by
> SU(2) invariance `[─д, ┼ЬтБ╗_tot] = 0`; some rows below still reference helper
> lemmas from the removed route pending re-documentation. See
> `.self-local/docs/tasaki-2-5-pf-route-design.md` and Issue #3542.

Generic-spin (`N = 2S`) version of Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.2 applied to the
**magnetization-`M` sector** of the un-dressed antiferromagnetic
Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a bipartite graph. The sector subtype
`magConfigS V N M := { ╧Г : V тЖТ Fin (N + 1) // magSumS ╧Г = M }` is the
natural index type since the dressed Heisenberg matrix is irreducible
on each sector. The core sector-matrix API lives in
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSector.lean`; the trailing
eigenvalue-uniqueness + complex-form MLM Theorem 2.2 statements
were moved to the companion
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSectorEigenvalueUnique.lean` in
refactor #30 (PR #2829). Tracked in Issue #412.

| Lean name | Statement |
|---|---|
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (lowering)**: if a magnetization-`M` sector vector `magSectorEmbedding ╬ж` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at energy `╬╝` and a total-Casimir eigenvector at `╬│` away from the sector's lowering-kernel value `(card┬╖N/2тИТM)(card┬╖N/2тИТMтИТ1)`, then `┼ЬтБ╗_tot ┬╖ magSectorEmbedding ╬ж` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at the **same** `╬╝` (via `[─д, ┼ЬтБ╗_tot]=0`), is non-zero (total-Casimir non-vanishing criterion), and lies in the next magnetization sector. This is the energy-preserving step that chains the per-sector PerronтАУFrobenius ground states (`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence`) across the admissible range; it uses **no** Marshall positivity of the lowered vector (the false hypothesis of the removed ferromagnetic-ladder route). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred` / `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred_of_mem_predictedGS` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (raising)** and its predicted-GS specialisation: the raising companions of `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ(_of_mem_predictedGS)`. `┼ЬтБ║_tot` maps a sector-`K` Heisenberg eigenvector (total-Casimir away from the raising-kernel value `(card┬╖N/2тИТK)(card┬╖N/2тИТK+1)`) to a non-zero same-energy eigenvector in the previous sector; predicted-GS membership in sector `M+1` strictly above the left endpoint discharges the Casimir hypotheses via `tasaki23_predictedCasimirValue_ne_raising_kernel_value_of_mem_of_left_lt`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMagSubspaceS` / `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS` (with `mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_iff`, `basisVecS_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`, `sublatticeMagSubspaceS_disjoint`) | **Sublattice magnetization grading**: the `┼Ь_A^(3)`-eigenspaces `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` and the diagonal eigenvalue `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ╧Г = тИС_{xтИИA}(N/2тИТ╧Г_x)`. Submodule API (zero/add/smul/neg/sub/finite-sum membership), disjointness of distinct eigenvalues, and basis-state membership. Sublattice analogue of `magSubspaceS`/`magEigenvalueS`; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 5) for the sublattice highest-weight argument bounding `(┼Ь_A)┬▓`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagnetization.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_sublattice_casimir_re_le` | **Sublattice Casimir bounds for the toy ground state**: the per-sector Marshall-positive toy GS is a joint Casimir eigenvector (#3657) whose `(┼Ь_A)┬▓`/`(┼Ь_┬мA)┬▓` eigenvalues `╬│_A, ╬│_B` obey `╬│_A.re тЙд s_A(s_A+1)`, `╬│_B.re тЙд s_B(s_B+1)` (apply the spectral max bound #3672 to the non-zero Marshall-dressed embedding). Discharges two hypotheses of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 3). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToySublatticeBounds.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_extremal_sector_magnetization_re_eq_predicted` | **Extremal sector realizes the predicted total spin**: for the left-endpoint sector `M = min(|A|,|┬мA|)┬╖N`, the magnetization eigenvalue `|V|┬╖N/2 тИТ M` has real part `= tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin A N = |s_A тИТ s_B|`. Supplies the extremal-sector hypothesis `hM` of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 4). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ExtremalSector.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_of_energy_le` | **Toy ground state is the predicted-Casimir witness (modulo the energy bound)**: in the extremal sector `M = min(|A|,|┬мA|)┬╖N`, the Marshall-positive toy GS with toy energy `╬╝ тЙд predicted тИТ s_A(s_A+1) тИТ s_B(s_B+1)` is a `(┼Ь_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at exactly `tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue A N`. Capstone assembling the joint eigenvector (#3657), sublattice bounds (#3677), toy energy formula (#3673), extremal sector (#3678), the two-sided pin (#3676), and Hermitian eigenvalue realness тАФ exactly the witness form required by #3656. The single remaining obligation is the reference energy bound `h╬╝` (Issue #3674, PR 5). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyWitness.lean`) |

The total spin dominates its `z`-projection: raising with `┼ЬтБ║_tot` only increases the magnetization, so the highest weight `M'` reached has `M' тЙе M`, giving `╬│ = M'(M'+1) тЙе M(M+1)`. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_of_sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_eq_zero` | **Sublattice highest-weight Casimir relation**: if `┼Ь_A^(3) w = m_A┬╖w` and `┼Ь_A^+ w = 0`, then `(┼Ь_A)┬▓ w = m_A(m_A+1)┬╖w`. From the sublattice Cartan identity `┼Ь_A^- ┼Ь_A^+ = (┼Ь_A^1)┬▓+(┼Ь_A^2)┬▓тИТ┼Ь_A^(3)` (so `(┼Ь_A)┬▓ = ┼Ь_A^-┼Ь_A^+ + ┼Ь_A^(3) + (┼Ь_A^(3))┬▓`); on a highest-weight `┼Ь_A^(3)`-eigenvector the `┼Ь_A^-┼Ь_A^+` term vanishes. Sublattice analogue of the total highest-weight relation; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 4) for the sublattice Casimir spectral max bound. At `m_A = тИТs_A` this gives `(┼Ь_A)┬▓ = s_A(s_A+1)` (max), so the all-down sublattice state realizes the maximal `(┼Ь_A)┬▓` тАФ a building block of the minimal-total-spin state (Issue #3674). The `su(2)` ladder identity for the `A`-sublattice on the all-up state, from the sublattice Cartan `┼Ь_A^+ ┼Ь_A^- = (┼Ь_A)┬▓ тИТ (┼Ь_A^(3))┬▓ + ┼Ь_A^(3)`, the maximal Casimir (#3691) and the iterate's magnetization `s_A тИТ k` (#3692). Inductive (sublattice analogue of ┬з2.4's total non-vanishing): if `v_{k+1}=0` then `┼Ь_A^+ v_{k+1} = (k+1)(|A|┬╖NтИТk) v_k = 0` (the ladder identity #3693), and the scalar is non-zero for `k < |A|┬╖N`, forcing `v_k = 0` against the IH. The spanning family of `W` (one per `(A,┬мA)`-magnetization pair) (Issue #3687, PR 11). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/JointLadderIterate.lean`) |

Within a fixed total magnetization the joint iterates are distinguished by their `A`-magnetization; the dimension input for the rankтАУnullity argument (Issue #3687, PR 12). | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eigenvalue_re_nonneg` | **The sublattice Casimir is positive semidefinite** (option (a) ingredient): any `(┼Ь_A)┬▓`-eigenvalue `╬│` (non-zero eigenvector) has `0 тЙд ╬│.re`. Since `(┼Ь_A)┬▓ = ┼Ь_A^(1)┬▓ + ┼Ь_A^(2)┬▓ + ┼Ь_A^(3)┬▓` is a sum of squares of Hermitian operators, `тЯиv, (┼Ь_A)┬▓ vтЯй = ╬г_╬▒ тАЦ┼Ь_A^(╬▒) vтАЦ┬▓ тЙе 0`. This lets the Casimir eigenvalue be written as `a(a+1)` with a *real* sublattice spin `a тЙе 0`, the form the coupled total-spin lower bound consumes (Issue #3542). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23SublatticeCasimirNonneg.lean`) |
| `toy_joint_eigenvector_energy_re_ge` | **Toy minimum-energy bound `─д_toy тЙе E`** (option (a) тАФ coupled bound #3723 + arithmetic #3716): a joint `(┼Ь_tot)┬▓`/`(┼Ь_A)┬▓`/`(┼Ь_┬мA)┬▓`-eigenvector `╧И` (eigenvalues `╬│_tot, ╬│_A, ╬│_B`) in a magnetization level (with `|┬мA| тЙд |A|`) has toy energy `(╬│_tot тИТ ╬│_A тИТ ╬│_B).re тЙе E = (s_AтИТs_B)(s_AтИТs_B+1) тИТ s_A(s_A+1) тИТ s_B(s_B+1)`. Writing the (non-negative #3724, `тЙд s(s+1)` #3672) sublattice Casimir eigenvalues as `a(a+1)`, `b(b+1)` (real spins via the square root), the coupled bound (#3723) gives `╬│_tot.re тЙе |aтИТb|(|aтИТb|+1)`, hence `(╬│_totтИТ╬│_AтИТ╬│_B).re тЙе |aтИТb|(|aтИТb|+1) тИТ a(a+1) тИТ b(b+1) = f(a,b) тЙе f(s_A,s_B) = E` (#3716). This is the interior-sector toy minimum-energy bound (pins `r_min(M) = E` with the predicted-energy witness #3715) (Issue #3542). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyMinEnergyBound.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_min_energy_arith_eq` | **Strict (equality) arithmetic core** (option (a)): if `f(a,b) = f(s_A,s_B)` on the box `0 тЙд a тЙд s_A`, `0 тЙд b тЙд s_B тЙд s_A` with `s_B > 0`, then `(a,b) = (s_A,s_B)` (the minimiser of `f(a,b) = |aтИТb|(|aтИТb|+1) тИТ a(a+1) тИТ b(b+1)` is the corner). When the toy energy attains its minimum `E` the toy-minimum-energy chain is tight, so this pins the toy ground state's sublattice spins to the maximal `(s_A,s_B)`, hence its total Casimir to the predicted value (Issue #3542). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyMinEnergyArithEq.lean`) |
| `toy_joint_eigenvector_totalCasimir_eq_predicted` (with `tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue_eq_sub`) | **A minimal-energy joint eigenvector has the predicted total Casimir** (option (a), interior-sector culmination): for `|┬мA| тЙд |A|`, `s_B > 0`, a joint `(┼Ь_tot)┬▓`/`(┼Ь_A)┬▓`/`(┼Ь_┬мA)┬▓`-eigenvector `╧И` (eigenvalues `╬│_tot, ╬│_A, ╬│_B`) whose toy energy `(╬│_tot тИТ ╬│_A тИТ ╬│_B).re тЙд E` (the predicted minimum) satisfies `╬│_tot = tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue A N`. The toy minimum-energy bound (#3725) forces `(╬│_tot тИТ ╬│_A тИТ ╬│_B).re = E`; the coupled bound + equality arithmetic (#3727) force the sublattice spins to the corner `(s_A,s_B)`, whence `╬│_tot.re = (s_AтИТs_B)(s_AтИТs_B+1) = tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue`. Each sector's ground state is lifted to a full-space `H`-eigenvector and shown to carry the predicted total Casimir (`tasaki23_sector_lift_and_casimir`, via #3731); the adjacent-sector Casimir constancy (`tasaki23_pf_sector_energy_eq_of_casimir`, #3713) then equates neighbouring energies (`tasaki23_common_energy_step`), and `Nat.le_induction` along `[min┬╖N, max┬╖N]` propagates the base-sector energy to the whole interval (`tasaki23_common_groundEnergy`). | `tasaki23_eigenvalue_ge_common` | **Global minimality of the common energy тАФ sector minтАУmax engine** (option (a), TIER 5, PR #3733): given the common energy `╬╝` realised by a Marshall-positive ground state in every admissible sector (`hcommon`, #3732) and a lower bound `hOutside` for the non-admissible sectors, every non-zero full-space eigenvector of `heisenbergHamiltonianS J N` at energy `╬╝'` satisfies `╬╝ тЙд ╬╝'`. Proof: a non-zero eigenvector has a non-zero magnetisation-sector restriction (`eq_sum_magSectorEmbedding_magSectorRestriction`), which is a sector eigenvector at `╬╝'` (`тАж_magSectorRestriction_of_full_eigen`); its non-zero real or imaginary part (`тАж_re/_im_of_complex_eigenvec`) is a real sector eigenvector; the CollatzтАУWielandt per-sector bound (`тАж_eigenvalue_ge_of_marshallPositive`) closes the admissible case, `hOutside` the non-admissible case. The non-admissible bound `hOutside` is the LiebтАУMattis energy monotonicity `E(S_tot)` beyond the band, isolated as an explicit hypothesis (Issue #3542). | `totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_normSq_eq`, `totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_ne_zero_of_pos_weight`, `totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_ne_zero_of_neg_weight` | **One total-spin ladder step is non-vanishing on a weight vector** (general-`J` `hOutside` thread, step 1, PR #3736): from `[┼ЬтБ║_tot, ┼ЬтБ╗_tot] = 2 ┼Ь┬│_tot` (the two Casimir rearrangements) and `(┼ЬтБ╗)тАа = ┼ЬтБ║`, a weight-`w` vector `╬ж` (`┼Ь┬│_tot ╬ж = w ╬ж`) satisfies `тАЦ┼ЬтБ╗_tot ╬жтАЦ┬▓ = тАЦ┼ЬтБ║_tot ╬жтАЦ┬▓ + 2 w тАЦ╬жтАЦ┬▓`. Hence `┼ЬтБ╗_tot ╬ж тЙа 0` when `╬ж тЙа 0` and `w.re > 0`, and dually `┼ЬтБ║_tot ╬ж тЙа 0` when `w.re < 0`. This is the SU(2) non-vanishing input for the inward-ladder discharge of the non-admissible-sector lower bound: an eigenvector outside the band can be moved to the band edge by `┼ЬтИУ_tot` without annihilation (Issue #3542). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42; LiebтАУMattis, J. Math. Phys. 3 (1962) 749 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23TotalLoweringNonvanishing.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_admissible_eigenvector_ge`, `raise_iterate_ne_zero`, `lower_iterate_ne_zero`, `tasaki23_general_hOutside` | **General-`J` non-admissible-sector lower bound via the SU(2) inward ladder** (general-`J` `hOutside` thread, steps 2тАУ3, PR #3737): discharges, for an ARBITRARY connected bipartite antiferromagnetic coupling `J`, the `hOutside` hypothesis of the global-minimality engine (#3733) тАФ every dressed-sector eigenvalue `╬╝M` in a non-admissible sector `M` is `тЙе` the admissible common energy `╬╝`. A non-admissible eigenvector is lifted to the full Hilbert space, then moved to the admissible band edge by iterating `┼ЬтБ║_tot` (if `M` is above the band) or `┼ЬтБ╗_tot` (if below): each step commutes with `H` (energy preserved, `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_totalSpinSOp{Plus,Minus}_mulVec`) and is non-zero (#3736, intermediate weights have the right sign), with weight shifts (`totalSpinSOp{Plus,Minus}_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_of_mem`). The landed non-zero eigenvector lies in an admissible sector, where the CollatzтАУWielandt per-sector bound `tasaki23_admissible_eigenvector_ge` (the reused admissible core of the engine) gives `╬╝ тЙд ╬╝M`. | `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` / `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` | **Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3 truly-unconditional capstone for the bipartite toy coupling** (#3887 extension chain, Thm23-#3887.12тАУ.21, PR #3891): the canonical `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` API is now the structural statement, dropping the vacuous-at-`N=1` `h_intermediate` hypothesis (`тИА ╧Д, тИА x, тИГ z, A z тЙа A x тИз (╧Д z).val < N`; unsatisfiable when `╧Д тЙб Fin.last 1`). The toy capstone `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` proves the conclusion from `(1 тЙд N, 1 тЙд |A|, 1 тЙд |┬мA|)`, the genuine physical hypotheses, by chaining structural variants of the entire MLM / sector existence / Casimir / lower-bound stack: complex sector Marshall eigenvector (#3887.12), full-Hilbert lift (#3887.13), four-level Marshall-positive uniqueness chain (#3887.14), Theorem 2.2 bundled full (#3887.15), Theorem 2.3 sector existence wrapper (#3887.16), toy sector energy lower bound (#3887.17), per-sector toy ground state at predicted (#3887.19), final capstone (#3887.21). At spin-1/2 (N=1) this is the **first truly-unconditional Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3 closure**. The already-`h_intermediate`-free pieces (`tasaki23_eigenvalue_ge_common`, `tasaki23_toy_sector_groundEnergy_le_of_witness`, `exists_predictedEnergy_sector_eigenvector_of_mem`) are reused directly. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42; LiebтАУMattis, J. Math. Phys. 3 (1962) 749 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23StructuralBipartiteToy.lean`) |
| `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_bipartiteCompletePositive` | **Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3 truly-unconditional capstone for general bipartite J** (PR #3893, Issue #3892): the canonical general-`J` closure proves `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3 A N J c` for ANY real symmetric non-negative bipartite coupling `J` positive on the complete bipartite graph at any `N тЙе 1`, under canonical orientation `|┬мA| тЙд |A|` with `s_B > 0`. This removes BOTH the toy-coupling restriction of PR #3891 and the vacuous-at-N=1 `h_intermediate` restriction of the older #3738 capstone. At spin-1/2 (N=1) this is the **first truly-unconditional Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3 closure for general bipartite J**. Chain: structural sector lift + Casimir (Step 1), structural adjacent-sector common-energy step (Step 2), structural common ground-state energy (Step 3, TIER 4 constancy), capstone (Step 4) combining Step 3 with PR #3891's `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence` and the already-`h_intermediate`-free `tasaki23_general_hOutside` + `tasaki23_eigenvalue_ge_common` + `heisenbergHamiltonianSReMatrixOnMagSector_eigenvalue_ge_of_marshallPositive`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42; LiebтАУMattis, J. Math. Phys. 3 (1962) 749 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23StructuralGeneralFinal.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_unique_groundState_has_zero_magnetization` | **Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4 partial conclusion: unique GS has zero `┼Ь┬│_tot` magnetization** (Issue #3739, PR #3896): if the anisotropic Heisenberg ground-state eigenspace has `finrank тДВ тЙд 1`, every non-zero ground state `╬ж` satisfies `┼Ь┬│_tot ╬ж = 0`. Proof: ╬ж, ┼Ь┬│_tot ╬ж, ╬Ш ╬ж all lie in the GS eigenspace (`H` commutes with `┼Ь┬│_tot` (#3741) and with the reflection `╬Ш` (#3745)). From `finrank тЙд 1` + ╬ж тЙа 0 and `Module.finrank_le_one_iff`, `┼Ь┬│_tot ╬ж = ╬│ ╬ж` and `╬Ш ╬ж = ╬┤ ╬ж` for some scalars. `╬Ш┬▓ = 1` тЯ╣ `╬┤┬▓ = 1` тЯ╣ `╬┤ тЙа 0`. Apply the anticommutation `╬Ш ┼Ь┬│_tot = -┼Ь┬│_tot ╬Ш` (#3744) to ╬ж: get `╬┤╬│ тАв ╬ж = -╬│╬┤ тАв ╬ж` тЯ╣ `2╬┤╬│ тАв ╬ж = 0` тЯ╣ `╬┤╬│ = 0` тЯ╣ `╬│ = 0` (since ╬┤ тЙа 0). This is the second conclusion of Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4 once uniqueness is established (uniqueness itself awaits the deformation argument from the SU(2) point, separate forthcoming work). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem24ZeroMagnetizationFromUniqueness.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_at_SU2_eigenspace_eq_heisenbergHamiltonianS` / `heisenbergHamiltonianS_unique_groundState_has_zero_magnetization` | **Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4 SU(2) base case via Theorem 2.3 reduction** (Issue #3739, PR #3897): at the SU(2) point `(╬╗=1, D=0)`, the anisotropic Hamiltonian reduces to the isotropic Heisenberg Hamiltonian (via `anisotropicHeisenbergS_one_zero`), so every eigenspace coincides. The first lemma gives the direct eigenspace identification; the second specializes PR #3896 to the SU(2) point, giving `┼Ь┬│_tot|╬жтЯй=0` for any non-zero GS of the isotropic Heisenberg Hamiltonian under the GS uniqueness hypothesis. Prepares the SU(2) base case for the deformation argument: once uniqueness is established at the SU(2) point (for symmetric `|A|=|┬мA|` via Theorem 2.2 + SU(2) singlet structure, separate forthcoming work), the `┼Ь┬│_tot|╬жтЯй=0` conclusion follows immediately. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem24SU2BaseCase.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_apply_eq_zero_of_magSumS_ne` | **Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4 sector-crossing matrix entries vanish** (Issue #3739, PR #3898): the matrix entry `anisotropicHeisenbergS J ╬╗ D N ╧Г ╧Д = 0` whenever `magSumS ╧Г тЙа magSumS ╧Д` тАФ the matrix-element form of U(1) invariance `[H, ┼Ь┬│_tot] = 0` (#3741). Proof: evaluate `H ┬╖ ┼Ь┬│_tot = ┼Ь┬│_tot ┬╖ H` at `(╧Г, ╧Д)`; since `┼Ь┬│_tot` is diagonal with entries `magEigenvalueS`, get `H ╧Г ╧Д ┬╖ M_╧Д = M_╧Г ┬╖ H ╧Г ╧Д`. When `magSumS ╧Г тЙа magSumS ╧Д`, Nat-cast injectivity gives `magEigenvalueS ╧Г тЙа magEigenvalueS ╧Д`, so `H ╧Г ╧Д = 0`. Key building block for sector-decomposition arguments toward Theorem 2.4 obligation (2.a) uniqueness. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSectorZero.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_magSectorProjection_eigen` | **Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4 sector projection of eigenvector is eigenvector** (Issue #3739, PR #3899): for an eigenvector `v` of the anisotropic Hamiltonian at `╬╝`, the sector projection `magSectorEmbedding (magSectorRestriction (M:=M) v)` is also an eigenvector at the same `╬╝`. Vector-level analog of PR #3898: only same-sector entries survive in the `mulVec` expansion (cross-sector entries vanish by U(1) invariance). This enables sector-by-sector spectral decomposition of any anisotropic H-eigenvector, key building block toward Theorem 2.4 obligation (2.a) uniqueness via the sector-decomposition + reflection argument. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicSectorProjectionEigenvector.lean`) |
| `linearIndependent_pair_of_magSubspaceS_distinct` | **Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4 distinct-sector pair LI** (Issue #3739, PR #3900): non-zero vectors in distinct magnetization sectors `MтВБ тЙа MтВВ` are linearly independent. Direct consequence of `magSubspaceS_disjoint` (existing): for `a тАв ╬жтВБ + b тАв ╬жтВВ = 0`, get `a тАв ╬жтВБ тИИ magSubspaceS ╬Ы N MтВБ тИй magSubspaceS ╬Ы N MтВВ = тКе`, so `a тАв ╬жтВБ = 0`, giving `a = 0` (since ╬жтВБ тЙа 0), and similarly `b = 0`. Key technical lemma for the sector-decomposition + reflection argument toward Theorem 2.4 obligation (2.a): non-zero vectors in three pairwise distinct sectors form a 3-LI family, contradicting obligation (1)'s `finrank тЙд 2`. | `linearIndependent_triple_of_magSubspaceS_distinct` | **Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4 distinct-triple-sector LI** (Issue #3739, PR #3901): three non-zero vectors in three pairwise distinct magnetization sectors `MтВБ тЙа MтВВ`, `MтВБ тЙа MтВГ`, `MтВВ тЙа MтВГ` are linearly independent. Direct application of mathlib's `Module.End.eigenvectors_linearIndependent'` to `Matrix.toLin' (totalSpinSOp3 ╬Ы N)` viewed as a linear endomorphism тАФ `magSubspaceS = End.eigenspace` of `totalSpinSOp3`. This is the contradiction lever for the SU(2) symmetric `finrank тЙд 1` argument: admissible-sector GS + non-admissible GS + reflected non-admissible GS = 3-LI family contradicting obligation (1) `finrank тЙд 2`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/MagSubspaceDistinctTripleLI.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_threeLI_of_admis_and_nonadmis` | **Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4 three-LI from admissible + non-admissible eigenvectors** (Issue #3739, PR #3902): for an admissible-sector eigenvector ╬ж (at `┼Ь┬│_tot = 0`) and a non-admissible-sector eigenvector ╬и (at `┼Ь┬│_tot = M' тЙа 0`) at the same energy, the family `{╬ж, ╬и, ╬Ш ╬и}` is linearly independent, where `╬Ш ╬и тИИ magSubspaceS ╬Ы N (-M')` is the reflected non-admissible eigenvector. Combines PR #3901's triple-LI with: (i) `manyBodyReversalS_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_neg` (reflection shifts sector M тЖТ -M); (ii) `╬Ш┬▓ = 1` giving `╬Ш ╬и тЙа 0` from `╬и тЙа 0`; (iii) `M' тЙа 0` тЯ╣ `0 тЙа M'`, `0 тЙа -M'`, `M' тЙа -M'` (the latter via `2M' = 0` тЯ╣ `M' = 0`). The contradiction lever for the SU(2) symmetric `finrank тЙд 1` argument: this 3-LI family combined with obligation (1) `finrank тЙд 2` forces no non-admissible-sector eigenstate at the GS energy. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem24ThreeLIFromNonAdmis.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_finrank_le_two_no_admis_plus_nonadmis` | **Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4 finrank тЙд 2 + admissible + non-admissible тЖТ False** (Issue #3739, PR #3903): if the anisotropic Hamiltonian eigenspace at `╬╝` has `finrank тЙд 2` AND contains both an admissible-sector eigenvector ╬ж (at `┼Ь┬│_tot = 0`) and a non-admissible-sector eigenvector ╬и (at `┼Ь┬│_tot = M' тЙа 0`), then `False`. Proof: PR #3902 gives `{╬ж, ╬и, ╬Ш ╬и}` linearly independent; `╬Ш ╬и` is also at energy ╬╝ via ╬Ш commutes with H (#3745); the 3-LI family lifts to the eigenspace `тЖеE` via `LinearIndependent.of_comp E.subtype`; `LinearIndependent.fintype_card_le_finrank` gives `3 тЙд finrank тДВ тЖеE`, contradicting `finrank тЙд 2` via `omega`. This is the direct contradiction in the SU(2) symmetric `finrank тЙд 1` argument. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem24FinrankLeTwoContradiction.lean`) |

If non-zero, PR #3903 derives False; hence the projection is `0`. This is the `M тЖФ тИТM` magnetization reflection used in the MattisтАУNishimori uniqueness argument (`E_M = E_{тИТM}` and, with the ground state in `H_0`, `┼Ь┬│_tot ╬ж_GS = 0`). Hence `─д'` is block-diagonal w.r.t. the even/odd magnetization parity, the decomposition on which the parity-sector PerronтАУFrobenius degeneracy bound runs (PR5). Proved by lifting the bond (#3771) and single-ion (`SingleIonOffDiag`) parity vanishing through Finset parity bookkeeping (agree-off тЯ╣ global parity = local parity). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AxisSwapParityBlock.lean`) |

Applied to `─д'` and the parity operator `P` (#3773), this bounds any `─д'`-eigenspace degeneracy by the sum of its even/odd-parity contributions тАФ reducing the `тЙд 2` ground-state bound to `тЙд 1` per parity block. | `parityReachableS_drainSetInto` | **Multi-site concentration via iterated drain** (Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3803): for a finite set `S` of sites disjoint from `aтВА` and adjacent to `b` (and `aтВА` adjacent to `b`), starting from `╧Г` with `(╧Г aтВА).val + тИС_{sтИИS} (╧Г s).val тЙд N` and `(╧Г b).val + 1 тЙд N`, the config that drains every `s тИИ S` (setting `╧Г' s = 0`) into `aтВА` (setting `╧Г' aтВА = (╧Г aтВА).val + тИС_{sтИИS} (╧Г s).val`) is `ParityReachableS`-reachable.  Proven by `Finset.induction_on` generalizing `╧Г` over `S`, composing single drains (#3802).  The multi-site concentration primitive for (d) reachability totality.  Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43 (file `Quantum/SpinS/ParityReachConcentrate.lean`) |
| `parityReachableS_concentrate_A_at_a0` | **A-side concentration at `aтВА`** (Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3804): starting from `╧Г` with `aтВА тИИ A` and a B-site `b тИЙ A`, the config that drains every A-site `a тЙа aтВА` into `aтВА` is `ParityReachableS`-reachable.  Specialises #3803 with `S = aSitesExcept A aтВА = Finset.univ.filter (fun v => A v = true тИз v тЙа aтВА)`.  Hypotheses: `A aтВА`, `┬м A b`, `(╧Г aтВА).val + тИС_{s тИИ aSitesExcept A aтВА} (╧Г s).val тЙд N`, `(╧Г b).val + 1 тЙд N`. | `parityReachableS_concentrate_B_at_b0` | **B-side concentration at `bтВА`** (Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3805): symmetric to #3804 with A/B swapped. Hypotheses: `┬м A bтВА`, `A a`, `(╧Г bтВА).val + тИС_{s тИИ bSitesExcept A bтВА} (╧Г s).val тЙд N`, `(╧Г a).val + 1 тЙд N`. | `parityReachableS_concentrate_AB` | **Combined A+B concentration to a two-site canonical form** (Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3806): composing #3804 (A-concentration via `bтВА` as temp) with #3805 (B-concentration via the post-A-concentration `aтВА тИИ A` as temp).  The resulting `╧Г_AB` has `╧Г_AB aтВА = (╧Г aтВА).val + ╬г_{aSitesExcept} (╧Г s).val` (total A-mass), `╧Г_AB bтВА = (╧Г bтВА).val + ╬г_{bSitesExcept} (╧Г s).val` (total B-mass), and `╧Г_AB = 0` on `aSitesExcept тИк bSitesExcept`. | `matrix_similar_eigenspace_inter_finrank_eq` | **(g.3) similarity-invariant intersection eigenspace finrank** (Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3835): generalises `matrix_similar_eigenspace_finrank_eq` (#3746 family) to intersections with a second operator's eigenspace, provided the similarity `U` commutes with that operator `Q`. Given `U * Uinv = 1`, `Uinv * U = 1`, `H' = Uinv * H * U`, AND `Q * U = U * Q`, then `finrank тДВ (eig H' ╬╝ тКУ ker(Q=c)) = finrank тДВ (eig H ╬╝ тКУ ker(Q=c))`. The core technical step for transferring per-parity-block PF bounds from the dressed `─д'_dressed` to the bare `─д'`, since the Marshall sign diagonal `╬Ш_A` commutes with the diagonal `magParityDiagS P`. Key observation: the Marshall sign diagonal `╬Ш_A` and the magnetization parity diagonal `P = magParityDiagS` are both diagonal and hence commute, so the intersection `eig ─д' ╬╝ тКУ ker(P=c)` is preserved by the Marshall similarity dressed тЖТ bare. Result for `p < 2`: `тИГ ╬╜ : тДЭ, finrank тДВ (eig (axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS J lam D N) (╬╜ : тДВ) тКУ ker(P=(-1)^p)) тЙд 1`. General `N` version requires an explicit `AxisSwapUnitaryS N` instance; spin-1/2 (`N = 1`) instance via `axisSwapUnitarySpinHalf`. This **closes the bare `─д` тЙд 2 link in the (g) chain**, conditional on per-parity-block PF hypotheses (discharged separately per sector by #3836). The remaining gap to unconditional obligation (1) is the ╬╜-sector matching (PF eigenvalues of even/odd sectors coinciding at the ground-state energy), which is mathematically distinct (Hermitian spectral minimisation) and deferred. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43тАУ44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BareAnisotropicEigLeTwoConditional.lean`) |
| `parity_block_submatrix_full_inter_finrank_eq` (with `parity_block_submatrix_eigenspace_finrank_le_full_inter`, `parityEmbedMap`) | **(h.1) reverse block-diag bridge** (Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3840): for a parity-block-diagonal complex `M`, the parity-`p` submatrix's ╬╜-eigenspace embeds INJECTIVELY into `eig M ╬╜ тКУ ker(P=(-1)^p)` via the zero-extension map (extend by 0 off `parityConfigS p`). Combined with the forward direction (#3832), gives the finrank equality `finrank тДВ (eig M.submatrix ╬╜) = finrank тДВ (eig M ╬╜ тКУ ker(P=(-1)^p))` for `p < 2`. The equality unlocks ╬╜-sector matching arguments: per-block submatrix eig finrank equals the full eig тКУ ker(P) finrank, so per-sector PF simplicity (#3834/#3836) directly transfers to constraints on the FULL eigenspace at that energy. | `eigenspace_finrank_eq_of_commuting_involution` | **(h.2) involution eigenspace decomposition тАФ finrank EQUALITY** (Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3841): strengthens `eigenspace_finrank_le_of_commuting_involution` (#3776) from `тЙд` to `=`. For finite-dim `T, P` with `TтИШP = PтИШT` and `PтИШP = id`, the `T`-eigenspace at any `╬╝` splits as a **direct sum** across the two `P`-eigenspaces `┬▒1`. The `тЙд` was tight because the two intersected `┬▒1` eigenspaces of `P` are disjoint (a single eigenvector cannot have two distinct `P`-eigenvalues), so `Submodule.finrank_sup_add_finrank_inf_eq` collapses to a sum (inf = 0). Used by (h.3) to derive the block-sum equality. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43тАУ44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/InvolutionEigenspaceDecompEq.lean`) |
| `matrix_eigenspace_finrank_eq_sum_parity_blocks` (with `dressedAxisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_..._sum_parity_blocks`, `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_..._sum_parity_blocks`) | **(h.3) block-sum finrank equality** (Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3842): combines (h.1) #3840 and (h.2) #3841 to give the block-sum equality for any parity-block-diagonal `M` commuting with `magParityDiagS`: `finrank тДВ (eig M ╬╝) = finrank тДВ (eig M.submatrix_0 ╬╝) + finrank тДВ (eig M.submatrix_1 ╬╝)`. Specialised to the dressed `─д'_dressed` and the bare `─д'` (both block-diagonal and commuting with `P`). Central tool for unconditional `тЙд 2` at any ╬╝ where both per-block bounds hold simultaneously. Combined with PF (per-sector simplicity at the per-sector spectrum minimum) and a Hermitian spectral identification of the GS energy as `min(╬╜_0, ╬╜_1)`, gives the unconditional bare `─д'` GS degeneracy `тЙд 2`. Given per-block bounds `finrank тЙд 1` at the per-block minimum eigenvalues, the full-eigenspace at `min(min_0, min_1)` has `finrank тЙд 2`. Case analysis: `min_0 = min_1` both contribute `тЙд 1`; `min_0 тЙа min_1` the higher block contributes `0` by (i.4) #3849, the lower `тЙд 1`. Bridge from per-block PF simplicity to the unconditional global ground-state degeneracy `тЙд 2` (modulo identification of PF eigenvalue with `hermitianMinEigenvalue`). General `N` version requires an `AxisSwapUnitaryS N` instance; spin-1/2 (`N = 1`) instance via `axisSwapUnitarySpinHalf`. **Analogue of (g.5) #3837 with per-block-min hypotheses threading the Hermitian spectral chain тАФ the remaining gap is identification of the per-sector PF eigenvalue with `hermitianMinEigenvalue submatrix_p` (which would discharge h0/h1 to yield the unconditional ground-state degeneracy тЙд 2 for bare anisotropic `─д`).** Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43тАУ44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BareAnisotropicMinLeTwo.lean`) |
| `dressedAxisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergSReMatrixOnParityBlock_pos_eigenvector_exists` | **(j.1) PF positive eigenvector for unshifted dressed-`─д'` submatrix** (Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3857): from PF on the shifted parity-block matrix (`B = c┬╖I тИТ M` for `M = dressed_re.submatrix`), the PF positive eigenvector `v > 0` is also a positive eigenvector of the un-shifted matrix `M` at `╬╜ = c тИТ ╬╝_PF`. The existence half of the identification `╬╜ = hermitianMinEigenvalue M`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43тАУ44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/DressedSubmatrixPFEigenvector.lean`) |

Uses the matrix identity #3831 `(dressed_re.submatrix).map ofReal = dressed_complex.submatrix`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43тАУ44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/ComplexDressedSubmatrixPFEigenvector.lean`) |

Proof: contrapositive of (i.4) #3849. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43тАУ44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/HermitianMinLeOfEigenvector.lean`) |

Antimonotonicity of `(c тИТ ┬╖)` then gives `max = c тИТ min`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43тАУ44 (file `Math/HermitianSpectrumShift.lean`) |

Fix: in the same-sublattice over/under hard case there is an *alternative path* requiring `╧Г z > 0` instead of `╧Г z < N`; at `N тЙе 1` every `(╧Г z).val` satisfies at least one of the two. Structural branching variant drops `h_intermediate` entirely, requiring only `hA_ne + hB_ne + 1 тЙд N`. | `axisSwapUnitarySSpinS_anisotropic_axisSwapped_eigenspace_finrank_eq` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_unconditional_general` | **General spin-S axis-swap discharge for Theorem 2.4 obligation (1)** (Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): the concrete `╧А/2` rotation about spin-axis 1, `axisSwapUnitarySSpinS N`, now instantiates the earlier `AxisSwapUnitaryS N` interface for every spin parameter `N`. The PR exposes no-argument wrappers for the axis-swap eigenspace-dimension equality, the conditional parity-block and submatrix-block `тЙд 2` bounds, the min-block `тЙд 2` bound, the Hermitian-minimum equality, and the structural general-N capstone `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_unconditional_general`. This discharges the explicit axis-swap-unitary argument in the strict case-(i) degeneracy chain; the SU(2)-uniqueness target wrappers are handled in the later obligation-(2) row, and Tasaki case (ii) now has conditional, strict-gap, and no-full-finrank bridges recorded in the case-(ii) rows, with the strict-gap derivation still explicit. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43тАУ44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AxisSwapUnitarySSpinS.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_sector_pf` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_sector_pf` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_balanced_sector_pf_at_target` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_eq_full` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_eq_full` | **General spin-S target conditional bridge with balanced-sector PF discharged** (Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): packages the final linear-algebra step after the general spin-S `finrank тЙд 2` degeneracy capstone. If the target full ground eigenspace has `finrank тЙд 2`, the balanced sector attains the full ground energy, and the balanced-sector ground eigenspace has `finrank тЙд 1`, then the full target ground eigenspace has `finrank тЙд 1`; the companion wrapper then gives zero total `┼Ь┬│` magnetization for every non-zero target ground state. The new general spin-S PF wrapper chooses a finite strict diagonal shift and applies the existing anisotropic sector PF/minimum theorem, so the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit balanced-sector PF callback. The direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input is superseded by the strict-gap wrappers in the next row; for case (ii), the conditional, strict-gap, and no-full-finrank bridges are recorded in the case-(ii) rows and the strict-gap derivation remains explicit. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43тАУ44 (files `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSTargetConditionalBridge.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSBalancedSectorPFTarget.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergParametricPath_in_case_ii_region` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_eq_full` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_eq_full` | **General spin-S case-(ii) path and conditional target bridge** (Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): proves that the deformation path from `(1,0)` to a target satisfying `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0` remains in the case-(ii) region on `[0,1]`, then repackages the balanced-sector PF target bridge under case-(ii) hypotheses. These first case-(ii) wrappers keep the remaining target inputs explicit as balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2`; they do not reuse the case-(i) axis-swap sign argument. Equal-`magSumS` pairs leave the Marshall-dressed real entry unchanged, while +/-2 pairs negate it; dressed negativity on transverse moves and dressed positivity on parity-bond or single-ion moves therefore become parity-gauged off-diagonal non-positivity. A diagonal shift bound plus this off-diagonal non-positivity gives entrywise non-negativity of `c тАв 1 - R`, and strict dressed signs transfer to strict shifted entries. The next local case-(ii) task is to prove those dressed signs under the strict local hypotheses (`-1 < lambda` for transverse moves, `1 < lambda` for parity-bond moves, and `D < 0` for single-ion moves), then discharge irreducibility and PF/min identification.
