Since `[─д,(┼Ьtot)┬▓]=0` (`heisenbergHamiltonianS_commute_totalSpinSSquared`), `(┼Ьtot)┬▓ ╬ж` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at the same `╬╝` in the same sector; its real and imaginary parts are real Heisenberg sector eigenvectors at `╬╝`, each a scalar multiple of `╬ж`'s Marshall-positive form by the one-dimensionality `tasaki23_heis_sector_eigenvec_proportional_of_marshallPositive`, so recombining gives `(┼Ьtot)┬▓ ╬ж = ╬│ ╬ж`. This pins the total spin of the ground state (total-spin determination, Tasaki ┬з2.5 p.42). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFCasimirEigenvector.lean`) |

| `tasaki23_lowered_ne_zero_of_marshall_pos` / `totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_magSectorEmbedding_apply_eq_site_sum` / `tasaki23_lowered_marshall_pos_of_site_sum_pos` / `tasaki23_lowering_identifies_adjacent_sector_energy` / `tasaki23_lowering_identifies_adjacent_sector_energy_with_nonzero` / `tasaki23_lowering_identifies_adjacent_sector_energy_of_site_sum_pos` | **Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3 adjacent-sector energy identification, conditional lowering step with non-vanishing and site-sum positivity form**: if an embedded `magSumS = M` source-sector eigenvector has eigenvalue `╬╝`, and its lowered vector `┼Ь^-_tot ╬и_M` satisfies the Marshall-positive hypothesis in the adjacent sector `M + 1`, then strict positivity already implies `┼Ь^-_tot ╬и_M тЙа 0`, and the full-Hilbert-space Theorem 2.2 uniqueness clause in sector `M + 1` identifies the target sector eigenvalue with `╬╝`. The lowered component is also expanded as a sum of the single-site lowering contributions `тИС x, ┼Ь^-_x ╬и_M`; therefore the same package can be invoked from the local site-sum strict positivity hypothesis. This combines non-vanishing from strict Marshall positivity, ladder eigenvalue preservation, the sector-support shift, and the #869 target-sector uniqueness theorem; the remaining critical-path input is to prove the sitewise Marshall-positivity hypothesis for the lowered vector. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (files `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23Local.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalDifferenceMarshall.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalDifferenceEnergy.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalDifferenceEnergyCasimir.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_raised_ne_zero_of_marshall_pos` / `tasaki23_raised_marshall_pos_of_site_sum_pos` / `tasaki23_raising_identifies_adjacent_sector_energy` / `tasaki23_raising_identifies_adjacent_sector_energy_with_nonzero` / `tasaki23_raising_identifies_adjacent_sector_energy_of_site_sum_pos` | **Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3 adjacent-sector energy identification, conditional raising step with non-vanishing and site-sum positivity form**: if an embedded `magSumS = M + 1` source-sector eigenvector has eigenvalue `╬╝`, and its raised vector `┼Ь^+_tot ╬и_{M+1}` satisfies the Marshall-positive hypothesis in the adjacent sector `M`, then strict positivity already implies `┼Ь^+_tot ╬и_{M+1} тЙа 0`, and the full-Hilbert-space Theorem 2.2 uniqueness clause in sector `M` identifies the target sector eigenvalue with `╬╝`. The raised component can also be invoked from the local site-sum strict positivity hypothesis for `тИС x, ┼Ь^+_x ╬и_{M+1}`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (files `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23Local.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalDifferenceMarshall.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalDifferenceEnergy.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalDifferenceEnergyCasimir.lean`) |
| `onSiteS_spinSOpMinus_mulVec_magSectorEmbedding_apply_eq_zero_of_target_zero` / `onSiteS_spinSOpMinus_mulVec_magSectorEmbedding_apply_single_site_pred` / `onSiteS_spinSOpMinus_mulVec_magSectorEmbedding_apply_single_site_pred_re` / `tasaki23_signed_single_site_lowering_component_pos_of_A_false` / `tasaki23_signed_single_site_lowering_component_neg_of_A_true` | **Single-site lowering component formula and local signed contribution split for Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3**: the `x`-summand of `┼Ь^-_tot` at a target sector configuration `╧Д : magConfigS V N (M+1)` is zero when `(╧Д x).val = 0`; when `0 < (╧Д x).val`, it is exactly the `spinSOpMinus` matrix coefficient times the source-sector coefficient at the unique predecessor obtained by replacing `╧Д x` with `(╧Д x).val - 1`, and its real part is the positive lowering coefficient times the predecessor coefficient's real part. Under source-sector Marshall positivity, the signed real contribution is strictly positive for `A x = false` and strictly negative for `A x = true`, matching the single-site Marshall predecessor sign change. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (files `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalLowering.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalLoweringPositivity.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_signed_single_site_lowering_component_nonneg_of_A_false` / `tasaki23_signed_single_site_lowering_component_nonpos_of_A_true` / `tasaki23_signed_lowering_offA_sum_nonneg` / `tasaki23_signed_lowering_onA_sum_nonpos` / `tasaki23_exists_lowerable_offA_of_offA_occupation_pos` / `tasaki23_signed_lowering_offA_sum_pos_of_exists_lowerable_offA` / `tasaki23_signed_lowering_offA_sum_pos_of_offA_occupation_pos` | **Boundary-inclusive lowered local sign bounds and sublattice filtered sums for Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3**: the strict local lowering split is extended across the zero-target boundary. Off `A`, every signed single-site lowering contribution is non-negative; on `A`, every signed single-site lowering contribution is non-positive. Summing over the filtered finite sets gives a non-negative off-`A` signed sum and a non-positive on-`A` signed sum. Positive off-`A` occupation produces an actually lowerable off-`A` witness, and either that witness or the aggregate occupation hypothesis gives strict positivity of the off-`A` filtered sum by `Finset.sum_pos'`. These are decomposition bounds for the remaining strict lowered site-sum positivity proof, not yet the dominance argument for the full sum. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (files `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalLoweringSignSum.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalDifferenceSiteSum.lean`) |
| `tasaki23SignedLoweringSiteContribution` / `tasaki23_signed_lowering_site_sum_eq_offA_add_onA` / `tasaki23_signed_lowering_site_sum_pos_of_onA_neg_lt_offA` / `tasaki23_lowered_marshall_pos_of_onA_neg_lt_offA` | **Lowered site-sum dominance bridge for Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3**: the signed local lowering contribution is named, the full signed lowered site-sum is decomposed exactly into its off-`A` and on-`A` filtered sums, and the pointwise dominance condition `-(onA sum) < offA sum` is packaged as sufficient for the existing strict site-sum positivity and lowered-vector Marshall-positivity hypotheses. This does not prove the dominance inequality itself; it isolates the remaining substantive proof obligation. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (files `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalCoefficient.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalDifferenceSiteSum.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalDifferenceMarshall.lean`) |

Under source-sector Marshall positivity, the signed real contribution is strictly positive for `A x = false` and strictly negative for `A x = true`, matching the same one-site Marshall sign product as the lowering predecessor case. Summing over the filtered finite sets gives a non-negative off-`A` signed sum and a non-positive on-`A` signed sum. Membership in `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N` supplies the total-Casimir identity by `tasaki23_totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_of_mem_bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted`, while the lowered/raised dominance estimate remains an explicit callback. Lowering at a site outside `A` gives the positive lowering matrix coefficient times the Marshall-signed predecessor coefficient, while lowering at a site inside `A` gives its negative. These identities isolate the coefficient relation that the remaining strict lowered-site-sum / lowered-Marshall-positivity proof must sum over the two sublattices. The off-`A` filtered signed lowering sum is the corresponding coefficient sum, while the on-`A` filtered signed lowering sum is the negative of its coefficient sum. This converts the remaining lowered-site-sum positivity problem into a coefficient-level dominance problem. | `tasaki23_lowering_predecessor_signed_coefficient_eq_positive_source` / `tasaki23_lowering_predecessor_signed_coefficient_pos_of_lowerable` / `tasaki23_lowering_predecessor_signed_coefficient_nonneg` | **Positive lowered predecessor coefficients for Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3**: for a Marshall-signed real source vector `╬ж ╧Г = sgn_A(╧Г) * v ╧Г`, the two Marshall signs in `tasaki23LoweringPredecessorSignedCoefficient` cancel. At a lowerable site the coefficient becomes the positive lowering matrix coefficient times `v` at the predecessor, hence is strictly positive when `v > 0`; the boundary-inclusive coefficient is non-negative at every site. This gives the coefficient-sum comparison a sign-normalized local form. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalCoefficient.lean`) |

The signed coefficient sum for the Marshall-signed source vector is rewritten as this positive-source coefficient sum over any finite site set, so dominance of the positive-source sum over `┬мA` against the sum over `A` feeds directly into strict lowered site-sum positivity and Marshall positivity of the lowered vector. This connects the remaining coefficient dominance input to the operator-level sublattice ladder components needed for the predicted-GS/Casimir argument. This is the operator-level input needed for the predicted-GS/Casimir argument. The same identities are also transported to the total-lowering image by predicted-GS lowering closure. This gives the Casimir-side hypotheses needed to prove the sublattice lowering component comparison from the predicted-GS structure. | `sublatticeSpinSDot_eq_op3_add_ladder` / `tasaki23_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_of_mem_bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted` / `tasaki23_two_sublatticeSpinSDot_mulVec_of_mem_bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted` / `tasaki23_two_cross_ladder_mulVec_of_mem_bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted` / `tasaki23_cross_ladder_sum_mulVec_eq_energy_sub_two_op3_of_predictedGS` / `tasaki23_cross_ladder_sequential_mulVec_eq_energy_sub_two_op3_of_predictedGS` / `tasaki23_cross_ladder_raised_lowered_components_eq_energy_sub_two_op3_of_predictedGS` | **Predicted-GS toy cross-ladder bridges for Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3**: predicted toy ground-state membership is also unpacked into the pointwise toy-Hamiltonian eigenvector identity at `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N`. Using `─д_toy_S = 2 тАв ┼Ь_A ┬╖ ┼Ь_┬мA`, this yields the corresponding eigenvector identity for the cross-sublattice spin-dot operator, and `sublatticeSpinSDot_eq_op3_add_ladder` rewrites that dot product as `┼Ь_A^3 ┼Ь_┬мA^3 + (1/2)(┼Ь_A^+ ┼Ь_┬мA^- + ┼Ь_A^- ┼Ь_┬мA^+)`. The isolated ladder-sum corollary moves `┼Ь_A^+ ┼Ь_┬мA^- + ┼Ь_A^- ┼Ь_┬мA^+` to the left-hand side as the predicted toy-energy term minus twice the `┼Ь_A^3 ┼Ь_┬мA^3` contribution, the sequential form rewrites that left side as `┼Ь_A^+(┼Ь_┬мA^- ╬и) + ┼Ь_A^-(┼Ь_┬мA^+ ╬и)`, and the raised-lowered component form commutes the second term to `┼Ь_┬мA^+(┼Ь_A^- ╬и)`. This is the operator bridge needed for comparing the `A` and `┬мA` lowering components. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (files `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpinDot.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23Predicted.lean`) |

This is the component-level bridge needed before proving the local Marshall-signed coefficient comparison from the cross-ladder equation. This states the local comparison in the difference form naturally produced by the real predecessor source-weight identity. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalCoefficientRaisingSourceSum.lean`) |

The outside-sector final wrapper can therefore run through the explicit-lowerable boundary while accepting the named predecessor-difference callback instead of a separate explicit lowerable local-dominance hypothesis. The positivity bridges rewrite the lowerable attached-sum difference from the real source-weight callback to this boundary form and therefore prove the lowered-vector Marshall-positive component; the source-form site-sum bridge also turns the same difference directly into the strict single-site lowered sum callback. The named callback adapter exposes this site-sum form as the API consumed by the new interval chain and final theorem wrapper: predicted-GS membership supplies the re-embedded source-weight identity and lowered sublattice-Casimir/support data at each successor step, the predecessor-difference callback is converted to strict lowered site-sum positivity, and the direct site-sum successor chain finishes the common-energy proof without first converting the boundary to the raising-source dominance final callback. The fully threaded callback shape carries predicted-GS, real source-weight, sublattice-Casimir, and successor-support data for both the lowered-vector Marshall component and the site-sum callback. The final boundary uses this range lemma to replace the interval-specific `magConfigS` non-emptiness callback by a canonical physical-range non-emptiness callback, while keeping the threaded predicted-GS input, local predecessor-difference comparison, and outside-sector ground-representative lower bounds. Internally it now discharges the non-emptiness input and invokes the explicit source common-energy final boundary directly. The resulting full-space admissible-reach callback feeds the lower-family bridge, and the discharged final boundary passes that lower family directly to the source common-energy final boundary. The support lemmas first record that `(┼Ь_A)^2` commutes with `┼Ь_A^-` and with the complement lowering ladder; the Theorem 2.3 bridges then show `┼Ь_A^- ╬ж` and `┼Ь_┬мA^- ╬ж` remain in the corresponding maximum sublattice-Casimir eigenspaces. This supplies the component-level Casimir input for the remaining comparison between the two sublattice lowering pieces. The `A`-lowered component `┼Ь_A^- ╬ж` also remains in the maximum `┬мA`-sublattice Casimir eigenspace, and the `┬мA`-lowered component `┼Ь_┬мA^- ╬ж` also remains in the maximum `A`-sublattice Casimir eigenspace. Thus both individual lowering components lie in the joint maximum sublattice-Casimir eigenspace needed for the remaining component comparison. This records both the Casimir structure and the sector-support fact needed before applying the raised-lowered cross-ladder identity to the remaining component comparison. The callback receives the source predicted-GS membership, the identity applying `┼Ь_A^+` and `┼Ь_┬мA^+` to the two lowered components, and both lowered-component memberships in `tasaki23LoweredJointMagSubspace A N M`. This isolates the remaining comparison under exactly the cross-ladder equation and subspace structure already proved for predicted toy ground states. At each successor step the callback receives the propagated `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N` membership plus the two facts `┼Ь_A^- ╬ж тИИ tasaki23JointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace A N` and `┼Ь_┬мA^- ╬ж тИИ tasaki23JointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace A N`, then returns the strict pointwise Marshall-signed component inequality. The non-vanishing lemmas then prove that the iterated ladder output remains nonzero whenever every intermediate magnetization avoids the corresponding one-step ladder-kernel Casimir value. The complex-form `marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_complexGroundState_full`
is the COMPLEX-Hilbert-space form of Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.2 in the
magnetization sector: the ground state of the un-dressed quantum
Heisenberg Hamiltonian restricted to the sector is unique (up to a
positive real scalar in its real part) and has the Marshall sign
structure `╬ж ╧Г := ((sign A ╧Г.1).re * v ╧Г : тДВ)` with `v > 0`.

`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` (PR #3337) is the **final-statement form**
of the `|A| тЙа |┬мA|` case. The hypothesis bundle matches
`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergHamiltonianS_groundState_full`
(#869) exactly. The conclusion bundles, per admissible sector
`M тИИ tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N`, the Marshall-positive
sector eigenvector existence and within-sector uniqueness from
#869, together with a global energy-minimality clause. The
predicted total spin and predicted degeneracy enter as the
cardinality identity `|tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N| =
tasaki23PredictedDegeneracy A N = 2 S_tot + 1` with
`S_tot = ||A| тИТ |┬мA||┬╖N/2 = tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin A N`. The
proof iterates #869 across `tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N`.

References: H. Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, Springer 2020, ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.2 (pp. 39тАУ43), Theorem 2.3
(p. 42); E. Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains*
(3rd ed.), Springer 2006, ┬з1.2 (pp. 27тАУ28) for the underlying
PerronтАУFrobenius theorem.

### Spin-`S` saturated ferromagnetic state (Tasaki ┬з2.4 generalised)

Generic-spin (`N = 2S`) version of Tasaki ┬з2.4 P1i for the
**saturated ferromagnet**: the all-aligned (constant-spin) basis
state `|╧Г_тКдтЯй = тКЧ_x |cтЯй` with `╧Г x = c` for all `x : V`. The two
extremal weights `c = 0` (highest weight, "all up") and
`c = Fin.last N` (lowest weight, "all down") are the highest- and
lowest-weight vectors of the `J_tot = |V|┬╖S = |V|┬╖N/2` irreducible
SU(2) representation in the multi-site Hilbert space. Tracked in
Issue #412; assembled in PRs #875тАУ#879. All theorems live in
`Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedState.lean`.

| Lean name | Statement |
|---|---|
| `allAlignedConfigS V N c` | constant configuration `╧Г x = c` (PR #875) |
| `allAlignedStateS V N c` | basis state at constant `c`, equal to `basisVecS (allAlignedConfigS V N c)` (PR #875) |
| `magSumS_allAlignedConfigS` | `magSumS = |V|┬╖c.val` (PR #875) |
| `magEigenvalueS_allAlignedConfigS` | `magEigenvalueS = |V|┬╖N/2 тИТ |V|┬╖c` (PR #875) |
| `totalSpinSOp3_mulVec_allAlignedStateS` | `┼Ь^z_tot ┬╖ |cтЯй = (|V|┬╖N/2 тИТ |V|┬╖c) ┬╖ |cтЯй` for any `c` (PR #875) |
| `magSumS_allAlignedConfigS_zero` | `c = 0` тЯ╣ `magSumS = 0` (PR #875) |
| `magSumS_pos_of_ne_allAlignedConfigS_zero` | the all-up configuration is the **unique** `magSumS = 0` configuration (PR #875) |
| `magSumS_allAlignedConfigS_last` | `c = Fin.last N` тЯ╣ `magSumS = |V|┬╖N` (PR #876) |
| `magSumS_lt_card_mul_of_ne_allAlignedConfigS_last` | the all-down configuration is the unique configuration with `magSumS = |V|┬╖N` (PR #876) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | the **all-up state is a Heisenberg eigenvector for ANY coupling** тАФ magnetization conservation `[H, ┼Ь^z_tot] = 0` + uniqueness of the M=0 configuration (PR #875) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero_eigenvalue` | explicit Heisenberg eigenvalue formula on all-up: `╬г_x J(x,x)┬╖N(N+2)/4 + ╬г_{xтЙаy} J(x,y)┬╖N┬▓/4` (PR #875) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` / `_eigenvalue` | symmetric c = N (all-down) Heisenberg eigenvector + same eigenvalue formula (PR #876) |
| `onSiteS_spinSOpPlus_apply_allAlignedConfigS_zero` | `(onSiteS x ┼Ь^+) ╧Г' ╧Г_тКд = 0` (PR #877) |
| `onSiteS_spinSOpPlus_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | `(onSiteS x ┼Ь^+).mulVec |╧Г_тКдтЯй = 0` (PR #877) |
| `totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **`┼Ь^+_tot ┬╖ |╧Г_тКдтЯй = 0`** (highest-weight annihilation, PR #877) |
| `onSiteS_spinSOpMinus_apply_allAlignedConfigS_last` / `_mulVec_` / `totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` | symmetric lowest-weight annihilation `┼Ь^-_tot ┬╖ |╧Г_тКетЯй = 0` (PR #877) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | the all-up state is a `(┼Ь_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector (PR #878) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_apply_diag_allAlignedConfigS_zero` | explicit Casimir diagonal value `|V|┬╖N(N+2)/4 + (|V|┬▓тИТ|V|)┬╖N┬▓/4` (PR #878) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero_eigenvalue` | **`(┼Ь_tot)┬▓ ┬╖ |╧Г_тКдтЯй = (|V|┬╖N/2)┬╖(|V|┬╖N/2 + 1) ┬╖ |╧Г_тКдтЯй`** тАФ operator-level form of "all-up is the highest-weight vector of the J_tot = |V|┬╖S irreducible SU(2) representation" (PR #878) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` / `_apply_diag_` / `_eigenvalue` | symmetric lowest-weight Casimir eigenvalue (same value) (PR #879) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_commute_totalSpinSOp1` / `_Op2` / `_OpPlus` / `_OpMinus` | `Commute`-form conversions: H commutes with each axis-total operator (PR #881) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_commute_totalSpinSOpMinus_pow` / `_Plus_pow` | iterated power Commute by induction (PR #881) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_totalSpinSOpMinus_pow_allAlignedStateS_zero` | for any k, `(┼Ь^-_tot)^k ┬╖ |╧Г_тКдтЯй` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at the same eigenvalue as `|╧Г_тКдтЯй` (PR #881) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_totalSpinSOpPlus_pow_allAlignedStateS_last` | symmetric for `┼Ь^+_tot` on all-down (PR #881) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_commute_totalSpinSOp1` / `_Op2` / `_OpPlus` / `_OpMinus` / `_OpMinus_pow` / `_OpPlus_pow` | Casimir Commute-form analogues (PR #882) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_totalSpinSOpMinus_pow_allAlignedStateS_zero` | for any k, `(┼Ь^-_tot)^k ┬╖ |╧Г_тКдтЯй` preserves the Casimir eigenvalue `(|V|┬╖N/2)┬╖(|V|┬╖N/2+1)` (PR #882) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_totalSpinSOpPlus_pow_allAlignedStateS_last` | symmetric (PR #882) |
| `totalSpinSOp3_commutator_totalSpinSOpMinus` | multi-site Cartan: `[┼Ь^z_tot, ┼Ь^-_tot] = -┼Ь^-_tot` (PR #883) |
| `totalSpinSOp3_commutator_totalSpinSOpPlus` | multi-site Cartan: `[┼Ь^z_tot, ┼Ь^+_tot] = +┼Ь^+_tot` (PR #883) |
| `magConfigS_zero_eq_allAlignedConfigS` / `magConfigS_card_zero` / `magConfigS_zero_instNonempty` | the `M = 0` magnetization sector contains exactly the all-up configuration `allAlignedConfigS V N 0`; cardinality 1; non-empty (PR #885, file `Quantum/SpinS/MagConfigExtremalCardinality.lean`) |
| `magConfigS_last_eq_allAlignedConfigS` / `magConfigS_card_last` / `magConfigS_last_instNonempty` | symmetric `M = |V|┬╖N` sector contains exactly the all-down configuration `allAlignedConfigS V N (Fin.last N)`; cardinality 1; non-empty (PR #885) |
| `totalSpinSOp3_mulVec_totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | single-step magnetic-quantum-number shift: `┼Ь^z_tot ┬╖ (┼Ь^-_tot ┬╖ |╧Г_тКдтЯй) = (|V|┬╖N/2 тИТ 1) ┬╖ (┼Ь^-_tot ┬╖ |╧Г_тКдтЯй)` тАФ the once-lowered all-up state is an `┼Ь^z_tot` eigenvector with magnetic quantum number `m_max тИТ 1` (PR #886) |
| `totalSpinSOp3_mulVec_totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` | symmetric: `┼Ь^z_tot ┬╖ (┼Ь^+_tot ┬╖ |╧Г_тКетЯй) = (тИТ|V|┬╖N/2 + 1) ┬╖ (┼Ь^+_tot ┬╖ |╧Г_тКетЯй)` (PR #886) |
| `totalSpinSOp3_mulVec_totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_eigenvec` / `_OpPlus_mulVec_eigenvec` | generic single-step shift on any `┼Ь^z_tot` eigenvector: `┼Ь^z_tot ╧И = ╬╗ ╧И` тЗТ `┼Ь^z_tot (┼Ь^тИУ_tot ╧И) = (╬╗ тИУ 1) (┼Ь^тИУ_tot ╧И)`. Proven via the multi-site Cartan rearrangement `┼Ь^z_tot ┬╖ ┼Ь^тИУ_tot = ┼Ь^тИУ_tot ┬╖ ┼Ь^z_tot тИУ ┼Ь^тИУ_tot` lifted to `mulVec` (PR #887) |

If the signed `┼Ь_x^-┼Ь_y^+` real part matches the signed `┼Ь_x^+┼Ь_y^-` real part and the signed longitudinal `┼Ь_x^3┼Ь_y^3` real part is half of it, then signed ladder positivity implies signed dot-product positivity; combined with PR #4071 this gives the Marshall-coefficient cross-sublattice wrapper under those component equalities. The two-site phase-invariant bridge gives `C33 = C22` and `C11 = C22`; the `spinSOp3` bridge identifies `C33` with the existing `twoSpinZZCorrelationS`; the transverse ladder identity gives `C11 + C22 = 1/2(C^{+-} + C^{-+})`; PR #4073 gives equality of the signed ladder real parts, hence `Re(g C33) = 1/2 Re(g C^{+-})`. A sector vector with strictly positive coefficients is zero-extended by `magSectorEmbedding`; off-sector terms in the ladder expectation vanish, on-sector terms are positive-coefficient multiples of the signed matrix entries, and the same axis-phase/eigenspace bridge supplies the signed dot-product positivity conclusion. | `phase_unit_of_unitary_mulVec_eq_smul_of_ne_zero` / `exists_phase_unit_of_finrank_eigenspace_le_one_of_unitary_commute_of_ne_zero` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_tasaki23_balanced_pf_cross` | **Problem 2.5.d balanced Perron-Frobenius endpoint**: extracts the balanced sector PF vector from the Theorem 2.3 common-energy data, rewrites the existing real-sign embedding into the sector-supported Marshall form using `marshallSignS_eq_ofReal_re`, and derives the axis-swap / z-rotation unit-modulus phases without normalizing the vector. The endpoint returns the common Hermitian minimum, the strictly positive balanced coefficients, the non-zero embedded eigenvector, the full eigenspace `finrank тЙд 1` input from the SU(2) endpoint, and the signed cross-sublattice two-spin correlation positivity for that concrete PF vector. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23), with Theorems 2.3-2.4 context, pp. 42-44 (PR #4078, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dBalancedPFEndpoint.lean`) |
| `twoSpinCorrelationS_sign_cases_of_tasaki23_balanced_pf_cross` | **Problem 2.5.d balanced Perron-Frobenius sign cases**: applies the final bipartite-gauge sign conversion to the concrete balanced PF endpoint from PR #4078. The wrapper preserves the Hermitian minimum, strictly positive balanced coefficients, non-zero embedded eigenvector, and full eigenspace `finrank тЙд 1` package, while replacing the signed-positivity conclusion by the four Boolean same-sublattice positive / cross-sublattice negative sign cases for the original two-spin correlation. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23), with Theorems 2.3-2.4 context, pp. 42-44 (PR #4079, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dBalancedPFSignCases.lean`) |
| `twoSpinCorrelationS_re_neg_of_tasaki23_balanced_pf_cross` | **Problem 2.5.d balanced Perron-Frobenius cross sign**: extracts the concrete non-vacuous cross-sublattice conclusion from PR #4079. Under `A x тЙа A y`, the Boolean sign cases collapse to the original two-spin correlation inequality `(twoSpinCorrelationS x y ╬ж).re < 0` for the same balanced PF vector, while preserving the Hermitian minimum, positive coefficients, non-zero eigenvector, and `finrank тЙд 1` package. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23), with Theorems 2.3-2.4 context, pp. 42-44 (PR #4080, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dBalancedPFCrossSign.lean`) |
| `spinSOpPlus_one_eq_spinHalfOpPlus` / `_Minus_` / `_Op1_` / `_Op2_` / `_Op3_` | **spin-`S` тЖФ spin-`1/2` bridge at `N = 1`**: `spinSOp{Plus, Minus, 1, 2, 3} 1 = spinHalfOp{Plus, Minus, 1, 2, 3}` (each is the corresponding half-Pauli matrix) (PRs #922 + #923, file `Quantum/SpinS/SpinHalfSpecialization.lean`) |
| `onSiteS_spinSOp3_mulVec_allAlignedStateS` / `allAlignedStateS_expectation_onSiteS_spinSOp3` / `_sq` / `onSiteS_spinSOp3_sq_mulVec_allAlignedStateS` | **single-site `┼Ь^(3)_x` and `(┼Ь^(3)_x)┬▓` on `|c..cтЯй`**: `┼Ь^(3)_x ┬╖ |c..cтЯй = (N/2 тИТ c.val) ┬╖ |c..cтЯй` and expectation of `(┼Ь^(3)_x)┬▓` is `(N/2 тИТ c.val)┬▓` (PR #925, file `Quantum/SpinS/SingleSiteZExpectation.lean`) |
| `allAlignedStateS_expectation_onSiteS_spinSOp1_sq_add_spinSOp2_sq` | **xy-plane Casimir expectation**: `тЯи((┼Ь^(1)_x)┬▓ + (┼Ь^(2)_x)┬▓) ┬╖ |c..cтЯйтЯй = N(N+2)/4 тИТ (N/2 тИТ c.val)┬▓`. From #920 minus #925; for `c=0` gives `S/2` (PR #926, file `Quantum/SpinS/SingleSiteXYExpectation.lean`) |
| `basisVecS_expectation_onSiteS_spinSOp1` / `_spinSOp2` / `allAlignedStateS_expectation_onSiteS_spinSOp1` / `_spinSOp2` | **transverse mean is zero**: `тЯиbasisVecS ╧Г, ┼Ь^(╬▒)_x ┬╖ basisVecS ╧ГтЯй = 0` for `╬▒ = 1, 2` (transverse operators are purely off-diagonal). Specialised to `|c..cтЯй` (PR #927, file `Quantum/SpinS/SingleSiteTransverseMeanZero.lean`) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_singlet_correlation_full_sum` | **singlet correlation sum vanishes**: for `┼Ь_tot┬▓ ╬ж = 0`, `тИС_{x, y} тЯи╬ж, ┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y ┬╖ ╬жтЯй = 0` (PR #929, file `Quantum/SpinS/SingletCorrelationSum.lean`) |
| `correlation_full_sum_eq_totalSpinSSquared_expectation` / `allAlignedStateS_zero_correlation_full_sum` / `_last_` | **universal correlation = Casimir expectation**: `тИС_{x, y} тЯи╬ж, ┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y ┬╖ ╬жтЯй = тЯи╬ж, ┼Ь_tot┬▓ ┬╖ ╬жтЯй`; specialised to `|╧Г_тКд/тКетЯй` gives `m_max(m_max + 1)` (PR #930, file `Quantum/SpinS/CorrelationSumCasimir.lean`) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvector_correlation_full_sum` / `_normalized` | **eigenvector correlation sum**: for `┼Ь_tot┬▓ ╬ж = ╬╗ тАв ╬ж`, `тИС_{x, y} тЯи╬ж, ┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y ┬╖ ╬жтЯй = ╬╗ ┬╖ тЯи╬ж, ╬жтЯй` (= `╬╗` for normalized) (PR #931, file `Quantum/SpinS/CorrelationEigenvector.lean`) |
| `correlation_diag_sum_eq_full_state_norm` / `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvector_correlation_offdiag_sum` | **off-diagonal correlation sum**: universal diagonal `тИС_x тЯи┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_xтЯй = |V|┬╖S(S+1) ┬╖ тЯи╬ж, ╬жтЯй`; eigenvector off-diagonal `тИС_{x тЙа y} тЯи┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_yтЯй = (╬╗ тИТ |V|┬╖S(S+1)) ┬╖ тЯи╬ж, ╬жтЯй` (PR #933, file `Quantum/SpinS/CorrelationOffDiagonal.lean`) |
| `allAlignedStateS_zero_correlation_offdiag_sum` / `_last_correlation_offdiag_sum` | **explicit off-diagonal value on saturated states**: `тИС_{x тЙа y} тЯи|╧Г_тКд/тКетЯй, ┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y ┬╖ |╧Г_тКд/тКетЯйтЯй = m_max(m_max + 1) тИТ |V|┬╖S(S+1) = N┬▓┬╖|V|┬╖(|V|тИТ1)/4` (PR #934, file `Quantum/SpinS/SaturatedOffDiagonalCorrelation.lean`) |
| `spinSDot_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero_of_ne` | **per-pair eigenvalue**: for `x тЙа y`, `┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y ┬╖ |╧Г_тКдтЯй = (N┬▓/4) ┬╖ |╧Г_тКдтЯй`. Proof via `spinSDot_eq_plus_minus`: ladder annihilations + `(3)(3) тЖТ (N/2)┬▓` (PR #939, file `Quantum/SpinS/SpinSDotAllAlignedZero.lean`) |
| `spinSDot_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last_of_ne` | symmetric raising-side per-pair eigenvalue on `|╧Г_тКетЯй` (PR #940, file `Quantum/SpinS/SpinSDotAllAlignedLast.lean`) |
| `allAlignedStateS_zero_expectation_spinSDot_of_ne` / `_last_expectation_spinSDot_of_ne` | **per-pair correlation**: `тЯи|╧Г_тКд/тКетЯй, ┼Ь_x ┬╖ ┼Ь_y ┬╖ |╧Г_тКд/тКетЯйтЯй = N┬▓/4 = S┬▓` for `x тЙа y` (PR #941, file `Quantum/SpinS/PerPairCorrelationExpectation.lean`) |
| `allAlignedStateS_zero_expectation_heisenbergHamiltonianS` / `_last_expectation_heisenbergHamiltonianS` | **Heisenberg expectation on saturated states**: `тЯи|╧Г_тКдтЯй, H ┬╖ |╧Г_тКдтЯйтЯй = saturatedFerromagnetEigenvalueS J N`; `тЯи|╧Г_тКетЯй, H ┬╖ |╧Г_тКетЯйтЯй = H(╧Г_тКе, ╧Г_тКе)` (PR #943, file `Quantum/SpinS/SaturatedHeisenbergExpectation.lean`) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_uniform_eq_totalSpinSSquared` | `heisenbergHamiltonianS (fun _ _ => 1) N = totalSpinSSquared V N` (uniform-J Heisenberg = total-spin Casimir) (PR #945, file `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergUniformCasimir.lean`) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_diag_allAlignedConfigS_last_eq_zero` | **`H(╧Г_тКе, ╧Г_тКе) = saturatedFerromagnetEigenvalueS J N`**: both extremal H-diagonals equal (via #875/#876 same explicit formula + uniqueness on non-zero eigenvectors) (PR #946, file `Quantum/SpinS/SaturatedHeisenbergSymmetric.lean`) |
| `allAlignedStateS_last_expectation_heisenbergHamiltonianS_eq_saturated` | clean form of `тЯи|╧Г_тКетЯй, H ┬╖ |╧Г_тКетЯйтЯй = saturatedFerromagnetEigenvalueS J N` (combines #943 + #946) (PR #948, file `Quantum/SpinS/SaturatedHeisenbergExpectationClean.lean`) |
| `saturatedFerromagnetEigenvalueS_uniform` | for uniform `J = 1`, `saturatedFerromagnetEigenvalueS = saturatedFerromagnetCasimirEigenvalueS = m_max(m_max + 1)` (PR #949, file `Quantum/SpinS/SaturatedHeisenbergUniformEigenvalue.lean`) |
| `saturatedFerromagnetEigenvalueS_explicit` / `saturatedFerromagnetEigenvalueS_im_zero` / `saturatedFerromagnetEigenvalueS_exists_real` | **explicit form and realness**: `saturatedFerromagnetEigenvalueS J N = тИС_x тИС_y J(x,y) ┬╖ (if x = y then N(N+2)/4 else (N/2)┬▓)`, and real couplings make this saturated energy a real scalar. (PR #951 and Tasaki ┬з2.5 Theorem 2.3 follow-up, file `Quantum/SpinS/SaturatedEigenvalueExplicit.lean`) |
| `explicit_uniform_eq_casimir_eigenvalue` | combinatorial simplification: explicit form at `J = 1` equals `m_max(m_max + 1)` (PR #953, file `Quantum/SpinS/SaturatedExplicitUniformSimp.lean`) |
| `allAlignedStateS_{zero,last}_expectation_heisenbergHamiltonianS_explicit` | explicit H expectation on saturated states: `тЯи|╧Г_тКд/тКетЯй, H ┬╖ |╧Г_тКд/тКетЯйтЯй = тИС_x тИС_y J(x,y) ┬╖ (if x = y then N(N+2)/4 else (N/2)┬▓)` (PR #954, file `Quantum/SpinS/HExpectationExplicit.lean`) |
| `allAlignedConfigS_injective` / `allAlignedStateS_ne_of_ne` | distinct constants give distinct configurations and distinct states for `[Nonempty V]` (PR #956, file `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateDistinct.lean`) |
| `allAlignedConfigS_eigenvalue_injective` / `allAlignedStateS_linearIndependent` | the family `{|c..cтЯй : c тИИ Fin (N+1)}` is `LinearIndependent тДВ` for `[Nonempty V]` via distinct `┼Ь^z_tot` eigenvalues (PR #957, file `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateLI.lean`) |
| `allAlignedStateS_span_finrank` | `Module.finrank тДВ (Submodule.span тДВ (Set.range allAlignedStateS)) = N + 1` for `[Nonempty V]` (PR #959, file `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateSpan.lean`) |
| `allAlignedStateS_inner_of_ne` | all-aligned states at distinct constants are orthogonal (PR #960, file `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateOrthogonal.lean`) |
| `allAlignedStateS_mem_magSubspaceS` | `|c..cтЯй тИИ magSubspaceS V N (|V|┬╖N/2 тИТ |V|┬╖c.val)` for any `c` (PR #962, file `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateMagSubspace.lean`) |

References: H. Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, Springer 2020, ┬з2.4 (pp. 30тАУ37, spin-1/2 case).

### Single-mode fermion (P2 skeleton)

Phase 2 entry point: the canonical anticommutation algebra of a single
fermion mode acting on `тДВ┬▓` with computational basis
`|0тЯй` (vacuum) and `|1тЯй` (occupied).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `fermionAnnihilation` | `c = !![0, 1; 0, 0] = |0тЯйтЯи1|` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionCreation` | `cтАа = !![0, 0; 1, 0] = |1тЯйтЯи0|` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionNumber` | `n = !![0, 0; 0, 1] = |1тЯйтЯи1|` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionNumber_eq_creation_mul_annihilation` | `n = cтАа ┬╖ c` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilation_sq` | `c┬▓ = 0` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionCreation_sq` | `(cтАа)┬▓ = 0` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionAnticomm_self` | `c ┬╖ cтАа + cтАа ┬╖ c = 1` (single-mode CAR) | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionNumber_sq` | `n┬▓ = n` (idempotent number operator) | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilation_conjTranspose` | `cс┤┤ = cтАа` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionCreation_conjTranspose` | `(cтАа)с┤┤ = c` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionNumber_isHermitian` | `n` is Hermitian | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionVacuum`, `fermionOccupied` | basis vectors `|0тЯй = (1, 0)`, `|1тЯй = (0, 1)` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilation_mulVec_vacuum` / `_occupied` | `c|0тЯй = 0`, `c|1тЯй = |0тЯй` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionCreation_mulVec_vacuum` / `_occupied` | `cтАа|0тЯй = |1тЯй`, `cтАа|1тЯй = 0` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionNumber_mulVec_vacuum` / `_occupied` | `n|0тЯй = 0`, `n|1тЯй = |1тЯй` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilation_eq_spinHalfOpPlus` | `c = ╧Г^+` (computational-basis identification) | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionCreation_eq_spinHalfOpMinus` | `cтАа = ╧Г^-` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilation_eq_spinSOpPlus_one` | `c = spinSOpPlus 1` (transitive bridge to generic spin-`S` at `N = 1`) | `Fermion/SpinSBridge.lean` (PR #936) |
| `fermionCreation_eq_spinSOpMinus_one` | `cтАа = spinSOpMinus 1` | `Fermion/SpinSBridge.lean` (PR #936) |
| `fermionNumber_eq_half_smul_one_sub_spinSOp3_one` | `n = (1/2) ┬╖ I тИТ spinSOp3 1` (standard physics identification `n = (I тИТ ╧Г^z)/2` lifted to spin-`S` at `N = 1`) | `Fermion/NumberSpinSBridge.lean` (PR #937) |
| `fermionAnnihilation_mul_fermionCreation_eq_one_sub_number` | `c ┬╖ cтАа = 1 тИТ n` (hole occupation) | `Fermion/AnnihilationCreationIdentity.lean` (PR #963) |
| `fermionAnnihilation_mul_fermionCreation_eq_half_smul_one_add_spinSOp3_one` | `c ┬╖ cтАа = (1/2) ┬╖ I + spinSOp3 1` (spin-`S` form) | `Fermion/CCDaggerSpinSBridge.lean` (PR #965) |
| `fermionAnnihilation_mul_fermionCreation_mulVec_vacuum` / `_occupied` | `(c ┬╖ cтАа) ┬╖ |0тЯй = |0тЯй`; `(c ┬╖ cтАа) ┬╖ |1тЯй = 0` (vacuum/occupied as eigenstates of `c ┬╖ cтАа`) | `Fermion/CCDaggerAction.lean` (PR #966) |
| `fermionVacuum_inner_self` / `fermionOccupied_inner_self` / `fermionVacuum_inner_fermionOccupied` / `fermionOccupied_inner_fermionVacuum` | vacuum/occupied are orthonormal | `Fermion/StatesOrthonormal.lean` (PR #968) |
| `fermionVacuum_expectation_fermionNumber` / `fermionOccupied_expectation_fermionNumber` | `тЯиnтЯй` on vacuum = 0; on occupied = 1 | `Fermion/NumberExpectations.lean` (PR #969) |
| `fermionVacuum_expectation_fermionAnnihilation_mul_fermionCreation` / `fermionOccupied_expectation_fermionAnnihilation_mul_fermionCreation` | `тЯиc ┬╖ cтАатЯй` on vacuum = 1; on occupied = 0 | `Fermion/CCDaggerExpectations.lean` (PR #971) |
| `fermionNumber_add_fermionAnnihilation_mul_fermionCreation_eq_one` | `n + c ┬╖ cтАа = 1` (resolution of identity in occupation basis) | `Fermion/ProjectionSum.lean` (PR #972) |
| `fermionAnnihilation_mul_fermionCreation_sq` | `(c ┬╖ cтАа)┬▓ = c ┬╖ cтАа` (idempotent projection) | `Fermion/CCDaggerIdempotent.lean` (PR #974) |
| `fermionNumber_mul_fermionAnnihilation_mul_fermionCreation_eq_zero` / `fermionAnnihilation_mul_fermionCreation_mul_fermionNumber_eq_zero` | `n ┬╖ (c ┬╖ cтАа) = 0`; `(c ┬╖ cтАа) ┬╖ n = 0` (orthogonal projections) | `Fermion/ProjectionsOrthogonal.lean` (PR #976) |
| `fermionNumber_commute_fermionAnnihilation_mul_fermionCreation` | `Commute n (c ┬╖ cтАа)` (both products zero) | `Fermion/ProjectionsCommute.lean` (PR #978) |
| `fermionAnnihilation_mul_fermionCreation_isHermitian` | `(c ┬╖ cтАа)с┤┤ = c ┬╖ cтАа` | `Fermion/CCDaggerHermitian.lean` (PR #980) |
| `fermionNumber_mul_fermionAnnihilation_eq_zero` / `fermionAnnihilation_mul_fermionNumber_eq_fermionAnnihilation` | `n ┬╖ c = 0`; `c ┬╖ n = c` | `Fermion/AnnihilationNumberIdentities.lean` (PR #982) |
| `fermionCreation_mul_fermionNumber_eq_zero` / `fermionNumber_mul_fermionCreation_eq_fermionCreation` | `cтАа ┬╖ n = 0`; `n ┬╖ cтАа = cтАа` | `Fermion/CreationNumberIdentities.lean` (PR #984) |
| `fermionAnnihilation_mul_fermionCreation_mul_fermionAnnihilation` / `fermionCreation_mul_fermionAnnihilation_mul_fermionCreation` | `c ┬╖ cтАа ┬╖ c = c`; `cтАа ┬╖ c ┬╖ cтАа = cтАа` (partial-isometry relations) | `Fermion/PartialIsometry.lean` (PR #986) |
| `fermionNumber_commutator_fermionAnnihilation` / `fermionNumber_commutator_fermionCreation` | `[n, c] = тИТc`; `[n, cтАа] = cтАа` (ladder commutators) | `Fermion/NumberLadderCommutators.lean` (PR #988) |
| `fermionAnnihilation_commutator_fermionCreation` | `[c, cтАа] = 1 тИТ 2 ┬╖ n` (fermion analogue of bosonic `[a, aтАа] = 1`; ┬▒1 on basis states) | `Fermion/CCDaggerCommutator.lean` (PR #989) |
| `fermionNumber_anticommutator_fermionAnnihilation` / `fermionNumber_anticommutator_fermionCreation` | `{n, c} = c`; `{n, cтАа} = cтАа` (number-ladder anticommutators, dual of PR #988) | `Fermion/NumberLadderAnticommutators.lean` (PR #990) |
| `fermionAnnihilation_trace_eq_zero` / `fermionCreation_trace_eq_zero` / `fermionNumber_trace_eq_one` / `fermionAnnihilation_mul_fermionCreation_trace_eq_one` | `tr(c) = 0`; `tr(cтАа) = 0`; `tr(n) = 1`; `tr(c ┬╖ cтАа) = 1` (single-mode trace identities) | `Fermion/Traces.lean` (PR #991) |
| `fermionNumber_pow_succ` / `fermionAnnihilation_mul_fermionCreation_pow_succ` | `n^(k+1) = n`; `(c ┬╖ cтАа)^(k+1) = c ┬╖ cтАа` for any `k : тДХ` (positive-degree power identities of the idempotent projections) | `Fermion/ProjectionPow.lean` (PR #992) |
| `fermionMultiNumber_anticommutator_fermionMultiAnnihilation_self` / `fermionMultiNumber_anticommutator_fermionMultiCreation_self` | `{n_i, c_i} = c_i`; `{n_i, c_iтАа} = c_iтАа` (multi-mode JW same-site ladder anticommutators, mirror of PR #990) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/NumberAnticommutators.lean` (PR #993) |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_commutator_fermionMultiCreation_self` | `[c_i, c_iтАа] = 1 тИТ 2 ┬╖ n_i` (multi-mode JW same-site `c_i`тАУ`c_iтАа` commutator, mirror of PR #989) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/CDaggerCCommutator.lean` (PR #994) |
| `fermionMultiNumber_pow_succ` | `n_i^(k+1) = n_i` for any `k : тДХ` (multi-mode JW idempotent projection power identity, mirror of PR #992) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/NumberPow.lean` (PR #995) |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_mul_fermionMultiCreation_eq_one_sub_number` / `fermionMultiNumber_add_fermionMultiAnnihilation_mul_fermionMultiCreation_eq_one` | `c_i ┬╖ c_iтАа = 1 тИТ n_i`; `n_i + c_i ┬╖ c_iтАа = 1` (multi-mode JW hole-occupation + resolution of identity, mirror of PRs #963 and #972) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/CDaggerCIdentity.lean` (PR #996) |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_mul_fermionMultiCreation_sq` / `fermionMultiAnnihilation_mul_fermionMultiCreation_pow_succ` | `(c_i ┬╖ c_iтАа)┬▓ = c_i ┬╖ c_iтАа`; `(c_i ┬╖ c_iтАа)^(k+1) = c_i ┬╖ c_iтАа` (multi-mode JW hole-projection idempotency + power, mirror of PRs #974 and #992) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/CDaggerCProjection.lean` (PR #997) |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_mul_fermionMultiCreation_isHermitian` | `(c_i ┬╖ c_iтАа)с┤┤ = c_i ┬╖ c_iтАа` (multi-mode JW hole projection Hermitian, mirror of PR #980) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/CDaggerCHermitian.lean` (PR #998) |

### Multi-mode fermion via JordanтАУWigner (P2 backbone)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `jwString N i` | `тИП_{j.val < i.val} ╧Г^z_j` (noncomm-product, pairwise commutativity from `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwString_zero` | `jwString N 0 = 1` (empty product) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation N i` | `c_i = jwString_i ┬╖ ╧Г^+_i` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation N i` | `c_iтАа = jwString_i ┬╖ ╧Г^-_i` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_zero` | `c_0 = ╧Г^+_0` (no JW string at the leftmost site) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_zero` | `c_0тАа = ╧Г^-_0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwString_commute_onSite` | `Commute (jwString N i) (onSite i A)` (string commutes past same-site operators) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_sq` | `c_i┬▓ = 0` (Pauli exclusion) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_sq` | `(c_iтАа)┬▓ = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwString_isHermitian` | `(jwString N i)с┤┤ = jwString N i` (product of pairwise-commuting Hermitian ╧Г^z is Hermitian) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_conjTranspose` | `(c_i)с┤┤ = c_iтАа` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_conjTranspose` | `(c_iтАа)с┤┤ = c_i` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwString_sq` | `(jwString N i)┬▓ = 1` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber N i` | `n_i := c_iтАа ┬╖ c_i` (site-occupation number operator) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_eq_onSite` | `n_i = onSite i (╧Г^- ┬╖ ╧Г^+)` (JW strings cancel via `J┬▓ = 1`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_isHermitian` | `n_i` is Hermitian | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_sq` | `n_i┬▓ = n_i` (idempotent, eigenvalues 0, 1) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnticomm_self` | `c_i ┬╖ c_iтАа + c_iтАа ┬╖ c_i = 1` (same-site CAR) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_commute` | `Commute (n_i) (n_j)` for any sites (simultaneously diagonal) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber N` | `N╠В := ╬г_i n_i` (total particle-number operator) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber_isHermitian` | `N╠В` is Hermitian | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_anticomm_two_site_cross` | simplest nontrivial cross-site CAR on `Fin 2`: `c_0 ┬╖ c_1 + c_1 ┬╖ c_0 = 0` (JW string at site 1 is `╧Г^z_0`, combined with `╧Г^+ ╧Г^z = -╧Г^+` and `╧Г^z ╧Г^+ = ╧Г^+`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_anticomm_two_site_cross` | adjoint form: `c_0тАа ┬╖ c_1тАа + c_1тАа ┬╖ c_0тАа = 0` on `Fin 2`, obtained by taking `conjTranspose` of the annihilation version | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_creation_anticomm_two_site_cross` | mixed cross-site: `c_0 ┬╖ c_1тАа + c_1тАа ┬╖ c_0 = 0` on `Fin 2` (same proof template as the annihilation-only version with `╧Г^+_1` replaced by `╧Г^-_1` at site 1) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_annihilation_anticomm_two_site_cross` | fourth off-diagonal CAR: `c_0тАа ┬╖ c_1 + c_1 ┬╖ c_0тАа = 0` on `Fin 2` (adjoint of the previous; completes the 2-site off-diagonal CAR relations) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_anticomm_zero_one` | generalisation to any chain length: `c_0 ┬╖ c_1 + c_1 ┬╖ c_0 = 0` on `Fin (N+1)` for any `N тЙе 1` (the JW string at site 1 is uniformly `╧Г^z_0` independent of `N`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_anticomm_zero_one` | dual: `c_0тАа ┬╖ c_1тАа + c_1тАа ┬╖ c_0тАа = 0` on `Fin (N+1)`, `N тЙе 1` (adjoint of the above) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_creation_anticomm_zero_one` | mixed: `c_0 ┬╖ c_1тАа + c_1тАа ┬╖ c_0 = 0` on `Fin (N+1)`, `N тЙе 1` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_annihilation_anticomm_zero_one` | mixed dual: `c_0тАа ┬╖ c_1 + c_1 ┬╖ c_0тАа = 0` on `Fin (N+1)`, `N тЙе 1` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwString_succ_eq` | recursive factorisation of the JW string: `jwString N тЯиi+1, _тЯй = jwString N i * onSite i pauliZ` (key general lemma for proving jwString at any specific site without raw `Finset.noncommProd` manipulation) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_anticomm_zero_two_fin_three` | first 3-site cross-site CAR: `c_0 ┬╖ c_2 + c_2 ┬╖ c_0 = 0` on `Fin 3` (using `jwString_succ_eq` to factor `jwString 2 2 = ╧Г^z_0 ┬╖ ╧Г^z_1`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_anticomm_zero_two_fin_three` | dual: `c_0тАа ┬╖ c_2тАа + c_2тАа ┬╖ c_0тАа = 0` on `Fin 3` (adjoint of the previous) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_creation_anticomm_zero_two_fin_three` | mixed: `c_0 ┬╖ c_2тАа + c_2тАа ┬╖ c_0 = 0` on `Fin 3` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_annihilation_anticomm_zero_two_fin_three` | mixed dual: `c_0тАа ┬╖ c_2 + c_2 ┬╖ c_0тАа = 0` on `Fin 3` (adjoint of the previous) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_anticomm_zero_two_general` | generalised to any N тЙе 2: `c_0 ┬╖ c_2 + c_2 ┬╖ c_0 = 0` on `Fin (N+1)` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_anticomm_zero_two_general` | dual: `c_0тАа ┬╖ c_2тАа + c_2тАа ┬╖ c_0тАа = 0` for any N тЙе 2 (adjoint) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_creation_anticomm_zero_two_general` | mixed: `c_0 ┬╖ c_2тАа + c_2тАа ┬╖ c_0 = 0` for any N тЙе 2 | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_annihilation_anticomm_zero_two_general` | mixed dual: `c_0тАа ┬╖ c_2 + c_2 ┬╖ c_0тАа = 0` for any N тЙе 2 (adjoint) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_anticomm_zero_pos` | **general cross-site CAR `{c_0, c_k} = 0`** for every `k : Fin (N+1)` with `0 < k.val` тАФ generalises the `_zero_one` / `_zero_two_general` specialisations. Proof: reduce to the anticommutator `{╧Г^+_0, jwString N k}`, which vanishes by induction on the string length (base: `{╧Г^+, ╧Г^z} = 0` at site 0; step: `╧Г^z_{k-1}` at site `k-1 тЙа 0` commutes past `╧Г^+_0`). | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_anticomm_zero_pos` | dual `{c_0тАа, c_kтАа} = 0` for every `k : Fin (N+1)` with `0 < k.val` (adjoint of the above) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_creation_anticomm_zero_pos` | mixed `{c_0, c_kтАа} = 0` for every `k : Fin (N+1)` with `0 < k.val` тАФ same inductive argument on the JW string anticommutator (the site-`k` factor is `╧Г^-_k` instead of `╧Г^+_k`; JW-string part is unchanged) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_annihilation_anticomm_zero_pos` | mixed dual `{c_0тАа, c_k} = 0` for every `k : Fin (N+1)` with `0 < k.val` (adjoint of the above) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwStringExceptAt` / `jwString_eq_onSite_mul_jwStringExceptAt` / `jwStringExceptAt_commute_onSite` | private factorisation helpers for the Jordan-Wigner string at an interior site (#210): for `i.val < j.val`, `jwString N j = onSite i pauliZ * jwStringExceptAt N j i`, and `jwStringExceptAt N j i` commutes with every single-site operator at site `i` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwString_anticomm_onSite_pos_spinHalfOpPlus` | operator-level anticommutator `{╧Г^+_i, jwString N j} = 0` for every `i j : Fin (N+1)` with `i.val < j.val` тАФ generalises `jwString_anticomm_onSite_zero_spinHalfOpPlus` (i = 0 case) to arbitrary interior `i`; building block for the fully general cross-site CAR `{c_i, c_j} = 0` (#210) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwString_anticomm_onSite_pos_spinHalfOpMinus` | companion `{╧Г^-_i, jwString N j} = 0` for every `i < j` (via `conjTranspose` of the `╧Г^+` version) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwString_commute_jwString` | any two Jordan-Wigner strings `jwString N i` and `jwString N j` commute (both are noncommutative products of `╧Г^z` over distinct sites) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_anticomm_lt` | **fully general cross-site CAR `{c_i, c_j} = 0` for `i < j`** (#210) on `Fin (N + 1)`. Proof: reduce via `jwString_anticomm_onSite_pos_spinHalfOpPlus` to an identity involving `JW_i ┬╖ JW_j = JW_j ┬╖ JW_i` (via `jwString_commute_jwString`), which makes the sum collapse | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_anticomm_lt` | dual `{c_iтАа, c_jтАа} = 0` for `i < j` (adjoint of the above) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_creation_anticomm_lt` | mixed `{c_i, c_jтАа} = 0` for `i < j` тАФ same structure as `_anticomm_lt` but with `╧Г^-_j` at site `j` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_annihilation_anticomm_lt` | mixed dual `{c_iтАа, c_j} = 0` for `i < j` (adjoint of the above) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `spinHalfOpPlus_mul_self` / `spinHalfOpPlus_mul_spinHalfOpMinus_mul_spinHalfOpPlus` | Pauli helper identities `╧Г^+ ╧Г^+ = 0` and `╧Г^+ ╧Г^- ╧Г^+ = ╧Г^+` | `Quantum/SpinHalfBasis.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_commutator_fermionMultiAnnihilation_self` | `[n_i, c_i] = -c_i` (number / annihilation commutator) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_commutator_fermionMultiCreation_self` | `[n_i, c_iтАа] = c_iтАа` (number / creation commutator, dual via adjoint) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `spinHalfOpMinus_mul_spinHalfOpPlus_commute_pauliZ` | matrix identity: `Commute (╧Г^- ╧Г^+) ╧Г^z` (both diagonal in the computational basis) | `Quantum/SpinHalfBasis.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_commute_fermionMultiAnnihilation_of_ne` | `Commute (n_i) (c_j)` for `i тЙа j` тАФ the number operator at site `i` commutes with any annihilation at a different site, via the `n ╧Г^z = ╧Г^z n` matrix commutativity absorbing the JW-string `╧Г^z_i` factor | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_commute_fermionMultiCreation_of_ne` | dual: `Commute (n_i) (c_jтАа)` for `i тЙа j` via adjoint | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber_commutator_fermionMultiAnnihilation` | `[N╠В, c_j] = -c_j` тАФ the total particle-number operator shifts annihilation down by one (sum of diagonal `[n_j, c_j] = -c_j` with vanishing off-diagonal terms) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber_commutator_fermionMultiCreation` | dual: `[N╠В, c_jтАа] = c_jтАа` (via adjoint) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber_commute_hopping` | `Commute N╠В (c_iтАа ┬╖ c_j)` тАФ the hopping operator preserves total particle number (shifts cancel: `[N╠В, c_iтАа] = c_iтАа` and `[N╠В, c_j] = -c_j`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | `Commute (n_i) (N╠В)` тАФ site occupation commutes with the total particle number (sum of pairwise commuting `[n_i, n_j] = 0`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionDensityDensity_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | `Commute (n_i ┬╖ n_j) (N╠В)` тАФ the density-density operator preserves total particle number, foundational for Hubbard-style on-site interactions | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionHopping`, `fermionHopping_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | the general single-particle hopping `H_hop = ╬г_{i,j} t_{i,j} c_iтАа c_j` and the proof that it commutes with `N╠В` (charge conservation of the kinetic Hamiltonian) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionDensityInteraction`, `fermionDensityInteraction_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | the general densityтАУdensity interaction `V_int = ╬г_{i,j} V_{i,j} n_i n_j` and the proof that it commutes with `N╠В` (paired with `H_hop` this gives charge conservation for any Hubbard-type Hamiltonian) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionGenericHamiltonian`, `fermionGenericHamiltonian_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | the canonical charge-conserving fermion Hamiltonian `H = H_hop + V_int` and the proof that `[H, N╠В] = 0`, the unified statement of charge conservation for single-species Hubbard / extended Hubbard models | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_mul_isHermitian` | `(n_i ┬╖ n_j)` is Hermitian for any sites (commuting Hermitian factors) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionDensityInteraction_isHermitian` | `V_int = ╬г V_{ij} n_i n_j` is Hermitian when every coupling entry is real (`star V_{ij} = V_{ij}`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionHoppingTerm_conjTranspose` | `(c_iтАа ┬╖ c_j)с┤┤ = c_jтАа ┬╖ c_i` (single hopping term) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionHopping_isHermitian` | `H_hop = ╬г t_{ij} c_iтАа c_j` is Hermitian when `t` is Hermitian (`star (t i j) = t j i`); proved via term-wise conjTranspose + `Finset.sum_comm` for the index swap | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionGenericHamiltonian_isHermitian` | `H = H_hop + V_int` is Hermitian when `t` is Hermitian and `V` is entry-wise real; one-line corollary of the two summand Hermiticities via `Matrix.IsHermitian.add` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionGenericGibbsState N ╬▓ t V` | Gibbs state `gibbsState ╬▓ (H_hop + V_int)` for the Hubbard-skeleton Hamiltonian | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionGenericGibbsState_isHermitian` | Hermiticity (when `t` is Hermitian and `V` is real) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionGenericGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `Commute ╧Б_╬▓ H` (always true for the Gibbs state of any operator with itself) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiVacuum N` | the JW vacuum on `Fin (N+1)` modes тАФ the all-up many-body basis vector `|тЖСтЖСтАжтЖСтЯй` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_mulVec_vacuum` | every annihilation operator kills the vacuum: `(c_i).mulVec (fermionMultiVacuum N) = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_mulVec_vacuum` | each `n_i ┬╖ |vacтЯй = 0` (since `n_i = c_iтАа c_i` and `c_i |vacтЯй = 0`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber_mulVec_vacuum` | the vacuum is an `N╠В`-eigenstate of eigenvalue 0 | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionHopping_mulVec_vacuum` | `H_hop ┬╖ |vacтЯй = 0` (each `c_iтАа c_j |vacтЯй = c_iтАа 0 = 0`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionDensityInteraction_mulVec_vacuum` | `V_int ┬╖ |vacтЯй = 0` (each `n_i n_j |vacтЯй = n_i 0 = 0`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionGenericHamiltonian_mulVec_vacuum` | `H ┬╖ |vacтЯй = 0` for the full Hubbard skeleton (linearity) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber_mulVec_singleParticle` | `c_iтАа |vacтЯй` is an `N╠В`-eigenstate of eigenvalue 1 (uses `[N╠В, c_iтАа] = c_iтАа` and `N╠В |vacтЯй = 0`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber_mulVec_twoParticle` | `c_iтАа c_jтАа |vacтЯй` is an `N╠В`-eigenstate of eigenvalue 2 (Leibniz on the commutator gives `[N╠В, c_iтАа c_jтАа] = 2 c_iтАа c_jтАа`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber_mulVec_eigenstate_of_commute` | generic charge-eigenstate helper: if `[N╠В, X] = ╬▒ X` and `N╠В v = 0` then `N╠В (X v) = ╬▒ (X v)`; abstracts the single- and two-particle constructions | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `spinfulIndex N i ╧Г` | bijection `(i, ╧Г : Fin 2) тЖж 2 * i + ╧Г тИИ Fin (2*N+2)`, embedding two-species data into a single-species JW chain | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `spinfulIndex_eq_iff`, `exists_spinfulIndex` | shared injectivity (`spinfulIndex N a r = spinfulIndex N b s тЖФ a = b тИз r = s`) and decomposition (`тИГ a r, k = spinfulIndex N a r`) of the spinful index | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionUpAnnihilation`, `fermionDownAnnihilation`, `fermionUpCreation`, `fermionDownCreation` | spinful annihilation / creation operators as wrappers around the underlying single-species operators at `2i` (up) and `2i+1` (down) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionUpNumber`, `fermionDownNumber` | spinful site-occupation numbers `n_{i,тЖС}`, `n_{i,тЖУ}` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardOnSiteInteraction N U` | the on-site Hubbard interaction `H_int = U ╬г_i n_{i,тЖС} ┬╖ n_{i,тЖУ}` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardOnSiteInteraction_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | `[H_int, N╠В] = 0` (charge conservation) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardOnSiteInteraction_isHermitian` | `H_int` is Hermitian when `U` is real (`star U = U`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardKinetic N t` | the spinful tight-binding kinetic operator `T = ╬г_{╧Г} ╬г_{i,j} t_{i,j} c_{i,╧Г}тАа c_{j,╧Г}` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardKinetic_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | `[T, N╠В] = 0` (charge conservation of the kinetic operator) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardKinetic_isHermitian` | `T` is Hermitian when `t` is a Hermitian matrix (`star (t i j) = t j i`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardHamiltonian N t U` | the canonical (single-band) Hubbard Hamiltonian `H = T + U ╬г n_{iтЖС} n_{iтЖУ}` on `Fin (2N+2)` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardHamiltonian_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | `[H, N╠В] = 0` (charge conservation) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardHamiltonian_isHermitian` | `H` is Hermitian when `t` is Hermitian and `U` is real | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsState N ╬▓ t U` | the Hubbard Gibbs state `gibbsState ╬▓ H_Hubbard` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsState_isHermitian` | Hermiticity (Hermitian `t`, real `U`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `Commute ╧Б_╬▓ H_Hubbard` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber`, `fermionTotalDownNumber` | spinful conserved charges `N_тЖС = ╬г_i n_{iтЖС}`, `N_тЖУ = ╬г_i n_{iтЖУ}` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ` | total spin polarisation `S^z_tot = (1/2)(N_тЖС тИТ N_тЖУ)` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_commute_fermionTotalDownNumber` | `[N_тЖС, N_тЖУ] = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_commute_fermionTotalNumber` / `fermionTotalDownNumber_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | `[N_тЖС, N╠В] = [N_тЖУ, N╠В] = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | `[S^z_tot, N╠В] = 0` (spin polarisation commutes with total number) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_commute_hubbardOnSiteInteraction` / `fermionTotalDownNumber_commute_hubbardOnSiteInteraction` | `[N_тЖС, H_int] = [N_тЖУ, H_int] = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ_commute_hubbardOnSiteInteraction` | `[S^z_tot, H_int] = 0` (free corollary) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionUpAnnihilation_mulVec_vacuum` / `fermionDownAnnihilation_mulVec_vacuum` | every spinful annihilation kills the JW vacuum | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionUpNumber_mulVec_vacuum` / `fermionDownNumber_mulVec_vacuum` | each spinful site number kills the vacuum | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_mulVec_vacuum` / `fermionTotalDownNumber_mulVec_vacuum` | `N_тЖС ┬╖ |vacтЯй = N_тЖУ ┬╖ |vacтЯй = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_vacuum` | `S^z_tot ┬╖ |vacтЯй = 0` (the vacuum is unpolarised) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardKinetic_mulVec_vacuum` / `hubbardOnSiteInteraction_mulVec_vacuum` / `hubbardHamiltonian_mulVec_vacuum` | each annihilates the vacuum (so `|vacтЯй` is a 0-energy / 0-particle eigenstate) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `spinfulIndex_up_ne_down` | the up-channel position `2 i` is never the down-channel position `2 j + 1` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalDownNumber_commute_fermionUp{Creation,Annihilation,Number}` and the dual `fermionTotalUpNumber_commute_fermionDown{Creation,Annihilation,Number}` | the spinful number on one species commutes with every operator of the other species (different JW positions) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalDownNumber_commute_upHopping` / `fermionTotalUpNumber_commute_downHopping` | the spinful same-╧Г hopping term `c_{i╧Г}тАа c_{j╧Г}` commutes with the opposite-spin total number `N_{╧Г'тЙа╧Г}` (cross-spin half of `[H_kinetic, N_╧Г] = 0`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |

#### Hubbard spin symmetry тАФ full SU(2) invariance (Tasaki ┬з9.3.3)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_isHermitian` / `fermionTotalDownNumber_isHermitian` | `N_тЖС` and `N_тЖУ` are Hermitian (sum of Hermitian number operators) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_commutator_fermionUpCreation` | `[N_тЖС, cтАа_{i,тЖС}] = cтАа_{i,тЖС}` (up-spin sub-chain analogue of `[N╠В, cтАа_i] = cтАа_i`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalDownNumber_commutator_fermionDownCreation` | `[N_тЖУ, cтАа_{i,тЖУ}] = cтАа_{i,тЖУ}` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_commute_upHopping` | `[N_тЖС, cтАа_{i,тЖС} c_{j,тЖС}] = 0` (same-species hopping preserves spin-up count) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalDownNumber_commute_downHopping` | `[N_тЖУ, cтАа_{i,тЖУ} c_{j,тЖУ}] = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_commute_hubbardKinetic` / `fermionTotalDownNumber_commute_hubbardKinetic` | `[N_тЖС, H_kin] = [N_тЖУ, H_kin] = 0` (each spin species conserved by kinetic term) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_commute_hubbardHamiltonian` | `[N_тЖС, H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬з9.3.3, eq. (9.3.35)) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalDownNumber_commute_hubbardHamiltonian` | `[N_тЖУ, H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬з9.3.3, eq. (9.3.35)) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ_commute_hubbardHamiltonian` | `[S^z_tot, H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬з9.3.3, p. 333) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinPlus` / `fermionTotalSpinMinus` | `┼Ь^+_tot = ╬г_i cтАа_{i,тЖС}c_{i,тЖУ}`, `┼Ь^-_tot = (┼Ь^+_tot)тАа` тАФ SU(2) raising/lowering operators (Tasaki ┬з9.3.3, p. 332) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinPlus_conjTranspose` | `(┼Ь^+_tot)тАа = ┼Ь^-_tot` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionUpAnnihilation_commutator_fermionTotalSpinPlus` | `[c_{j,тЖС}, ┼Ь^+_tot] = c_{j,тЖУ}` (Tasaki ┬з9.3.3, eq. (9.3.36)) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionDownCreation_commutator_fermionTotalSpinPlus` | `[cтАа_{j,тЖУ}, ┼Ь^+_tot] = тИТcтАа_{j,тЖС}` (Tasaki ┬з9.3.3, eq. (9.3.36)) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionUpCreation_commute_fermionTotalSpinPlus` / `fermionDownAnnihilation_commute_fermionTotalSpinPlus` | `[cтАа_{i,тЖС}, ┼Ь^+_tot] = 0` and `[c_{j,тЖУ}, ┼Ь^+_tot] = 0` (Tasaki ┬з9.3.3, eq. (9.3.36)) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinPlus_commute_hubbardHamiltonian` | `[┼Ь^+_tot, H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬з9.3.3, eq. (9.3.35)) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinMinus_commute_hubbardHamiltonian` | `[┼Ь^-_tot, H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬з9.3.3, eq. (9.3.35), proved by adjoint) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |

#### Hubbard all-up-spin state and saturated ferromagnetism (Tasaki ┬з11.1.1)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `hubbardAllUpState N` | fully spin-polarised basis vector: all spin-up orbitals occupied, spin-down empty (even JW indices = 1, odd = 0) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `fermionUpNumber_mulVec_allUpState` | `n_{i,тЖС} ┬╖ |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй = |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй` тАФ each spin-up number operator acts as identity on the all-up state | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `fermionDownNumber_mulVec_allUpState` | `n_{i,тЖУ} ┬╖ |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй = 0` тАФ no spin-down electrons; key to the vanishing of `H_int` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `hubbardOnSiteInteraction_mulVec_allUpState` | `H_int ┬╖ |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй = 0` тАФ no double occupancy in the fully-polarised state (Tasaki ┬з11.1.1, p. 373; eq. (10.1.5), p. 344) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `hubbardHamiltonian_mulVec_allUpState` | `H ┬╖ |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй = H_hop ┬╖ |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй` тАФ the Hubbard model in the all-up sector reduces to a non-interacting hopping problem | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `fermionDownAnnihilation_mulVec_allUpState` | `c_{i,тЖУ} ┬╖ |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй = 0` тАФ spin-down annihilation kills the all-up state (odd JW index unoccupied, so ╧ГтБ║ maps it to 0) (Tasaki ┬з11.1.1, p. 373) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `fermionUpCreation_mulVec_allUpState` | `cтАа_{i,тЖС} ┬╖ |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй = 0` тАФ spin-up creation kills the all-up state (even JW index already occupied, so ╧ГтБ╗ maps it to 0) (Tasaki ┬з11.1.1, p. 373) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `hubbardKinetic_mulVec_allUpState` | `H_hop ┬╖ |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй = (╬г_i t i i) тАв |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй` тАФ hopping eigenvalue: off-diagonal terms vanish by CAR anticommutation, diagonal terms give 1 each (Tasaki ┬з11.1.1, p. 373) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `hubbardHamiltonian_mulVec_allUpState_eigenstate` | `H ┬╖ |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй = (╬г_i t i i) тАв |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй` тАФ full Hamiltonian eigenstate: combines `H_hop` eigenvalue and `H_int ┬╖ |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй = 0` (Tasaki ┬з11.1.1, p. 373; eq. (10.1.5), p. 344) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinSquared` | total-spin Casimir `(┼Ь_tot)┬▓ = ┼ЬтБ╗┼ЬтБ║ + ┼Ь_z(┼Ь_z+1)` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_mulVec_allUpState` | `N_тЖС ┬╖ |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй = (N+1) тАв |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalDownNumber_mulVec_allUpState` | `N_тЖУ ┬╖ |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_allUpState` | `┼Ь^z_tot ┬╖ |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй = ((N+1)/2) тАв |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinPlus_mulVec_allUpState` | `┼ЬтБ║_tot ┬╖ |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй = 0` тАФ highest-weight state; no down-spin to raise | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinSquared_mulVec_allUpState` | `(┼Ь_tot)┬▓ ┬╖ |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй = S_max(S_max+1) тАв |тЖСтАжтЖСтЯй` where `S_max = (N+1)/2` (Tasaki ┬з11.1.1, p. 372) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinSquared_commute_hubbardHamiltonian` | `[(┼Ь_tot)┬▓, H] = 0` тАФ Casimir commutes with H (from SU(2) invariance, Tasaki ┬з9.3.3) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `isSaturatedFerromagnet` | **Definition 11.1** тАФ Lean predicate: there exists a ground-state energy `EтВА` such that every nonzero `H`-eigenvector with eigenvalue `EтВА` is a `(┼Ь_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector with eigenvalue `S_max(S_max+1)` (Tasaki ┬з11.1.1, p. 372) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ_commutator_fermionTotalSpinMinus` | `[┼Ь^z_tot, ┼Ь^-_tot] = -┼Ь^-_tot` тАФ SU(2) algebra relation; follows from site-wise `[┼Ь_z, cтАа_{i,тЖУ}c_{i,тЖС}] = -(cтАа_{i,тЖУ}c_{i,тЖС})` (Tasaki ┬з9.3.3, p. 332) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinMinus_mulVec_preserves_hamiltonian_eigenvalue` | if `H┬╖v = E┬╖v` then `H┬╖(┼Ь^-┬╖v) = E┬╖(┼Ь^-┬╖v)` тАФ applying `┼Ь^-` preserves Hamiltonian eigenvalues; follows from `[┼Ь^-, H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬з11.1.1, p. 373) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_spinMinus_step` | if `┼Ь_z┬╖v = m┬╖v` then `┼Ь_z┬╖(┼Ь^-┬╖v) = (m-1)┬╖(┼Ь^-┬╖v)` тАФ applying `┼Ь^-` decrements `┼Ь_z` eigenvalue by 1; follows from `[┼Ь^z, ┼Ь^-] = -┼Ь^-` (Tasaki ┬з2.4, eq. (2.4.9); ┬з11.1.1, p. 373) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |

#### Hubbard hard-core subspace (Tasaki ┬з11.2)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `hubbardDoubleOccupancy N i` | same-site Hubbard double-occupancy operator `n_{i,тЖС} n_{i,тЖУ}` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreSubspace.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreSubspace N` | linear subspace of vectors annihilated by every same-site double-occupancy operator, the no-double-occupancy sector used as unnumbered infrastructure for Tasaki Theorems 11.5 and 11.7 (1st ed., ┬з11.2, pp. 381-388) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreSubspace.lean` |
| `mem_hubbardHardcoreSubspace_iff` | membership in `hubbardHardcoreSubspace` is equivalent to vanishing of all `hubbardDoubleOccupancy N i` actions | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreSubspace.lean` |
| `hubbardDoubleOccupancy_mulVec_eq_zero_of_mem_hardcore` | every `hubbardDoubleOccupancy N i` annihilates each hard-core vector | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreSubspace.lean` |
| `hubbardOnSiteInteraction_mulVec_eq_zero_of_mem_hardcore` / `hubbardOnSiteInteraction_apply_eq_zero_of_mem_hardcore` | the on-site interaction `U ╬г_i n_{i,тЖС} n_{i,тЖУ}` annihilates every hard-core vector, both as a vector equation and pointwise | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreSubspace.lean` |

#### Hubbard hard-core projection (Tasaki ┬з11.2)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `hubbardHardcoreFactor N i` | single-site hard-core factor `1 - n_{i,тЖС} n_{i,тЖУ}` at spinful site `i` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreFactor_mul_self` | each hard-core factor is idempotent | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardDoubleOccupancy_mul_hardcoreFactor` | `n_{i,тЖС} n_{i,тЖУ} ┬╖ (1 - n_{i,тЖС} n_{i,тЖУ}) = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreFactor_commute` | hard-core factors at any two sites commute | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardDoubleOccupancy_isHermitian` / `hubbardHardcoreFactor_isHermitian` | the double-occupancy operator and each hard-core factor are Hermitian | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreFactor_mulVec_eq_self_of_mem` | every hard-core factor fixes each hard-core vector | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreProjection N` | hard-core projection `P╠В_hc = тИП_i (1 - n_{i,тЖС} n_{i,тЖУ})`, the non-commutative product of pairwise-commuting hard-core factors, unnumbered infrastructure for Tasaki Theorems 11.5 and 11.7 (1st ed., ┬з11.2, pp. 381-388) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreProjection_mul_self` | the hard-core projection is idempotent | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreProjection_isHermitian` | the hard-core projection is Hermitian | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardDoubleOccupancy_mul_hardcoreProjection` | every same-site double-occupancy operator annihilates `P╠В_hc`: `n_{j,тЖС} n_{j,тЖУ} ┬╖ P╠В_hc = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreProjection_mulVec_eq_self_of_mem` | `P╠В_hc` fixes every hard-core vector | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreProjection_mulVec_mem` | `P╠В_hc ┬╖ ╧И` always lies in `hubbardHardcoreSubspace` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |

#### Hubbard one-hole hard-core basis states (Tasaki ┬з11.2)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `hubbardOneHoleConfig N x ╧Г` | occupation configuration with a hole at site `x` and spin `╧Г` (`true = тЖС`) on every other site | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreBasis.lean` |
| `hubbardOneHoleConfig_apply_up` / `hubbardOneHoleConfig_apply_down` | the up- / down-orbital occupation values of that configuration at each site | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreBasis.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreBasisState N x ╧Г` | one-hole hard-core basis state `\|╬ж_{x,╧Г}тЯй`, the computational basis vector of `hubbardOneHoleConfig N x ╧Г` (Tasaki ┬з11.2, eq. (11.2.3); 1st ed., pp. 381-388) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreBasis.lean` |

| `hubbardHardcoreBasisState_inner` | orthonormality: `тЯи╬ж_{x,╧Г} \| ╬ж_{x',╧Г'}тЯй = 1` iff their configurations coincide, else `0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreBasis.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreBasisState_self_inner` | each basis state is normalised (self-overlap `1`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreBasis.lean` |

#### JordanтАУWigner string action on basis states (Tasaki ┬з11.2 infrastructure)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `onSite_pauliZ_mulVec_basisVec` | `╧Г^z_j ┬╖ \|cтЯй = (-1)^{c j} \|cтЯй` (single `╧Г^z` acts by the parity sign at `j`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/StringBasisVecAction.lean` |
| `jwString_mulVec_basisVec` | `jwString N i ┬╖ \|cтЯй = (тИП_{j<i} (-1)^{c j}) \|cтЯй` (the JW string acts by the fermion-parity sign of the occupied modes below `i`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/StringBasisVecAction.lean` |
| `jwSign N j c` | the JW string sign `тИП_{k<j} (-1)^{c k}` of a configuration | `Fermion/JordanWigner/AnnihilationCreationBasisVec.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_mulVec_basisVec` | `c_j \|cтЯй = jwSign N j c тАв \|c with jтЖж0тЯй` if `c j = 1`, else `0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/AnnihilationCreationBasisVec.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_mulVec_basisVec` | `cтАа_j \|cтЯй = jwSign N j c тАв \|c with jтЖж1тЯй` if `c j = 0`, else `0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/AnnihilationCreationBasisVec.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_mul_Annihilation_mulVec_basisVec` | a single hop `cтАа_p c_q \|cтЯй` = `(jwSign┬╖jwSign) тАв \|c with qтЖж0, pтЖж1тЯй` if `c q = 1` and the intermediate config is empty at `p`, else `0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/HopBasisVec.lean` |
| `jwSign_zero_config` / `fermionMultiCreation_mulVec_vacuum_eq_basisVec` | the string sign of the vacuum is `1`; `cтАа_j \|vacтЯй = \|single electron at jтЯй` (base case for the ordered-`cтАа` Tasaki basis (11.2.3)) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/VacuumCreationBasisVec.lean` |

|---|---|---|

| `exists_eq_hubbardOneHoleConfig_of_isOneHoleHardcore` | surjectivity: every one-hole hard-core configuration equals `hubbardOneHoleConfig N x ╧Г` for some `(x, ╧Г)` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreSpan.lean` |
